# PREFACE

It gives me immense pleasure to place before esteemed readers of astrology, the Second Volume of Brihat Parasara Hora Sastra in which commentary, illustrations as well as examples have been given at appropriate places to explain the fundamentals, axioms and principles involved. 

In the same breath, I must hasten to add that but for the active involvement, association and vast contribution of Shri P.K. Vasudeva, Jyotish Visharad, this text would not have been in the present lucid form and discernable format. In other words, he has been greatly helpful in presenting the renderings of the text in more than many ways and making this english translation closest to the concept. 

Brihat Parasara Hora Sastra in the most ancient scripture of Astrology expounded by Maharishi Parasara which is versatile and yet compact to comprehend. It touches upon the very basic as well as complexed dimensions of Astrology and also deals with every perceptible events, matter and attributes. It is for these attractive features of this work of Sage Parasara, it is reckoned as the fountain head of Vedic Astrology. 

These renderings of great Sage have been handed down to generations through chain of disciples and got scattered in the course of time. The ardent savants of Astrology have pieced it together and propagated this divine science through centuries and thus maintained the perpetuity, but for which the present lovers of Astrology would have been greatly deprived of it. We all feel indebted to those who have done this great service to us. 

It has been my endeavour to present this ancient treatise in its complete form as far as possible. Keeping this object in view, I laboured through all the available literature on this treatise of Parasara and added three more chapters (Two in Volume-I and One in Volume-II) which were found scattered. Although many editions of this work, in Sanskrit, Hindi and English which are in circulation, this book I think, subject to correction, carries the complete version. In Volume-II, 53 Chapters have been covered while Volume-I carried 47 Chapters, bringing overall total of 

100 Chapters. 

The initial 20 Chapters of this Volume have solely been devoted towards Dasa System. In the first Chapter, the Sage has described over 40 kinds of Dasas but his main thrust was on Vimshottari and Kalachakra Dasa systems. The Sage also dwelled on Chara Dasa longer than others. However, Vimshottari Dasa is the most popular one and widely practiced by the savants. As matter of fact, Dasa System is mainly figured only in the ancient works of Vedic Astrology. Dasa is the simplest system of predicting 'Timing of Events', yet determination of future events is extremely difficult and perhaps the most unsatisfying part of it. 

Continuing on Dasa System, a Chapter each has been assigned to the Antardasas of nine planets under Vimshottari Dasa. This speaks of the importance of Antardasas in the whole gamut of Dasa system. Keeping this very aspect in view, copious notes covering the versions of the other learned Astrologers such as Varaha Mihira, Kalyana Varma, Mantreswara and our experiences, have been provided duly supported with illustrative examples. 

Kalachakra is yet another Dasa on which Sage Parasara premised to a great deal and discussed this system with much larger detail and applications. In the recent time, many learned Astrologers have been found using this Dasa with encouraging results. Therefore, it is felt necessary to handle this Dasa to a fuller length. Explanatory notes on the interpretation have been added at appropriate places alongwith Tables which have been specially devised for spot and easy references. Towards the end, a few examples have been inserted, illustrating the application part of it. 

The peculiarity of Vedic Astrology exists in the variety of methods employed in the prognostication of horoscopes. Besides Dasa and transit systems, Ashtakavarga method of preditions, which has been expounded Sage Parasra in this treatise, is quite popular and is being followed by many eminent astrologers. The results of the Ashtakavarga are determined on the basis of precise mutual positions of the planets. It also takes into congrisance, the actions and interactions of transiting planets with regard to their radical positions, including the Ascendant. Through the combinations of auspicious and inauspicious influences giving rise by such 

(v) 

transitions, which could be measured numerically (the uniqueness of the system), certain important events are determined. All the principles, especially three types of Shodhanas (reductions), have been explained with a model example carried through all the seven chapters of Ashtakavarga to demonstrate the application of this system. 

The another important feature touched by Sage, is the typical method of predictions through Sudarshana Chakra in which the two illuminaries, the Sun and the Moon alongwith the Ascendant have been combined for delineating the effects. Besides, the concept of yearly Dasa, monthly Antardasas and two and a half days of Pratyantardasas have been conceptualised which are quite unique in its character. Explanatory notes on the interpretation of the house and Dasas, backed with examples have been given. 

Panchamahapurusha Yogas assume special position amongst the numerous Yogas (planetary combinations) and it is perhaps, for this reason, sage Parasara has assigned one full chapter to it. The enunciated priciples of Panchamahapurusha Yogas - producing five kinds of great men - are critically examined in Chapter-77 through notes duly supported with examples for the indepth study of these combinations in a fond hope that it will greatly help the readers. 

Right from the commencement of this highly acclaimed scripture, an impression was getting seated in the mind that the results expounded by the Sage are related to only male natives. On raising an intrinsic query by Maitreya, a disciple, Maharishi stated that all the narrated results are equally applicable to the female horoscopy as well but indeed, there are certain distinct features which are attributable to female only. Sage, therefore, contributed a chapter to focus on certain physical and metal peculiarities which do not happen to male but are only confined to female natives. The aphorisms set forth in this chapter have been explained through elaborate notes and examples to seek indulgence of the readers in this important segment of astrology. 

The later part of the book carries a few chapters exclusively on propitiation of planets (Graha Shanti). Here, Maharishi has prescribed the right efforts to alleviate the evil effects through 

(vi) 

propitiation of planets causing affliction in the nativity and also for the insupicious and abnormal births, by way of recitation of the right kind of Mantras, performing of other rites and rituals and giving charities. 

Towards the end, a Chapter on 'Horary System' which has been picked up from the other expositions on the subject by Sage Parasara, has been added with a view to give exposure to the complete works of the Maharishi, for the benefit of the readers at large. 

Although the text bears 'time' etc. in Ghatis and Palas, attempt has been made to simultaneously give the calculations in terms of hours and minutes, as this expression is more in vogue these days. 

I am aware that my formal thanks to Shri P.K. Vasudeva for his informal help would be grossly inadequate. 

I also owe thanks to Mr. Urs Strobel and Sibelt Meyer of MVU whose sustained inspiration and advice, helped me in a great deal, in accomplishing this massive task against many odds. 

I must as well thank Mess'rs N.C. Saxena, S.K. Gupta, V.K. Gupta and V.P. Singh, who have been helpful and supportive in many ways. Miss Manu Sharma draws special thanks for her help in carrying out the mathematical calculations in this book. 

I am ever grateful to Mr. Narinder Sagar (Prop. Sagar Publication) for his continuous encouragement and whole hearted support which made it possible to bring out this book in time. His dedicated efforts in popularising astrological works is incredible. May God bless him. 

It is hoped that this book will be received with the same warmth and enthusiasm as my Volume-I. No book is perfect in all respects and without blemishes. Therefore, I be excused for any omissions or errors that the readers may come across which will, ofcourse, be rectified in the next edition. Hence, I shall be grateful to receive suggestions to improve the value of the book by incorporating the necessary changes in future.

CHAPTER-48 1-110 

# DASA SYSTEMS :

Types of Dasas; 1. Vimshottari Dasa - Span of Mahadasas, Balance of Dasa, Method of Calculation, 2. Astottari Dasa, Method of Calculation, Balance of Dasa, 3. Shodsottari Dasa, 4. Dwadashottari Dasa, 5. Panchottari Dasa, 6. Shatabdika Dasa, 7. Chaturashitisama Dasa, 8. Dwisaptatisama Dasa, 9. Shastiyayani Dasa, 10. Shat-Trimshasama Dasa, 11. Kala Dasa, 12. Chakra Dasa, 13. Kalachakra Dasa - Savya and Apsavya Nakshatras, Description of Deha and Jeeva, Navamsas of the quarlors of the Nakshatras, Different types of motions of the sign, Results of the Navamsas, Effects of planets in Deha and Jeeva signs. Interpretation of Kala Chakra Dasa, 14. Chara Dasa, signs of dual lordship, 15. Sthira Dasa, 16. Yogardha Dasa, 17. Kendradi Dasa, Karaka-Kendradi Graha, 18. Karaka Dasa, 20. Mandooka Dasa, 21. Shoola Dasa, 22. Trikona Dasa, 23. Drige Dasa, 24. Panchswara, 25. Yogini Dasa, 26. Pinda, Amsa Dasa, 27. Naisargika Dasa, 28. Ashtakavaraga Dasa, 29. Sandhya Dasa, 30. Pachaka Dasa, 32. Tara Dasa. 

CHAPTER-49 111-132 

# RESULTS OF DASAS

Vimshottari Mahadasas, The Sun, The Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury, Ketu, Venus: Benefic and malefic results of the planets surface during the Mahadasas of these planets. 

CHAPTER-50 133-143 

# RESULTS OF THE DASAS OF THE LORDS OF THE HOUSES:

Specific results of Nakshatra's Dasas, Inter-relationship and combinations of the twelve houses and their planetary lords to indicate favourable and unfavourable results of the Dasas. 

CHAPTER-51 144-157 

# RESULTS OF THE KALACHAKRA DASAS :

Results of the iords of the signs Sun, Moon, etc., detailed results of the Navamsa of signs Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, 

Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarious and Pisces. 

CHAPTER-52 

158-177 

RESULTS OF CHARA DASAS : 

Auspicious and inauspicious results of the Chara, Sthira Dasas; Results of Ascendant and twelve houses, obstructive houses of Chara signs, Results of the Dasa of a planet in an inimical sign, Results according to the disposition and status of the planets. Results of the Antardasas of the planets. 

CHAPTER-53 

178-187 

COMPUTATION OF ANTARDASAS : 

Method of computation of Antardasas of Planets under Vimshottari, Chara, Kendra Dasas etc., duly illustrated; Computation of Antardasas of the signs and sequence and special dimensions of the Antardasas of the signs; Computation of Antardasas of Pinda etc. 

CHAPTER-54 

188-215 

RESULTS OF THE ANTARDASAS IN THE MAHADASA OF THE SUN : 

Results of the Antardasas of the Sun, the Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury, Ketu and Venus in the Mahadasa of the Sun on the basis of favourable and unfavourable disposition, configuration, strength and status of the lords of the Antardasas vis-a-vis the lord of the Mahadasa, Ascendant etc., Alleviatory measures to guard against the evil effects of the Antardasas. 

CHAPTER-55 

216-234 

RESULTS OF THE ANTARDASAS IN THE MAHADASA OF THE MOON : 

Results of the Antardasas of the Moon, Mars, Rahu etc., in the Mahadasa of the Moon on the basis of favourable, unfavourable disposition, configuration, strength and status of the lords of the Antardasas vis-à-vis the lord of the Mahadasa, Ascendant etc., Alleviatory measures to guard against the adverse effects of the Antardasas.

235-256 

RESULTS OF THE ANTARDASAS IN THE MAHADASAS OF MARS :

configuration, strength and status of the lords of the Antardasas visa-vis the lord of the Mahadasa, Ascendant etc., Alleviatory measures to guard against the adverse effects of the Antardasas.

# RESULTS OF THE ANTARDASAS IN THE MAHADASA OF SATURN :

Results of the Antardasas of Saturn, Mercury, Ketu etc., in the Mahadasa of Saturn on the basis of favourable and unfavourable disposition, configuration, strength and status of the lords of the Antardasas vis-à-vis the lord of the Mahadasa, Ascendant etc., Alleviatory measures to guard against the evil effects of the Antardasas. 

CHAPTER-60 329-354 

# RESULTS OF THE ANTARDASAS IN THE MAHADASA OF MERCURY :

Results of the Antardasas of Mercury, Ketu, Venus etc., in the Mahadasa of Mercury on the basis of favourable and unfavourable disposition, configuration, strength and status of the Mahadasa, Ascendant etc., Alleviatory measures to guard against the adverse effect of the Antardasa. 

CHAPTER-61 355-378 

# RESULTS OF THE ANTARDASAS IN THE MAHADASA OF KETU:

Results of the Antardasas of Ketu, Venus, the Sun etc., in the Mahadasa of Ketu on the basis of favourable and infavourable disposition, configuration, strength and status of the Mahadasa, Ascendant etc., Alleviatory steps to guard against the evil effect of the Antardasas. 

CHAPTER-62 379-400 

# RESULTS OF THE ANTARDASAS IN THE MAHADASA OF VENUS :

Results of the Antardasas of Venus, the Sun, the Moon etc., in the Mahadasa of Venus on the basis of favourable and unfavourable disposition, configuration, strength and status of the Mahadasa, Ascendant etc., Alleviatory measures of guard against the adverse effect of the Antardasas. 

CHAPTER-63 401-428 

# RESULTS OF THE PRATYANTERDASASIN THE ANTARDASA OF PLANETS :

Method of computation of the Pratyantardasa duly illustrated, Results of the Pratyantardasas of the Sun, and other planets reference tables and illustrative horoscopes. 

CHAPTER-64 429-452 

# RESULTS OF THE SOOKSHMANTARDASAS IN THEPRATYANTARDASA OF PLANETS :

Method of computation of Sookshmadasas, Results of the Sookshmadasas in the Pratyantardasas of Sun and other planets with illustrative horoscopes. 

CHAPTER-65 453-471 

# RESULTS OF THE PRANADASAS IN THE SOOKSHMADASA OF PLANETS :

Method of computation of Pranadasa giving results in Ghatis and hours duly tabulated. Results of the Pranadasas in the Sookshmadasas of the planets with illustrative horoscopes. 

CHAPTER-66 472-497 

# RESULTS OF ANTARDASAS IN THE KALACHAKRA DASA SYSTEM :

Method of computing Antardasas of Kalchakra Dasa, with tables for 

both Savya and Apsavy<sup>1</sup> Chakras. Results of the Antardasas of the Dasas of Sign-Amsas, with Illustrative horoscopes. 

CHAPTER-67 498-504 

RESULTS OF THE DASAS OF NAVAMSA-SIGNS IN THE KALACHAKRA DASA : 

Results of signs Aries to Pisces Amsas. 

CHAPTER-68 505-530 

# ASHTAKVARGA

The purpose behind expanding the system of Ashtakavarga which indicates results relating to the life including longevity of the native. Determination of results on the basis of mutual positions of planets. The terms "Karna", (Bindus or Dots) and "Sthana" (Rekhas, or small vertic line), are fully explained. Ashtakavarga Chakras (Charts) for all the planets and Ascendant showing auspicious and inauspicious positions. 

CHAPTER-69 531-545 

TRIKONA SHODHANA (Reductions) IN ASHTAKAVARGA SYSTEM : 

Principles and method of Trikona Shodhana (Triangular Reduction) with a model example explaining each step in detail and illustrative manner summarising the principles for easy comprehension. Tabulation of benefic (auspicious) positions (Rekhas) for all the planets including Ascendant. 

CHAPTER-70 546-549 

EKADHIPATYA SHODHANA (REDUCTION) IN ASHTAKAVARGA SYSTEM : 

Principles and method of Ekadhipatya Shodhana with their application on the model example with full explanation. Summerising the principles for better understanding. 

CHAPTER-71 550-553 

PINDA SHODHANA (REDUCTION) IN ASHTAKAVARGASYSTEM : 

Principles and method of Pinda Shodhana with its application on the model example with detailed explanations and tabulation of the result. 

# CHAPTER-72

554-571 

# RESULTS OF ASHTAKAVARGA :

Determinants of the Ashtakvargas - matter, attributes and events to be considered from planets. Method of locating determination of favourable and unfavourable results on the basis of benefic positions (Rekhas) and Shodya Pinda from the Ashtakavarga of different Planets. 

- Ashtakavarga of the Moon - mother house, village. 

- Ashtakavarga of Mars - borthers, valour, courge, patience etc. 

- Ashtakavarga of Mercury - family friends, maternal uncle etc. 

- Ashtakavarga of Jupiter - knowledge, religion, children etc. 

- Astakavarga of Venus - happiness and sorrows of wife, wealth, marriage property etc. 

- Astakavarga of Saturn - Death longevity etc. 

Illustrations in respect of Trikona and Ekadhipatya Shodhanas of the planets and therefrom working out Shodya Pinda with its application. 

# CHAPTER-73

572-577 

# LONGEVITY FROM ASHTAKAVARGA :

Procedure of fixing the longevity through the use of Astakavarga System. Span of life (Ayu) assigned to Rekhas (benefic positions) to determine the longevity. 

# CHAPTER-74

578-588 

# SAMUDAYA (COLLECTIVE) ASHTAKAVARGA :

Procedure of fixing Samudaya Chakra (Chart). Results of houses compatible with the number of Rekhas-special results of Samudaya Ashtakavarga. Results denoted by Rekhas in Samudaya Ashtakvarga - use of Samudaya Ashtakavarga for all the auspicious functions. 

# CHAPTER-75

589-601 

# PLANETARY RAYS :

Planetary Rays described - Allotment of Unit of Rays to all planet at deep exaltation point. Refinement over the determined number of Rays. Working out of Rays through illustration. Results of Rays on the basis of total number of units obtained in a nativity. 

# CHAPTER-76

602-618 

# SUDARSHANA CHAKRA :

Structure of Sudarshana Chakra. Special features of Sudarshana Chakra, special effects of the auspicious and inauspicious planets. Determination 

of Cyclic Dasas viz., yearly Dasas, monthly Andasas and two and a half days Pratantar Dasas of each cycle and method of interpretation of therefrom through an illustrative example. 

CHAPTER-77 619-634 

# CHARACTERISTICS OF PANCHAMAHAPURUSHA :

Defining Panchamahapurasha Yogas viz., Ruchaka, Bhadra, Hamsa, Malavya and Sasa. Detailed analyses of the results through examples. 

CHAPTER-78 635-639 

# RESULTS OF PRIMORDIAL COMPOUND OF FIVE ELEMENTS (PANCHABHUTAS) :

Planets rule over five elements (Tatwas) Fire, Earth, Ether, Water and Air described. Determination of temperament on the basis of the predominance of the strength of a planet ruling particular Tatwa. 

CHAPTER 79 640-649 

# SATWA, RAJO AND TAMO GUNAS :

Results on the basis of Gunas (Quality) classifications of Gunas-Uttama (best), Madhama (moderate), Adhama (worst) and Udaseena (neutral). Characteristics on the basis of these classification. Philosophy benide these Gunas fully explained. 

CHAPTER 80 650-658 

# LOST HOROSCOPY :

Erecting of horoscope based on question time of the person, whose details are unknown through an illustrative example. 

CHAPTER 81 659-663 

# YOGAS CAUSING ASCETICISM :

Varieties of Asceticism (Pravrajya) on the basis of the strength of planet forming such Yoga. 

CHAPTER 82 664-694 

# FEMALE HOROSCOPY :

Peculiarties of functions of 7th and 8th houses in female horoscopy. Feminine and masculine characteristics. Results to be interpreted strongest between the Ascendant and the Moon. Analysis of the results on the basis of the Ascendant or the Moon falling in the Trimsamsa of various planets which indicate characteristics of the female native and her husband. Results of the signs and Navamsas of the 7th house and planets posited in the 8th house. Yogas of barrenness and widowhood. 

Fortunate and unfortunate Yogas. Yoga for the still born child. Yoga of Visha-Kanya and cancellation of the same. Yoga for Lesbianism. Yogas of death before and alongwith husband. 

CHAPTER 83 695-713 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE PARTS OF WOMAN'S BODY 

： 

Auspicious and inauspicious results of the size, shape, lustre marks like Conch, Swastika, Chakra, lotus, fish etc. on the various parts (links) of the body of a woman. These results are as well applicable for the male natives. 

CHAPTER 84 714-717 

BODY-MARKS LIKE MOLES ETC. 

Results of the marks like mole, warts, whirling hairs, sign etc. on the body. 

CHAPTER 85 718-738 

CURSES OF THE PREVIOUS BIRTH : 

Yogas of childlessness attributing to the curses of the previous birth of the Serpant, forefathers, mother, brother, material uncle, Brahmins, wife, departed spirits. Propitatory measures to alleviate the evil effects. 

CHAPTER-86 739-744 

PROPITIATION OF PLANETS CAUSING AFFILIATION: 

Propiliatory measures through Yugnas, worship of delics etc. for the attainment peace, perpetual prosperity, virtuous knowledge, longevity etc. Specification of idols to be worshipped, method of conducting worship of planets. Number of recitation of Mantras. Mode of performing Havana. 

CHAPTER 87 745-746 

INAUSPICIOUS BIRTHS : 

Cause of inauspicious births and the propitory measures. 

CHAPTER 88 747-749 

PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING AMAVASYA : 

Effects of Amavasya births. Method of worship and other religious rites to be performed to extirpate the affliction. 

CHAPTER 89 750-752 

BIRTHS DURING KRISHNA-CHATAURDASHI : 

Results of births in the different parts of Krishna-Chataurdashl. Prescribed 

rituals for propitiatory measures for the alleviation of evil effects. 

CHAPTER 90 753-754 

# BIRTHS DURING BHADRA AND OTHER INAUSPICIOUSYOGAS :

Kinds of inauspicious Yogas producing evil effects and alleviatory measures to be adopted. 

CHAPTER 91 755-756 

# BIRTHS IN THE RELATIVES BIRTH NAKSHATRAS:

Evil effects caused by the births in the Nakshatras of brother and parents and alleviatory measures to ward off such evil effects. 

CHAPTER 92 757-760 

# PROPRIITION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING SOLAR INGRESS (SANKRANTI).

Names of solar ingress falling on the week days causing affliction. Prescribed rituals for propitiatory measures to mitigate the ill effects. 

CHAPTER 93 761-763 

# PROPRIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING ECLIPSES:

Evil effects of the births during the solar or lunar eclipses explained. Method of propitation for the alleviation of such afflictions. 

CHAPTER 94 764-766 

# PROPRIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING GANDANTA:

Three types of Gandanta namely : Tithi, Nakshatra and Ascendant (Lagna) and there evil effects. Method of propitiation to mitigate such affications. 

CHAPTER 95 767-770 

# PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS FOR BIRTHSDURING ABHUKTA MOOLA :

Evil effects of the births during Abhukta Moola (Jyesta and Moola Gandanta) described. Propitiatory measures and religious rituals to get such affliction alleviated. 

CHAPTER 96 771-773 

# PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING JYESHTAGANDANTA :

Evil effects of the births during Jyeshta Gandanta. Method of propitiation 

to ward off such afflictions. 

CHAPTER 97 

774-775 

PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR TRITARA BIRTHS : 

Kinds of afflictions causing Tritara births i.e., birth of a daughter after three sons or vis-a-versa and propitiatory measures to alleviate the evil effects. 

CHAPTER 98 

776-778 

PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR ABNORMAL BIRTHS: 

Abnormal births (delivery) explained. Evil effects of these births. Procedure of propitiation to get relieved from such afflictions. 

CHAPTER 99 

779-793 

HORARY SYSTEM 

General principles of Horary Systems. To know events or matter from the houses of the query chart. Deceitful or misleading queries. Query of success or failure. Query held in first or thought of-about Dhatu, Jeeva or Moola. Queries about Travelling, Returning house and Distress during Journey. Queries about Pregnancy, Birth and Death of child. Queries about Death and Recovery of Patient. 

CHAPTER 100 

794-797 

EPILOGUE

# DASA SYSTEMS

1: Maitreya said, O Supreme Sage! You are omniscient and have infinite knowledge. Kindly be merciful and enlighten me, in detail, about the prevalent "Planetary Dasas" (Periods). 

# Types of Dasas :

sahuy yuot khya 1

2-5: Maharishi Parasara said, O Brahmin! You have asked a most appropriate question which will benefit one and all. I now unfold the secret of the prevailing Dasa systems. 

There are various types of Dasas in vogue but for a common man, Vimshottari (विरागोल्ति) Dasa is the predominant one. Some have mentioned particularly Astottari (अस्थोल्ति) and Shodsottari (स्थोल्ति) Dasas. O Brahmin! Ancients have also referred to Dwadasottari (धानोल्ति), Panchottari (पनोल्ति), Shatabdika (स्थानायका) Dasas and in the same category there are also Chaturashiti-Sama (धुरूल्ति समा), Dwisaptati-Sama (धिकान्वि समा), Shashtihayani (स्थिकान्वि), Shatrishanta-Sama (धतिकान्वि समा) Dasas. All these Dasas are based on Nakshatras. 

# Other kinds of Dasas :

6-11: The Rishis have also made mention of Kala (काला) and Chakara (मिका) Dasas. But amongst the remaining Dasas, Kala Dasa has the greatest recognition. O Brahmin! Besides these Dasas, some have said about Chara, Sthir, Kendra, Karaka and Brahmagraha (रिरागा) Dasas. O Vipra! apart from these, Mandooki (मामूल्या), Shoola (मिका), Yogardha (मोगांा), Driga (मृगा) followed by Trikona (तिका) and Rashi (रागा) Dasas are also there. 

O Brahmin! Panchswara Dasa, Yogini Dasa, Pinda Dasa, Amsa Dasa, Naisargie Dasa, Ashtavarga Dasa, Sandhya Dasa, Pachaka Dasa and other Tara Dasa and the like are the different form of Dasas. However, out of these various Dasa forms, all are not acceptable by common consensus. 

Notes : There are many Dasas referred to in this classical work of Sage Parasara. Amongst these, Vimsottari, Kalachakra, Astottari, Chara, Yogini, Shoola, Astakvarga Dasas etc. are being persuaded in the different parts of the country. However, Vimshottari Dasas is the most popular one and predominantly used throughout the country. This Dasa system sets a time frame of events in the life span of a native. These events and happenings are delineated on the basis of the strength and disposition of the planets having their Dasas, Antardasas etc., ruling at that particular point of time. Besides, the result will also largely depend upon the interrelated influences of the Dasa lords with the other planets i.e. conjunction, aspects etc. in the birth chart. 

In some editions, Anshaja Dasa has not been mentioned but the word (ल्यादी) figured in the 10th Shloka, gives a clear indication of this Dasa. As such Anshaja Dasa also needs to be considered. 

In the foregoing stanzas, Maharishi Parasara has described about 32 types of Dasas. For the convenience and quick recapitulation of the esteemed readers, these Dasas are tabulated as per Table-1. 

# 1. Vimshottari Dasa

Kutitikakat: SmmarRy  ruruy dshaaahya:1

12-14: Commencing from Kritika, the Dasa lords in succession would be Sun, Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury, Ketu, Venus; in the three cycles of Nakshatras. The number of Nakshatras between Kritika and the birth Nakshatra be divided

by 9. The remainder will indicate the Dasa lord if counted from the Sun. (Let us suppose the remainder being 5, then the Dasa lord will be Jupiter at the time of birth. The subsequent Dasas will be in the same order i.e. after Jupiter comes the Dasa of Saturn, Mercury, Ketu and so on). 

O Brahmin! The full span of life of man in 'Kalayuga' is said to be 120 years (Purna Ayu). Therefore, amongst the various Dasas, Vimshottari Dasa is the prime Dasa system. 

Notes : There are 27 Nakshatras. In order to find out the Dasa ruling at the time of birth, count the number of Nakshatras which have passed from Kritika to the birth Nakshatra and divide the number by 9 i.e. total number of planets including Rahu and Ketu and each Nakshatra has a planet assigned as its lord. 

The remainder will indicate the Dasa lord counting it from the Sun. Suppose the birth was in Dhanista Nakshatra i.e. 23rd Nakshatra. From Kritika, its number comes to 21 (both inclusive) the remainder will be 3 if this figure is divided by 9. The third planet Dasa-lord is Mars from Sun. Hence, at the time of birth, Dasa of Mars will be in operation. 

# Span of Mahadasas (Main Periods)

deshaasamata: kmaee  aeg dshaaesvga jne 

nupalata nabvcbnabvcb nagvcbnra nga nka: 1194 11 

15: The number of years assigned to each planet (lord of the Dasa) the Sun, the Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury, Ketu and Venus are 6, 10, 7, 18, 16, 19, 17, 7, 20 respectively. The Dasa at the time is determined from the Nakshatra in which Moon is in transit at the time of birth which is called birth (Janma) Nakshatra. 

Notes: As stated above, there are 27 Nakshatras and 9 planets. Therefore, each planet will have lordship over three Nakshatras. The Sun has lordship over Kritika and the 9th Nakshatra thereafter is Uttara Phalguni and yet after another 9 Nakshatras, comes Uttara Ashada. Likewise 3 sets of Nakshatras are allotted to the other planets. As disclosed in the preceding stanza, the birth Nakshatra is the one occupied by the Moon at the time of the birth and lord of birth Nakshatra will have 

the Dasa running at birth. 

From the table given below, the readers can early ascertain the planet, their Dasa years and Nakshatras represented by them. 


TABLE-1A Planetary Dasa years and their Nakshatras

Note: $\mathrm{P} =$ Poorva, $\mathrm{U} =$ Uttara 


# Balance of Dasa

davahamannn mayalatcln mghoogon n hult faklma I dhyaay ma uukvatvabg y oagm maanadu vshoohitam 1951 

16: Multiply the Dasa years of the planet, in whose Dasa the birth takes place by Bhayaata [The expired (elapsed) period of the Nakshatra] and divide it by Bhabhoga [The full period to be traversed by the Nakshatra]. The resultant value will represent the period consumed in the Dasa of the planet till the time of birth. By deducting this figure from the full duration of the Dasa of the planet, the balance Dasa of the said planet is arrived at. (For terms Bhagyaata and Bhabhoga please refer to page 65 of volume 1). 

# Note:

Example-1: Data: Date of birth 11/12-6-1981; Time of birth - 1-30 (night), Place of birth; Latitude $28^{\circ}$ -43' N, Longitude $77^{\circ}$ -55'E (The calculations are based on Vishwa Panchanga of B.H. University, Varanasi, India). 

# Method of calculation

From the above Panchanga, the following data are recorded : 

Birth at 50 Gh. 45 Pal. (in 4th quarter of Hasta Nakshatra) 

Bhayaata = 61 Gh. 13 Pal. 

Bhabhoga $= 64$ Gh. 44 Pal. 

# Step - 1

Number of Nakshatras between Kritika and Hasta =11 

Dasa $= 11 + 9$ ; remainder 2. 

The 2nd Dasa from Sun will be of the Moon which is of 10 years duration. 

# Step - 2

In order to find out the expired period of the planet up to the time of birth, the following equation (as per shloka-16) may be applied: 

Elapsed portion = Bhayaata X Duration of Dasa of the planet of the Dasa Bhabhoga 

Bhayaata $= 61$ Gh. 13 Pal. $= 3673$ Pal. 

Bhabhoga $= 64$ Gh. 44 Pal. $= 3884$ Pal. 

= Elapsed portion $= \frac{3673 \times 10}{3884} = \frac{36730}{3884}$ of Dasa upto birth 

# Calculations :

<table><tr><td>Division</td><td>Dividend</td><td>Quotient</td></tr><tr><td>3884</td><td>36730</td><td>9</td></tr><tr><td>(-)</td><td>34956</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>1774 x 12*</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>3884</td><td>21288</td><td>5</td></tr><tr><td>(-)</td><td>19420</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>1868 x 30**</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>3884</td><td>56040</td><td>14</td></tr><tr><td>(-)</td><td>54376</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>1664</td><td></td></tr></table>

$= 9$ years, 5 months, 14 days. 

Note: 1 yr. = 12\*; months = 30\*\* days 

# Alternative method of calculation

$$
\frac {3 6 7 3 0}{3 8 8 4} = 9. 4 5 6
$$

$$
= 9 \text {y e a r s} + 0. 4 5 6
$$

$$
\begin{array}{l} 0. 4 5 6 \times 1 2 = 5. 4 7 2 = 5 \text {m o n t h s} + 0. 4 7 2 \\ 0. 4 7 2 \times 3 0 = 1 4. 1 6 = 1 4 d a y s \\ \end{array}
$$

9 years, 5 months and 14 days. 

Out of the total period of 10 years of the Moon, 9 years, 5 months and 14 days had since consumed at the time of birth, leaving the balance of Dasa of 6 months and 16 days. 

# Alternative Method

In the present time, the majority of the astrologers work out balance Dasathrough "Ephemeris" instead of Panchangas. The ephemeris record the planetary movements etc. in degree and minutes rather than in Ghatis and Palas. Let us take another example and work out balance of Dasa by employing the data from Lahiri Ephemeris for the year 1994. 

Example - 2: "Lahiri's Ephemeris" Birth Data : Date of birth : 15-8-1994; Time of birth : 4.30 p.m.; Place of birth : Latitude : $28^{\circ}$ -43' N Longitude $77^{\circ}$ -55'E. 

# Method of Calculation :

# Step 1

To start with, we need to focus on the Moon's longitude at the time of birth to locate the birth Nakshatra. 

The movements of the planets in the ephemeris are with respect to 5-30 a.m., each day. The Moon therefore, traversed by 11 hrs. at the time of birth (5-30 AM - 4-30 p.m.) on that day. 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td colspan="2">Deg. Mts.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of the Moon on 16-8-1994</td><td>7</td><td>22</td><td>5</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of the Moon on 15-8-1994</td><td>(-)</td><td>7</td><td>08</td></tr><tr><td>Motions in 24 hrs.</td><td>0</td><td>14</td><td>2</td></tr></table>

By referring to log table (Table-2) we find :- 

$\mathrm{Log}14^{\circ} - 02^{\circ}$ $= 0.23305$ 

Log 11 hrs. $= \frac{0.33882}{0.57187}$ 

Near equivalent of log. 0.57181 = 6dg. 26 mts. 

The longitude of the Moon at 4.30 PM on 15.8.1994 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mts.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of the Moon at 5.30 a.m.</td><td>7</td><td>08</td><td>03</td></tr><tr><td>Traversed up to 4.30 p.m. (+)</td><td>0</td><td>6</td><td>26</td></tr><tr><td>Moon&#x27;s longitude at 4.30 p.m.</td><td>7</td><td>14</td><td>29</td></tr></table>

The longitude of the Moon at the time of birth : Scorpio 14 deg. 29 mts. 

[If the table is not availale, Moon's longitude can also be found out in this way. Moon's 24 hrs. (1440') motion on 15.8.94 is $14^{\circ} - 02'$ (842'). Time lapsed upto 4-30 PM from 5-30 AM = 11 hrs. (660 '). The Moon's movement upto 4-30 PM (birth time) = 842 x 660 + 1440 = 380 = 6°-26'.]

# TABLE-2

# Step - 2 : Balance of Dasa :

The longitudinal span of a sign (Rasi) is $30^{\circ}$ and of Nakshatra is $13^{\circ} - 20^{\circ}$ . As such two and a quarter Nakshatras or 9 quarters of Nakshtras are accommodated under each sign. That is (27 Nakshatra 2 $1/4 \times 12 = 27$ ) or 108 quarters ( $27 \times 4 = 108$ ) are in the ambit of twelve signs. 

The sign, number of Nakshatra under each sign (quarter-wise) and the planetary lords of the the Nakshatras have been tabulated vide Table-3, for the convenience of the readers and placed at Table 3. This table will serve as a ready reckoner to locate the birth Nakshatra and Dasa lord from the longitude of the Moon at the time of birth. 

The longitude of the Moon in Scorpio Sign $14^{\circ}$ -29' falls in the 4th quarter of Anuradha Nakshatra having the lordship of Saturn. Hence, Saturn Dasa was in operation at the time of birth whose duration is of 19 years. 

i) Total period (length) of Anuradha Nak. 

$$
\text {i n} \quad 3 ^ {\circ} - 2 0 ^ {\prime} \text {t o} 1 6 ^ {\circ} - 4 0 ^ {\prime}) = 1 3 ^ {\circ} - 2 0 ^ {\prime} = 8 0 0 ^ {\prime}
$$

ii) Balance period of Anuradha Nak. 

$$
\left[ \left(1 6 ^ {\circ} - 4 0 ^ {\prime}\right) - \left(1 4 ^ {\circ} - 2 9 ^ {\prime}\right) \right] = 2 ^ {\circ} - 1 1 ^ {\prime} = 1 3 1 ^ {\prime}
$$

iii) Total span of Saturn Dasa $= 19$ yrs = 6840 days 

Balance of Dasa at birth : 

$$
\begin{array}{l} = \frac {6 8 4 0 \times 1 3 1}{8 0 0} \\ = 1 1 2 0 \text {d a y s} \\ \end{array}
$$

3 years-1 month-10 days. 

# Calculation of Balance of Dasa from the table :

A most all the ephemarids carry a table for calculating the balance of Vimshottari Dasa at birth on the basis of sidereal longitude of the : $\text{son}$ . A similar table is also placed as Table-4 to ascertain the balance of Dasa without getting involved into the complicated calculations.

# Method to locate balance Dasa :

The first column indicates Moon's longitude from $00^{\circ}$ to $30^{\circ}$ at an interval of $20'$ . The subsequent four columns represent a group of three signs. Down below in these columns, we find the planet (being the Dasa lord) and years, months and days corresponding to the longitude of the Moon. 

In order to find the balance of the Dasa, we have first to locate the nearest Moon's longitudinal position. Similarly, the column of the Moon sign is to be identified. Now move horizontally from this point of nearest Moon's longitude up to the column of natal Moon sign and record the balance Dasa in years, months and days. Thereafter, the proportionate part for the longitude of Moon (i.e. with in $20^{\circ}$ ) may be located from Table-4B under the column of identified Dasa lord and subtract from the figure arrived at from Table-4A to know the exact Dasa balance of Dasa at the time of birth of the native. 

An illustration will give a clear insight on the use of the table. We take the data of the alternative method of example-1. In this case, the Moon is in Scorpio with longitude of $14^{\circ}$ -29' at the time of birth. The nearest Moon's longitude in the Table is $14^{\circ}$ -20' and the balance of Dasa at this longitude is to be found out from the last column which represents signs Cancer, Scorpio and Pisces. It indicates ruling Dasa of Saturn with balance period as: 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Years</td><td>Months</td><td>Days</td></tr><tr><td>At 14°-20&#x27;</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>27</td></tr><tr><td>for 0&#x27;-9&#x27; (Less)</td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>(from table-4B)</td><td>0</td><td>2</td><td>17</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>3</td><td>1</td><td>10</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>3 years</td><td>1 month</td><td>10 days</td></tr></table>

# Mahadasa (Main Period) :

In the previous pages, there is frequent mention of 'Dasa'. The expression 'Dasa' over there actually relates to Mahadasa or Main Period 

of the concerned planet. Besides, Mahadasa, there are Antar Dasa (sub period), Pratyantar Dasa (Sub-Sub period) etc. these terms will be adequately explained as we progress further in this chapter. Therefore, the expression 'Dasa' intends for Mahadasa (Main period) and shall not be confused with any other kind of Dasas viz. Antar or Pratyantar etc. (Sub or Sub-Sub period). The balance of Dasa of the planet at the time of birth is worked out in the preceding paragraphs is also in respect of unconsumed Mahadasa of the said planet. With this unexpired major period of the planet, commences the Dasa cycle of 120 years in Vimsottari Dasa system. Let us lift the position of balance Dasa from Example-2 and draw the Dasa cycle. 

<table><tr><td>Mahadasa of the Planet</td><td>Day</td><td>Month</td><td>Year</td></tr><tr><td>Date of birth</td><td>15</td><td>8</td><td>1994</td></tr><tr><td>Balance Dasa of Saturn</td><td>10</td><td>1</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Saturn Dasa upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>1997</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Mercury upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2014</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Ketu upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2021</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Venus upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2041</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Sun upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2047</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Moon upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2057</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Mars upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2064</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Rahu upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2087</td></tr><tr><td>Period of Jupiter upto</td><td>25</td><td>9</td><td>2103</td></tr></table>

# 2. ASTOTTARI DASA

Lalgnneghaata, kaneoogaray 15n 

Aapctotarati dshaa vipr ！vishoya rroedhoadelit: |190 

Chutuokr Tritatay Taksmaat U Chutuokr Tritatay yun:1 

Eevv 11 

Suhyavghan: kuj: 50mmy: gaijivk 

「迦 言：

RrS: Pooe-ndv o NaG: SaPovnVnOv 

Goto: 

17-20: Maharishi Parasara said, O Brahmin! If Rahu excepting in the Ascendant (Lagna) is posited in an Angle (Kendra) or a Trine (Trikona) from the lord of Ascendant (Lagna lord), some learneds adopt Astottari Dasa system. 

The Astottari Dasa commences with the Sun Dasa covering four Nakshatras with Ardrabeing the first one. Three Nakshatras thereafter are assigned to the Moon. Next four go to Mars followed by three Nakshatras for Mercury Dasa. Again, next four Nakshatras are entrusted to Saturn Dasa which is succeeded by Jupiter Dasa with subsequent three Nakshatras. Rahu Dasa comes next with four and last is the Dasa of Venus with three left over Nakshatras. (These planets are the lord of the Nakshatras which are under their fold). The ruling Dasa lord is determined through counting in the above order upto the birth Nakshatra. 

O Brahmin! Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Saturn, Jupiter, Rahu and Venus are the Dasa lords. Excepting Ketu, all the other planets are covered in this system. The Dasa period starting from the Sun is 6, 15, 8, 17, 10, 19, 12 and 21 years respectively. 

Notes : The Astottari Dasa system is mainly practised in the Eastern and North-Eastern part of the country. The eminent Astrologers of these parts, rely more on Astottari system rather than any other Dasa, (Except Vimsottari Dasa system which has its own place in these parts also) the astrologers though of the opinion that in 'Kalyuga', Vimsottari Dasa system is more efficient than Astottari. 

There is unanimity on the fact that if the native is born in Shukla Paksha, (brighter half of the lunar course) the prediction should be done through Vimsottari Dasa and for the birth during Krishna Paksha, (darker half of the lunar course) Asttotari Dasa system be employed. 

Astottari Dasa has 108 years cycle and Ketu is not in the scheme of things. However, Abhijita Nakshatra has been included and as such 28 Nakshatras are operative in this system. It may, however, be noted that Abhijita has no independent status. The span (arc or length of operation) of Abhijita Nakshatra constitutes of the last quarter of Uttar Ashada and first 1/15th part of Shravana Nakshatras. One Nakshatra has an arc of $13^{\circ} - 20'$ (800'). With the contribution of Uttar Ashada and Shravana Nakshatra, the arc of Abhijita works out as under: 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Mts.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>1/4th part of Uttara Ashada</td><td>= 200</td><td>- 00</td></tr><tr><td>1/15th part of Shravana</td><td>= 53</td><td>- 20</td></tr><tr><td>The arc of Abhijita</td><td>= 253</td><td>- 20</td></tr></table>

Consequently, the arc of Uttara Ashada and Shravana Nakshatras gets reduced to : 

<table><tr><td>Uttara Ashada</td><td>=</td><td>800&#x27; - 200&#x27;</td><td>=</td><td>600&#x27; - 00&quot;</td></tr><tr><td>Shravana</td><td>=</td><td>800 - (53-20&#x27;)</td><td>=</td><td>746&#x27; - 40&quot;</td></tr></table>

The native is said to have been born in Uttar Ashada if the birth is in the first three quarters of the Nakshatra. Similarly, the birth will be considered, in Shravana Nakshatra and if it is after $53' - 20''$ of the 1st quarter of the Nakshatra. When birth occurs in the gap of $253' - 20''$ between Uttara Ashada and Shravana Nakshatras, the native supposed to have taken birth in Abhijita Nakshatra. The Bhayaata and Bhabhoga of these three Nakshatras should be worked out keeping these factors in view. 

# Method of Calculating Astottari Dasa :

davahadabnayapamana nuyu maan a yev h!   
eukkne kme dharamaan n bojeyy bjjasatlatm 112   
tataaataaataaagaaomgaaoy a uokot mogy ch saayoyetl   
vsharai rarivde va a tataatctatcakalmaaadiyohel . 112211 

21-22: O Brahmin! "In the event of malefic planets, the span (period) of each Nakshatra is 1/4th of the prescribed period of the planet while in the case of benefic planets, each Nakshatra's span is to the extent of 1/3rd period of the planet. The expired period of the birth Nakshatra is ascertained in the same manner as in Vimsottari Dasa system, that is the period traversed by the birth Nakshatra in Astottari Dasa be multiplied by Bhayaata and divided by Bhabhoga of the Nakshatra. The Dasa balance is also arrived at in the same fashion". 

Kc 

Lahdahdahpctoeratri chinnya klaayyov bivohyol: 112311 

23: O Brahamin! "If the birth is in the day time during Krishna Paksha or in the night in Shukla Paksha and in order to ascertain specific results, Astottari Dasa be adopted". 

Notes : The fundamental difference between Vimsottari and Astottari Dasa systems is that in Vimsottari system, the Nakshatras are allotted in the planetary order. Such as Kritika is ruled by the Sun, Rohini is assigned to the Moon and Mrigshira to Mars and so on, while the Astottari Dasa is altogether on different pattern. Here, malefic planets are allotted four Nakshatras and benefic planets get three Nakshatras. For example : the Sun has Aridra, Punarvasu, Pushya and Ashlesha Nakshatras in his fold, but Jupiter being a benefic planet gets three Nakshatras viz., Dhanishta, Satbisha and Poorva-Bhadarpad. Further, in this Dasa system, the period of the planet is equally distributed amongst the Nakshatra over which it has the dominance. Thus Mars has 8 years of Dasa which has been apportioned between Hasta, Chitra, Swati and Vishakha Nakshatras each getting 2 years. Similar treatment is given to the Nakshatras of the other planets. On the basis of Shlokas 17 to 21, Astottari Dasa table is drawn and shown as Table No.-5.

Bhadra</td><td>3</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>27</td><td>Revati</td><td>3</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>1</td><td>Aswini</td><td>3</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>2</td><td>Bharani</td><td>3</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td></td><td>TOTAL</td><td>12</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td TOTAL</td><td>108</td><td>00</td></tr></table>

Notes: Each Nakshatra arc of $13^{\circ} - 20'$ (800') except Uttara-Ashada, Abhijita and Shravana Nakshatras, their arc is $10^{\circ}$ (600'), $4^{\circ} - 13' - 20''$ (253'-20") and $12^{\circ} - 26' - 40''$ (746'-40") respectively. 

# Balance of Dasa

As per Shloka 21-22, the procedure of ascertaining 'Dasa Balance' of the planet at the time of birth is the same as in the case of Vimsottari Dasa. The first step is to locate the associated Nakshatra of the Moon at birth time. (Janma Nakshatra). The corresponding planet, of the birth Nakshatra (refer Table-3) will have the ruling Dasa at the time of birth. As stated earlier so also is evident from Table-5, the total period of the planet has been equally divided amongst the Nakshatras in its fold. Accordingly, Bhayaata and Bhabhoga of the birth Nakshatra is to be found out like Vimsottari Dasa system and balance Dasa is ascertained. The periods of the subsequent Nakshatras (of the Dasa lord) to the birth Nakshatras, if any, is to be added to arrive at the Dasa balance of the planet being the Dasa lord. 

From Table-5, it may be seen that the span of each Nakshatra is $880^{\prime}(13^{\circ} - 20^{\prime})$ except the Nakshatras of Saturn as indicated in the foot note. The Satrun's Nakshatra are having varied spans due to the introduction of Abhijita Nakshatra whose arc is carved from Uttara Ashada and Shravana Nakshatras as explained previously. Therefore, at the time of birth if any of these Nakshatras is involved, a special care must be taken in calculating the Dasa balance. Since span of Uttara Ashada is reduced by one quater, only first three quaters shall be taken into account for Bhabhoga of this Nakshatra. 

# Example - 3

<table><tr><td></td><td></td><td>Gh.</td><td>Pal.</td></tr><tr><td>Suppose Bhabhoga of Uttara-Ashada</td><td>=</td><td>64</td><td>20</td></tr><tr><td>Bhayaata (period already consumed)</td><td>=</td><td>25</td><td>20</td></tr></table>

It shows that the native was born in the 2nd quarter of Uttara-Ashada (one quater = 16Gh:5Pal) and Bhayaata is between 1st and 2nd quarters. Since 1/4 span goes to Abhijita and is reduced from Bhabhoga, the operative Bhabhoga of Uttara Ashada will be :- 

$$
\begin{array}{c c} \text {G h .} & \text {P a l .} \\ 6 0 & 2 0 \\ (-) & 1 6 & 0 5 \\ \hline 4 8 & 1 5 \end{array} = 2 8 9 5 \text {P a l .}
$$

However, Bhayaata 25 Gh. 20 Pal. (1520 Pal.) will remain unchanged. 

Uttar-Ashada is assigned to Satum who has period of 10 years or 120 months and each Nakshatras has 2 yrs.-6 months = 30 months (see Table-4). Therefore : 

$$
\frac {\text {E x p i r e d P e r i o d} = \frac {\text {B h a y a t a} \times 3 0}{\text {B h a b h o g a}} = \frac {1 5 2 0 \times 3 0}{2 8 9 5}}{2 8 9 5} = \frac {4 5 6 0 0}{2 8 9 5}
$$

$$
\frac {\text {B h a y a t a} \times 2 . 5 \text {y r s}}{\text {B h a b h o g a}} = \frac {1 5 2 0 \times 2 . 5}{2 8 9 5} = 1. 3 1 \text {y r}.
$$

$$
1. 3 1 = 1 \mathrm {y r} + 0. 3 1
$$

$$
0. 3 1 \times 1 2 = 3. 7 2 = 3 \text {m o n t h s} + 0. 7 2
$$

$$
0. 7 2 \times 3 0 = 2 1. 6 = 2 2 d a y s
$$

$$
= \quad 1 \mathrm {y r} \quad 3 \text {m o n t h s} \quad 2 2 \text {d a y s}
$$

$$
(+) 2 \quad 6 \quad 0 0 \quad (\text {P e r i o d o f A s h l e y a})
$$

$$
\begin{array}{c c c} 3 & 9 & 2 2 \\ \hline \end{array} \quad (\text {E x p i r e d p e r i o d o f S a t u m})
$$

Balance of Saturn's Dasa 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Yrs</td><td>Months</td><td>Days</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>10</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>(-)</td><td>3</td><td>9</td><td>22</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>6</td><td>2</td><td>8</td></tr></table>

<table><tr><td>Divisor</td><td>Dividend</td><td>Quotient</td></tr><tr><td>2895</td><td>45600</td><td>15</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>43425</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>2175 x 30</td><td></td></tr></table>

<table><tr><td>Divisor</td><td>Dividend</td><td>Quotient</td></tr><tr><td>2895</td><td>65250</td><td>22</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>62690</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>1560 x 60</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>2895</td><td>93600</td><td>32</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>92640</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>960 x 60</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>2895</td><td>57600</td><td>19+1</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>55005</td><td>20</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>2595 say 1</td><td></td></tr></table>


OR


15 months, 22 days 32 Gh, 20 Pal 

or 

1 yr 3 months, 22 days 32 Gh, 20 Pal 

Since at the time of birth 2yrs.-6 months (30 months) period of Poorva Ashada had already been consumed, this period should be added to the expired portion of Uttara-Ashada to arrive at the expired period of Satum. 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Years</td><td>Months</td><td>Days</td><td>Gh.</td><td>Pal</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>2</td><td>6</td><td>00</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>(+)</td><td>1</td><td>3</td><td>22</td><td>32</td><td>20</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>3</td><td>9</td><td>22</td><td>32</td><td>20</td></tr></table>

By deducting the period already consumed in the saturn Dasa till the time of birth from the natal period of 10 years, we get the 'Dasa Balance' of Satum at birth time. 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Years</td><td>Months</td><td>Days</td><td>Gh.</td><td>Pal.</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>10</td><td>00</td><td>00</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>(-)</td><td>3</td><td>9</td><td>22</td><td>32</td><td>20</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>6</td><td>2</td><td>7</td><td>27</td><td>40</td></tr></table>

# Alternative Method :

The 'balance Dasa' can also be worked out in the following manner: 

Deduct the expired period of Uttara-Ashada from the full period of the Nakshatra. 

<table><tr><td>Months</td><td>Days</td><td>Gh.</td><td>Pal.</td></tr><tr><td>30</td><td>00</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>(-)</td><td>15</td><td>22</td><td>32</td></tr><tr><td>14</td><td>7</td><td>27</td><td>40</td></tr></table>

Add 60 months (30 months each of Abhijita and Sharavana (last two Nakshatras of Saturn) we get : 

<table><tr><td>Months</td><td>Days</td><td>Gh.</td><td>Pal.</td></tr><tr><td>14</td><td>7</td><td>27</td><td>40</td></tr><tr><td>(+) 60</td><td>00</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>74</td><td>7</td><td>27</td><td>40</td></tr></table>

6 years, 2 months, 7 days 27 Gh. and 40 Pal. 

Says 6 years, 2 months and 7 days. 

Like Vimsottari Dasa, Astottari's balance Dasa can also be calculated on the basis of longitude of the Moon at birth time. The method is like this, convert the longitude into minutes and divide it by $800'$ ( $13^\circ - 20'$ arc of the Nakshatra). The quotient will indicate the expired period of the preceding Nakshatras and the remainder will represent the period consumed by the birth Nakshatra. By deducting from the total span, the unexpired period of the birth Nakshatra is arrived at. 

# Example-4:

Longitude of the Moon, from the example of Vimshottari Dasa may be referred. 

Moon's longitude : 0 Sign, 26 deg., 47 mts. 25 Sec. 

$$
\begin{array}{l} = 1 6 0 7 ^ {\prime} - 2 5 ^ {\prime \prime} o r 1 6 0 7. 4 2 \\ = 1 6 0 7 ^ {\prime}. 4 2 + 8 0 0 = 2 \text {r e m a i n d e r} 7. 4 2 \\ \end{array}
$$

The full periods of Aswini and Bharani Nakshatra have since expired and 7.42' of Kritika (birth Nakshatra) has also been consumed. From Table-5, it may be seen that Kritika is the first Nakshatra of Venus. Hence Venus Dasa is running at the time of birth. 

# Balance period of Kritika :

The total period of Kritika is 7 years or 2555 days. 

Period already consumed Kritika $= \frac{7.42 \times 2555}{800} = 23.70$ Days say 24 days 

Balance period = Yrs. Months Days
Total Period = 7 00 00
Less: Period consumed = 00 00 24
6 11 6 

# Balance Dasa of Venus :

Balance of Kritika = 6 11 6  
Period of Rohini = 7 00 00  
Period of Mrigshira = 7 00 00  
20 11 6 

20 years, 11 months and 6 days 

# Alternative Method :

The balance of Dasa of Venus could be worked out straight away. Since Kritika is the first Nakshatra in Venus, the period consumed by this Nakshatra will be the period in Venus Dasa at the time of birth. Therefore, the expired period of Venus will be as follows: 

$$
= \frac {7 . 4 2 \times 2 1 \times 3 6}{2 4 0 0} = 2 3. 3 7 \text {d a y s} \text {s a y} 2 4 \text {d a y s}
$$

# Balance Dasa of Venus :

Note: In astrology 360 days are taken in a year 30 days each for all the 12 months.. 

# 3. Shodshottari Dasa

Chn-ohroRaragot Kooo 00yohoragot 1   
Lgnne nuaa Pkafalzakv y Dvirchinrtay aodshoartari I 12r I   
puymaaonnnmnae maovd y na sckya gajtatae tata I   
raivimao gukhmnd: koteuovcnuo 00hoyo 00g: I 124 I   
iit kmaud davahayia: oeya raai n gnaha: I   
kdaee koraatara: saokyaa dhylannt vatsarra: kmaul I 125 I 

24-26: If Ascendant (Lagna) is in the Hora of the Moon in Krishna Paksha (waning Moon) or in the Sun's Hora during Shukla Paksha (waxing Moon), Shodsottari Dasa be adopted for prognosication. Count the number of Nakshatras from Pushyami to the birth Nakshatra and divide it by 8. The remainder will point out the Dasa of the planet barring Rahu, if counted in the order of the Sun, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Ketu, Moon, Mercury and Venus. The Dasa years are from 11 to 18 years with an accretion by one year for each planet in seriatim (i.e., Sun 11, Mars 12, Jupiter 13, Saturn 14, Ketu 15, Moon 16, Mercury 17 and Venus 18 Years). 

Notes : Maharishi recommended to employ Shodshottari Dasa system for Ascendent being in the Hora of the Moon if the birth of the native is in the Krishna Paksha and also in converse situations i.e. birth is in Shukla Paksha and Ascendent is in the Sun Hora. There are only eight planets in this system and Rahu is out of it. The calculation of the expired period of the Shadsottari Dasa are identical to Vimshottari Dasa system. 

# Example-5:

A native has taken birth in Mrigshira Nakshatra and his birth was in the Krishna Paksha and the Ascendent was in Moon's Hora, the Shodsottari Dasa system will give satisfactory results. 

Suppose Bhayaata of Mrigshira is 20 Gh. - 30 Pal. (1230 Pals.) and Bhabhoga being 60 Gh., 10 Pal. (3610 Pals.). As per the Table-6 the Sun Dasa will be ruling at the time of birth (From Pushyami the Mrigshira Nakshatra is ahead of 25 count and if we divide this figure by 8, the remainder comes to 1 i.e. Ist planet which is Sun) Sun has period of 11 years : 

Expired Period of the Sun : $\frac{1230 \times 11}{3610} = \frac{13530}{3610} = 3.748$ yrs. 

$= 3$ years,8 months,29 days. 

Alternative method of Calculation : 

$$
\begin{array}{l} 3. 7 4 8 \text {y r s} = 3 \text {y r s} + 0. 7 4 8 \\ 0. 7 4 8 \times 1 2 = 8. 9 7 6 \text {m o n t h s} = 8 \text {m o n t h s} + 0. 9 7 6 \\ 0. 9 7 6 \times 3 0 = 2 9. 2 8 d a y s = 2 9 d a y s \\ = 3 \text {y e a r s}, 8 \text {m o n t h s}, 2 9 \text {d a y s} \\ \end{array}
$$

# Balance Dasa of the Sun :

# 4. DWADASHOTTARI DASA

ghuokangakr prajalatak y vibinnty a dahayoletarl 1   
jannmamatu paoaagn ayabvtu saxya hvi bnuotlcttata I 2v 1   
suyu: guk: viakti jao: kuojo manno ne nghakkr:   
binna guk k dhagaiiaiagaa dhchayatut saphetl: sma: | 12c 

27-28: Dwadashottari Dasa be pursued for those natives whose Ascendant falls in the Navamsa of Venus. Number of the Nakshatras from the birth Nakshatra to Revati Nakashatra be reckoned and divided by eight. The remainder will indicate the planet as the lord of Dasa by serially counting from the Sun, Jupiter, Ketu, Mercury, Rahu, Mars, Saturn and the Moon having period 6, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, 19 and 21 years respectively (An addition of 2 years in the successive Dasas). The Dasa of Venus is not in this scheme. 

Notes : In Dwadashottari Dasa system, the Dasa having been consumed upto the birth i.e., Bhayaata and Bhabhoga are reckoned on the pattern of Vimsottari Dasa. The Dasa pattern is illustrated in Table No.-7 appended below where from it may be noted that Nakshatras are arranged 

in the reverse order :

# 5. PANCHOTTARI DASA

nnaaahaaanrnnnne

EeKoTARarAee 1 

gulmna: 1 1 1 

29-31: When a native is born in the Cancer Ascendant (Lagna) and also having Cancer Dwadashamsa (1/12th Division), Panchattori Dasa be adopted. Divide the number of Nakshatras arrived at by counting from Anuradha to the birth Nakshatra by 7 digit and the remainder will indicate the planet of the ruling Dasa in the order of Sun, Mercury, Saturn, Mars, Venus, Moon and Jupiter having Dasa (years) of 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17 and 18 years respectively. O Brahmin! In this Dasa system there are seven planets, leaving aside Rahu and Ketu. 

Notes : Panchottari Dasa is applicable to only those natives who 

have cancer Ascendant and also Cancer as the Ascendant of Dwadasamsa. Like other Dasasystems narrated by the Maharishi so far, in this Dasa also the Bhayaata and Bhabhoga are arrived at on the lines of Vimshottari Dasa.

# 6. SHATABDIKA DASA

Vagatamgata 1 

1321 

रिसागाको रिसितो जेया दिल्ला दिल्लसमाडँया ｜ 

RavivahvchvNroo 3yogurshov joiyo oom: yaninlctay 113311 

Kemamadewetu Dusahaaiaa 

naka naka: karaamakwahs mamasraewa da jolatm 113 

32-34: In the event of Ascendent (Lagna) being Vargottama (बागार्यम) i.e. having the some Ascendent in Navamsa (1/9th Division), the Shatabdika Dasa system will be suitable for prognostication. Count the number of Nakshatras from Revati to the birth Nakshatra and divide it by 7. The remainder will indicate the Dasa lord in the order of the Sun Moon, Venus Mercury, Jupiter, Mars, Saturn. The period of these Dasa lords are 5, 5, 10, 10, 20, 20 and 30 years 

respectively. 

Notes : When the sign of the Ascendant happened to be the same as that of Navamsa Ascendant, it is called Vargottama Navamsa. Suppose the Ascendant is of Leo sign and the longitude of the Ascendant is between $13^{\circ}$ -20' and $16^{\circ}$ -40', the Ascendant of the Navamsa will also be of Leo sign. Any native having this kind of situation, in that event Shatabdika Dasa system may be adopted. There is an easy way to assimilate the pattern in which Vargottama Navamsa (1/9th part) will take place. The Ist Navamsa of the movable sign, middle Navamsa of the fixed sign and the last Navamsa of the dual sign will always be a Vargottama Navamsa. 

Like Dwadashottari Dasa system, here also the number of Nakshatras are reckoned from Revati to the birth Nakshatra but the counting is done in the direct order rather than in the reverse order as was the case in the Dwadashottari system. The other distinguishing feature is that here only 7 planets instead of 8 are considered and Rahu and Ketu are totally out of reckoning and are replaced by Venus. Rest of the procedure for calculating the balance period of the Dasa lord remained the same as laid down in the Vimsottari Dasa system. 


TABLE-9 SHATABDIKA DASA

# Illustration :

In Example-5, Mrigshira is taken as the birth Nakshatra which is 6th if counted from Revati (See Table-9). Since it is less than 7, the remainder remains as 6 only and hence Mars Dasa will be running at the time of birth. From the said example, the 'FACTOR' Bhayaata/Bhabhoga $(1230 + 3610)$ comes to 0.341. Hence the expired period of Mars Dasa $= 0.341 \times 20 = 6.82$ yrs. or 6 years 9 months 25 days. Subtracting from 20 yrs (total period), the balance Dasa of Mars will come to 13 yr. 2 months 5 days. 

# 7. CHATURASHITI - SAMA DASA

35-36: At the time of birth, if the 10th house is occupied by the 10th lord, Chaturashiti-Sama Dasa system will be useful. Number from Svati to the birth Nakshatra be counted and the figure is divided by 7. The remainder will indicate the Dasa order reckoned from the Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus and Saturn in that order. Each planet has an identical Dasa of 12 years. 

Notes: The procedure for ascertaining the ruling Dasa of the planet at the time of birth in Chaturashti-Sama Dasa system is the same as in the case of preceding system. The only glaring difference amongst these systems is the nomination of first Nakshatra which is the Sun and the planetary Dasa order follows there from. In all the cases, the starting Dasa is of the Sun but the number of Dasa segments are between 7 and 9. The peculiarity in this system is that all the seven Dasas are of an identical duration of 12 years each, making the cycle of 84 years and that is why it is called Chaturashti. Rest of the calculations for fixing the Dasa balance etc., as in other cases, are in line with the Vimsottari Dasa system.

37-39: In Dwisaptati-Sama Dasa system, counting is done from the Moola to the birth Nakshatra. The figure is divided by 8 and the remainder points out the planet-Dasa lord in that order starting from the Sun, the Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn and Rahu (Ketu is ignored). All the eight Dasas are of 9 years duration. Bhayaata and Bhaboga are considered in the same manner as in the Vimsottari Dasa system. This Dasa be gainfully applied in those cases where either the 7th lord is in the Ascendant or the Ascendant is posited in the 7th house. 

Notes: Here the Dasa order is in the natural arrangement of the planet. Of course, Ketu has been ignored. Based on the principle enunciated in Shloka 37-39, the Dwisaptati Sama Dasa Table is drawn as under for the convenience of the readers.

# 9. SHASTI HAYANI DASA

Mabdaakcoi  lgaanranaaiaeaahnnae aee 

dahaaat  aay  chutuak  c  ay  cheit yun: yun: | |yo | 

Gurovokuoyutanai 1 

L: 11 

40-41: If the sun is located in the Ascendant Shasti Hayani Dasa will be most suitable for such natives. Commencing from Aswini Nakshatra, a cluster of 3 and 4 Nakshatras, taken alternatively would represent the Dasa lord in the order of Jupiter, the Sun, Mars, the Moon, Mercury, Venus, Saturn and Rahu. First three Dasas of the planets would each be of 10 years duration and the last five Dasas would have 6 years each. 

Notes: Shasti Hayani Dasa system works on the pattern of Astottari Dasa because here also the Nakshatras are considered in a group formation and not in seriatim as in the case of the other Dasa systems discussed so far. Besides, like Astottari Dasa, Abhijita Nakshatra is also being considered and therefore, instead of 27 there are 28 Nakshatras in the reckoning. However, the Dasa cycle of Shastihayani is the half of Vimsottari Dasa i.e. 60 years. (Three Dasas are of 10 years and the remaining 5 ones are of 6 years duration). 

If we take cue from example-5, the birth is in the Mrigshira Nakshatra which is 5th from Aswini. Therefore, the birth is in the Dasa of the Sun of 10 years period. There are 4 Nakshatras in this group, each will be having a span of 2 1/2 years. Further, the period of Rohini Nakshatra has already been exhausted. The Bhayaata/Bhabhoga factor is $1230 + 3610 = 0.341$ . 

Expired period : $0.341 \times 2.5 \, \text{yr.s.} = 0.8525 = 0 \, \text{yr.}$ 

of Mrigshira : $0.8525 \times 12 = 10.23 = 10$ months 

$0.23 \times 30 = 6.9$ days 

say $= 7$ days

42-43: In the event of the birth being in the day time and also the Ascendant being in the Sun's Hora or in the night birth and Ascendant happened to be in the Hora of the Moon, Shat-Trimsha-Sama Dasa be adopted. Count the number of Nakshatras from Shravana to the birth Nakshatra and have it divided by digit 8. The remainder number would indicate the Dasa lord in seriatim commencing from the Moon, the Sun, Jupiter, Mars, Mercury, Saturn, Venus and Rahu. The period of the Dasa would be in the natural 

order i.e. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 years respectively. 

Notes: The total span cycle is of 36 years in this system. Ketu is ignored. The balance Dasa etc. is calculated based on Bhayaata and Bhabhoga of the birth Nakshatra on the lines followed in the Vimsottari Dasa system :

# 11. KALA DASA

Sukumyathayaswamy: Puivy Prarvatniyaee 

44-49: The periods of 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) astride the setting of the semi-disc of the sun and like wise, 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) proceeding and 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) succeeding the Sun rise i.e. a span of 10 Ghatis (4 hrs.) each is termed as the twilight period (Sandhya) of evening and morning respectively. As such both these Sandhyas are constituted of 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) the 20 Ghatis period (4 hrs.) of the day (i.e. the gap between morning and evening Sandhyas) is called POORNA (पूपाद) and similarly, 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) of the night is named as MUGDHA (मुध्या). 10 Ghatis Sandhya of the sunrise and 10 Ghatis Sandhya of the sun set are called KHANDA (सनिका) and SUDHA (सनिका) respectively. 

If the birth occurs in Poorna or Mugdha, multiply the period (Ghatis) consumed at birth in the said portion by 2 and divide it by 15. The product be expressed in years, months etc. and reckoned as a constant factor. Multiply this factor by 1 to 9 in natural order, the Kala Dasa of the corresponding planet starting from Sun to Rahu, again in normal planetary order is achieved. In case the birth is in the span of Khanda or Sudha, the expired period upto the birth be multiplied by 4 and then divided by 15. This product also be used as a constant factor in calculating the Kala Dasa of the planets in the same manner. 

Notes : The technical terms used in these Shalokas are explained further for the proper comprehension of the readers. 

(i) Khanda (राग्या) : Day and night consist of 60 Ghatis (24 hrs). The period of 10 Ghatis i.e. 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) prior and 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) later to the Sun rise is called morning twilight (Sandhya). The Sandhya of the morning is called Khanda. 

(ii) Sudha (Ghatis): In the identical fashion 10 Ghatis of the evening i.e. 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) astride sun set time constitute the evening twilight (Sandhya). 

(iii) Poorna (v): The period of 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) between both the Sandhyas i.e. Khanda to Sudha is known by the name Poorna. 

(iv) Mugdha $(\text{贡} \text{贡})$ : Similarly, the 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) portion from Sudha to Khanda is termed as Mugdha. 

The readers must have noticed from the text that the only intricacy in calculating the Kala Dasa is to first identify the segment (amongst the four above segments) in which the birth has taken place and then to figure out as to how much time has elapsed from the beginning of the said segment and the birth time. 

The rest of the calculations and pin-pointing the ruling Dasa at the time of the birth is a simple affair. However, the principles set out for this purpose are reiterated as under :- 

(i) If a native is born in the period between Poorna or Mugdha, it is first of all necessary to know as to how much time has been passed (in terms of Ghatis or hrs./minutes) in Poorna or Mugdha upto the birth. The said time be multiplied by 2 and then divided by 15. The resultant value shall be multiplied by the numbers assigned to the planet (Sun-1; Moon-2; Mars-3; Mercury-4; Jupiter-5, Venus-6; Saturn-7; Rahu-8 and Ketu-9) to arrive at the Dasa of the respective planet. 

(ii) The procedure for calculating Dasas of the planet if the birth is in the duration of Khanda or Sudha, except the elapsed period upto birth is multiplied by 4 instead of 2 and then divided by 15, rest of the steps remain unchanged. 

(iii) It may also be understood that if the birth is on Sunday, the first Dasa will that be of the Sun and if Monday, the Dasa cycle will start from the Moon and so on. Since Dasa of Rahu and Ketu are also in the scheme, their Dasas will figure at 8th and 9th portion. 

# Example - 6 :

Suppose the birth is at 2 Gh. 25 Pal. after the sunrise on Monday. Since it is less then 5 Gh., the birth is reckoned in 'Khanda' (Sandhya). In order to find out the time elapsed from the commencement of Khanda, 5 Gh. (period prior to the sunrise) be added which comes to 7 Gh., 25 Pal.- period and since has been passed in Khanda segment. 

# Calculations :

$$
\begin{array}{l} 7 \mathrm {G h}. - 2 5 \mathrm {P a l} = 7. 4 1 6 \mathrm {G h}. \times 4 = 2 9. 6 6 [ 2 5 + 1 0 0 / 6 6 ] \\ 2 9. 6 6 + 1 5 = 1. 9 7 7 = 1 \mathrm {y r .} + 0. 9 7 7 \\ 0. 9 7 7 \times 1 2 = 1 1. 7 2 4 = 1 1 \text {m o n t h s} + 0. 7 2 4 \\ 0. 7 2 4 \times 3 0 = 2 1. 7 2 = 2 2 \text {d a y s} (\text {D a y}) \\ = 1 \text {y r}, 1 1 \text {m o n t h s}, 2 2 \text {d a y s} \\ = \text {S u n D a s a} = \quad A \times 1 = 1 \text {y r} 1 1 \text {m o n t h s} 2 2 \text {d a y s} \\ = \text {M o o n D a s a} = \quad A \times 2 \quad = 3 \text {y r s .} 1 1 \text {m o n t h s} 1 4 \text {d a y s} \\ = \text {M a r s D a s a} = \quad A \times 3 = 5 \text {y r s .} 1 1 \text {m o n t h s} 6 \text {d a y s} \\ \end{array}
$$

Likewise, the Dasas of the other planets could be worked out. Since the birth being on Monday, the first Dasa would be of the Moon. 

The method of calculating Kala Dasa enunciated in the similar books published by M/s. Khemchand Shrikrishan Das Publications (Bombay) and Thakur Prashad & Sons Booksellers (Varanasi) is at variance. According to them, if the native is born in Poorna segment, the elapsed period in Poorna [time from sunrise to birth-5 Gh.] shall be multiplied by 6 instead of 2. Similar treatment be given in the event of the birth being in part of Mugdha. Similarly, for Khanda and Sudha segments the elapsed period in their respective portions is to be multiplied by 12 rather by 4. 

Further, the product in respect of all the above cases need to be divided by 45 (against 15). The resulting figure should be taken as a constant factor (Dhruanka). The procedure for calculating individual planetary Dasa remained the same i.e. the factor is to be multiplied by the serial number 1,2,3....8, 9 to ascertain the Dasas order of Sun, Moon, Mars, Rahu, Ketu respectively as illustrated in example-6. 

Here the common feature is that the multipliers are three times in both the cases and so is the division. Since multiplier and divisor are enhanced by the same denomination, the net result will remain the same. However, the reason for such divergent opinions could not possibly be figured out through any classical works known to this humble author. 

# 12. CHAKRA DASA

Rarra 1 

sannnnaay nntanrnnnne aee 

dshata  shaigra shaia nanae kekkuy dshaaamititi: 

KmHnAeKsRtAnanov 1114911 

50-51: In the event of birth taking place in the night, the Dasa will originate from the sign of the Ascendant (Lagna Rasi). If the birth happened to be in the day, the Dasa will start from the sign which tenants the lord of the Ascendant (Lagna Lord). When the birth occurs in the period of twilight (Sandhya), the sign of the second house will initiate the Dasa. Each Dasa of the sign is of 10 years duration. Since the pattern of these Dasas is compatible with the twelve zodiacal signs, it is appropriately called as Chakra Dasa system. 

Notes: The Chakra Dasa could be maximum of three kinds. First, when the native is born in the night, the Dasa will commence from the sign of the Ascendant followed by the subsequent 11 signs in their normal order. Suppose, at the time of birth Capricorn sign is rising, in that event, the Dasa cycle will begin with the Capricorn sign followed by Aquarius, Pisces, Aries and so on till Sagittarius sign. It is evident from the Shloka 51 that all these Dasas would be of 10 years duration. 

The second situation is when the native's birth is in the day time and the sign that occupies the lord of the Ascendant will initiate the Dasa cycle. Again if the Capricorn is the sign rising at the time of birth and Saturn is posited in the sign of Virgo, the Dasa cycle will begin from Virgo and succeeded by Libra, and so on. 

The third eventuality covered in this system is the occurrence of birth in the duration of twilight. In that case, the sign of the second house would give the lead. Taking cue from the previous two situations when Capricorn being the rising sign, the Dasa will commence from Aquarius followed by Pisces, Aries in that order. 

# 13. KALACHAKRA DASA

Aahayadhye 8a2ckr Nelva Kaalchkcdhag 8uve   
paehelty kaiyltata Puvr saadre yar pinnanakinana   
Ls3y: sare 8uuguyuoy 5evn aee 6i jnnnndn   
8uhaasmaaegm nunuaaagaa ygha jannnnt paoqddta: 14.311 

52-53: Maharishi Parasara asserted - O Vipra! Prostrating before Lord Shiva, I now perorate Kalachakra Dasa, as it was narrated to Goddess Parvati by Lord Shiva in the ancient time, for the savants to enable them to give righteous interpretations to the populace. 

Notes: Various kinds of Nakshatras based Dasa systems have been advocated by the Sage for the purpose of prognosticating future events. However, four of them are having great bearing in the field of predictive astrology. They are i) Vimshottari Dasa, ii) Astottari Dasa, iii) Yogani Dasa and iv) Kalachakra Dasa. All these four systems are fundamentally reckoned from the longitude of the Moon at the time of birth as a base. As a matter of fact, the position of a particular Nakshatra in the longitude of the Moon at birth is called Bhayaata. On the basis of Bhayaata, the Dasas are calculated. With the change of the lord of Nakshatras, in different Dasa cycles, it is not possible to have any kind of inter-relation amongst them. 

Kalachakra (wheel of time) Dasa is a very important Dasa system, despite the fact that it appears slightly difficult because of non-universality of the rules and difference in their interpretations. Nevertheless, it is still popular on account of its employing wider spectrum of the Nakshatras in the system. 

Kalachakra Dasa commences within each quarter (Pada) of Moon's Nakshatra at birth. 27 Nakshatras form 108 quarters (1 Nakshatra has 4 quarters) one quarter of the Nakshatra represents 1/9th part (Navamsa) of a sign (Rashi). Therefore, Kalachakra Dasa is a Navamsa - Rashi Dasa. The Dasa system has striking similarity with the Navamsa Dasa of the Jamini Sutras (aphorisms). 

Kalachakra Dasa system can be effectively applied in predicting the Maraka (time of death) and Arista (misfortune) of the native. Actually, through this Dasa system, astounding predictions have been made. 

Kalachakra Dasa system is capable of delineating correct events relating to the material comforts, happiness, success in ventures etc. Therefore, Kalachakra Dasa can be studied and applied with great advantage.

54-55: Outline two charts of twelve compartments each by drawing vertical and horizontal lines for Savya (clockwise) and Apsavya (anti-clockwise). Affix sign Aries in the second compartment of each chart. This is called Dwadash-Rashiatmaka Kalachakra.

56-58: A group of first three Nakshatras viz., Aswini, Bharani and Kritika be placed in Savya Chakra (Chart) and the next group of these Nakshatras starting from Rohini in the Apsavya Chakra (Charts). Similarly, the next three nakshatras will go to the Savya Chakra followed by another three Nakshatras in the Apsavya Chakra, and so on. With this pattern of distribution of the Nakshatras, the Savya Chakra will house 15 and the Apsavya Chakra 12 Nakshatras. The four quarters (Padas) of Aswini, Punarvasu, Hasta, Moola, Poorva Bhadra; Kritika, Ashelsha Swati, Uttara Bhadra and Revati be identified in the same fashion as of 4 quarters (Padas) of Aswini Nakshatras.

60: In the first quarter of the Aswini Nakshatra Aries represents Deha and Sagittarius Jeeva. The lords of each of signs from Aries to Sagittarius in seriatim would become lords of the nine Dasas.

61: In the second quarter of Aswini Capricorn is Deha and Gemini signifies Jeeva. The lords of Capricorn to Gemini would act as Dasa lords in the natural order.

63-64: In the fourth quarter of the Nakshatra commencing from Aswini, Cancer and Pisces represent Deha and Jeeva respectively. And O, Brahmin! The lords of signs from Cancer to Pisces shall be the Dasa lords.

67: Aquarius and Virgo signs in the 2nd quarter (Pada) of Bharani Nakshatra respectively represent Deha and Jeeva and the lords of Aquarius to Virgo signs are the lords of the Dasas in their natural order. 

$$

68: In the third quarter of Bharani, Libra represent, Deha and Virgo Jeeva. And the signs : Libra, Scarpio, Saggitamn. Capricorn, Aquarious, Pisces, Scorpio, Libra and Virgo, have their lords respectively as Dasa lords.

69-70: Bharani's 4th quarter (Pada) goes to Cancer as Deha and Sagittarius as Jeeva and the lords of signs 4, 5, 3, 2, 1, 12, 11, 10, 9 (anti clockwise) would act as the lords of the Dasas. 

O Brahmin! I have since described Savya Chakra exhaustively. Now I explain the Apsavya Chakra. Sketch out the 

chart of 12 compartments as was done earlier (see Table 14-B).

71-72: In the second compartment of the chart, write the signs from Scorpio in anti clockwise fashion i.e. Scorpio, Libra, Virgo and so on upto. O Brahmin! The great Savants have called the four Nakshatras, Rohini, Magha, Vishakha and Shravana as 'Apsavya' The representation of Deha and Jeeva in these four Nakshatras are delineated in the same manner as was made out in case of Rohini Nakshatra.
