28-29: The 4th place (रैयान) from the sign in which Mercury is posited signifies family, friends, maternal uncle etc. The sign bearing more number of Rekhas in the Ashtakavarga of Mercury. The transit of Mercury over that sign will confer happiness to them (family, maternal uncle, frierds etc.). 

After carrying out the Shodhanas of the Ashtakavarga of Mercury, the Nakshatras so obtained are when transited by Saturn, it will indicate distress to the family, friends, maternal uncle etc. In order words, the Shodya Pinda obtained after applying Trikona and Ekadhipatya Shodhanas in the Ashtakavarga of Mercury be multiplied by Rekhas of sign harbouring Mercury and the product be divided by 27. The Nakshatra denoted by the number of the remainder and the trinal Nakshatras therefrom (10th and 19th) are when transit by Saturn will cause distress to the family etc. In the same manner, the product be divided by 12 (Nos. of signs) and the sign indicated by the remainder and the 5th and the 9th signs therefrom are when transited by Saturn, brings about distress to the family, friends, maternal uncle etc. 

Notes : The status of the Ashtakavarga of Mercury in the Model horoscope (Example-93) showing the reduction is as follows: 

<table><tr><td>Planets</td><td>Sun Mer</td><td>Rah Mer</td><td>Rah Mer</td><td>Rah Mer</td><td>Rah Mer</td><td>Rah Mer</td><td>Ket</td><td>Ket</td><td>Ket</td><td>Mar Mon Jup</td><td>Ven Sat</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Signs</td><td>5</td><td>6</td><td>7</td><td>8</td><td>9</td><td>10</td><td>11</td><td>12</td><td>1</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td>Number of Rekhas</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>7</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>7</td><td>5</td><td>3</td><td>7</td></tr><tr><td>Trikona Shodhana</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>3</td><td>1</td><td>0</td><td>4</td><td>1</td><td>0</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Ekadhipatya Shodhana</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>1</td><td>1</td><td>0</td><td>4</td><td>1</td><td>0</td><td>3</td></tr></table>

The Rashi Pinda and Graha Pinda are to be computed in the manner as have been carried out in the case of Moons' Ashtakavarga. Accordingly, the Rashi Pinda and Graha Pinda are arrived at as 66 and 36 respectively with the (total) Shodya Pinda of 102 units. Mercury is posited in the sign of Leo and the 4th sign therefrom is Scorpio which contains 4 Rekhas. The product $(102 \times 4 = 408)$ when divided by 27 gives remainder as 3 and when divided by 12 will give zero as remainder. Therefore, the 3rd Nakshatra being Kritika and trinal Nakshatra therefrom (10th and 19th) would be Uttar Phalguni and Uttar Ashada and similarly, the 'Zero' sign equals to the 12th sign i.e. Pisces. The 5th and 9th signs therefrom are Cancer and Scorpio respectively. 

Therefore, the transit of Saturn over Nakshatras Kritika, Uttar Phalguni and Uttar Ashada and also signs Pisces, Cancer and Scorpio will cause distress and sorrows to the family, friends, maternal uncles etc.

30-33: From the 5th place to Jupiter, knowledge, religion, and son (children) are to be considered. If the sign at the 5th places containing more Rekhas, it will deliver happiness of the children. The number of Rekhas in the sign at the 5th place denotes that many number of children provided this sign being other than the sign of debilitation viz. Capricorn. Besides, the number of children may also be equal to the number of Rekhas in the Navamsa of the 5th house (from the Ascendant). 

In the Ashtakavarga of Jupiter, the number of Rekhas in the sign, 5th from the Jupiter be multiplied by the (total) Shodya Pinda of the Jupiter's Ashtakavarga. The product be divided by 27 and the transit of Saturn over the Nakshatra denoted by the remainder and the trinal Nakshatras (10th and 19th) therefrom and also the sign indicated by the remainder obtained on dividing the product by 12 and the trinal (5th and 9th) signs therefrom, will bring distress to the children and will also affect the knowledge and religion. 

Notes : The status of the Ashtakavarga of Jupiter in the Model horoscope showing the reductions is illustrated as follows :

The Rashi and Graha Pindas are computed in the manner, it is worked out in case of Moon's Ashtakavarga the position is as under: 

<table><tr><td>Rashi Pinda</td><td>=</td><td>48</td></tr><tr><td>Graha Pinda</td><td>=</td><td>46</td></tr><tr><td>Shodya Pinda</td><td>=</td><td>94</td></tr></table>

Jupiter is posited in the sign of Gemini and therefore, the 5th sign therefrom would be Libra which contains 3 Rekhas. The product $(94 \times 3 = 282)$ when divided by 27, leaves a remainder of 12. The 12th Nakshatra from Aswini Nakshatra will be that of Uttar Phalguni and the trinal Nakshatras (10th and 19th) therefrom will be Uttara Ashada and Kritika. Therefore, as and well Saturn transits over these three Nakshatras, it will cause distress to the children, the native will develop imprudence and irreligious propensities. 

Now, when we divide the product (282) by 12, we get a remainder of 6 which is denoted by sign Virgo and the trinal signs therefrom are Taurus and Capricom. Whenever Saturn will transit through these signs, there will be degradation in prudence, and religious pursuits and distress to the children.

34-36: The signs which contain more number of Rekhas in the Ashtakavarga of Venus and as and when Venus passes over these signs, the results like acquisition of wealth, woman (marriage) and property will be indicative towards the directions denoted by these signs. The possibilities of the gain of woman (marriage) will be in the direction or country (रेशा -places) indicated by the 7th house from Venus or trinal signs therefrom. 

The happiness or sorrows related to woman (wife) be considered from the Rekhas contained in the 7th house (sign) from the Venus duly multiplied by (total) Shodya Pinda etc.

The Rashi and Graha Pinda are calculated on the lines of Moon's Ashtakavarga and the final position so emerged is as follows: 

$$
\begin{array}{l l l} \text {R a s h i P i n d a} & = & 7 7 \\ \text {G r a h a P i n d a} & = & 2 0 \\ \text {S h o d y a P i n d a} & = & 9 7 \end{array}
$$

Venus is posited in the sign Cancer and the 7th sign therefrom is Capricorn which has 3 Rekhas and the product after multiplying these Rekhas with the total Shodya Pinda (3x97) comes to 291. The remainder after dividing this product by 27 is obtained as 21. The 21st Nakshatras from Aswini Nakshatra is Uttara Ashada and the trinal Nakshatras (10th and 19th) thereferom are Kritika and Uttar Phalguni. As and when Venus passes through these Nakshatras, the wife will get distressed. 

Now, divide the product (291) by 12 and we get remainder as 3. The 3rd sign from Aries is Gemini and the trinal signs therefrom is Libra and Aquarius. The transit of Venus over these sign will bring about distress to the wife of the native. 

# Results of the Ashtakavarga of Saturn :

37-40: The 8th place from the Saturn in a nativity is reckoned as Mrityu Sthana (place of death) and the same place also denotes the longevity and the longevity be considered from that position. Total up the number of Rekhas from the Ascendant to Saturn. The disease and enmity will be indicative in the year equal to the total number of the Rekhas. Also total up the Rekhas from Saturn to the Ascendant in the Ashtakavarga of Saturn and the danger of disease in the year equivalent to the total Rekhas be predicted. The sum total of both these number of Rekhas will denote the year in which death or death like distress will be indicative. If the Dasacaising Arishta ( अरिक्ति) also falls at the same period, it will inflict the death to the native. 

Notes : The status of the Ashtakavarga of Saturn in the Model horoscope (Example-93) showing the reductions etc., istabulated hereafter. 

<table><tr><td>Planets</td><td>Ven Sat</td><td>Sun Mer</td><td>Rah</td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td>Ket</td><td></td><td>Ascd</td><td>Mar Mon Jup</td></tr><tr><td>Signs</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>6</td><td>7</td><td>8</td><td>9</td><td>10</td><td>11</td><td>12</td><td>1</td><td>2</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Number of Rekhas</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>2</td><td>5</td><td>2</td><td>2</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>5</td><td>6</td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td>Trikona Shodhana</td><td>0</td><td>1</td><td>1</td><td>0</td><td>3</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>1</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>2</td></tr><tr><td>Ekadhipatya Shodhana</td><td>0</td><td>1</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>1</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>2</td></tr></table>

The Ascendant of the Model horoscope is Aries and Saturn is posited in the 4th house in sign Cancer. The total Rekhas from Aries to Cancer are $5 + 6 + 4 + 2 = 17$ . Therefore, in the 17th year, the native will face ailments (disease) and also enmity or hostile behaviour of other persons. Saturn is in the Cancer sign and the total number of Rekhas from Cancerto the Ascendant are 29 $(2 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 5 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 3 + 5)$ and total of both this sums $(17 + 29)$ comes to 46. Therefore, one should predict the death like distress will cause to the native in the 46th years of his age. If the Dasa of Markesha planet is in operation during the same year, it will inflict death to the native. 

The Moon's degress of the nativity is $1^{\circ} - 57'$ - $9''$ and therefore ... balance of Dasa in Vimshottari system will be of Mars (2 years, 5 months and 20 days). The 46th years of the native will fall in the year 2023 and at that time the Mahadasa of Saturn and Antardasa of Venus will be in operation - Venus is the lord of the 2nd and the 7th house - and hence, it will act as Markesha. However, Venus is not posited in the Maraka Sthana and therefore, it will only cause death like distress, miseries etc., and may not inflict death to the native. 

# Contemplation of death :

Vipinnd Sankhyaayu Guganvatehur Shanaeretamgo: Kaftei:   
Sapnabivirgharatiuhcchgshuyu LuyuMgh gatse shanni I 189   
Lutunrikcoagakgei vahiy jalakkuy Mufit yedel   
Aekchckravraayia va la tnurikcoogosy i te du yadetul I 182 

41-42: The number of Rekhas in the 8th place from Saturn (in the Ashtakavarga of Saturn) be multiplied by the total Shodhya Pinda and the product be divided by 27 and the Nakshatra equivalent to the remainder on counting from Aswini and its trial Nakshatras are when transited by Saturn, the death of the native is indicative. Besides, the product be divided by 12 and the sign denoted by the remainder and trinal signs therefrom are when passed over by Saturn, the native will encounter death like distress. 

Notes : The Rashi and Graha Pinda be computed in the manner illustrated in the Ashtakavarga of the Moon. The following figures are obtained : 

Rashi Pinda 99 

Graha Pinda 56 

Shodya Pinda 155 

There are two Rekhas in the 8th house from Saturn is posited (Saturn is posited in the Cancer sign) which falls in the sign of Aquarius in the nativity. Therefore, multiply the Shodya Pinda by 2 : 

$$
1 5 5 \times 2 = 3 1 0
$$

By dividing the product by 27, we get a remainder of 13. The 13th Nakshatra on counting from Aswini Nakshatra will that be of Hasta and the trinal Nakshatras therefrom (10th and 19th) will be denoted by Shravana and Rohini. As and when, Saturn passes through these three Nakshatras, there will be a possibility of the death of the native. 

In the same way, the product (310) be divided by 12, we get a remainder of 10. The 10th sign from Aries will be Capricorn and its trinal signs (5th and 9th) will be Taurus and Virgo. The transit of Saturn through these three signs will cause fear of death to the native. 

g 

T 1 

yT 1 

Jaiatakayd  duv  yoy tataimnna kalot gumn kafam | r 

43-44: All those places which do not secure any Rekha in the Ashtakavarga of Saturn and as and when Saturn will pass through these places, it will not do good to the native. The transit of Saturn over the signs having large number of Rekhas will be propitious for the native. This should be predicted with all certainty.

56-58½: Jupiter, the Ascendant, Mars, the Sun and Mercury in the 1st and the 4th houses; Jupiter, the Ascendant, Mars, the Sun, Mercury; the Moon and Venus in the 2nd house; Saturn, Jupiter and the Sun in the 3rd house; only Saturn in the 12th house; Venus, the Moon, the Ascendant, Mercury and Saturn in the 5th house; Venus, the Ascendant, Mercury and Saturn in the 6th house; the Ascendant, Mars, Jupiter, the Sun and the Moon in the 7th house; Jupiter, the Sun and Mars in the 8th house; Venus, the Sun, the Ascendant, the Moon and Mercury in the 9th house; all the planets excepting Saturn in the 11th house; Jupiter, Mercury, Mars, the Sun, Venus and the Ascendant in the 10th house are the Rekhaprada planets. 


Rekhaprada Chakra of Ashtakavarga of Jupiter :

8 years of Ayu. In this manner, study the number of the Rekhas in all the signs of the Ashtakavargas of all the planets and total up the Ayu contributed by them. Half the sum will denote Ashtakavarga's Ayu (longevity). 

Notes: Under the Ashtakavarga's method of fixing the longevity, first of all, the period as described in the foregoing Shlokas, is fixed on the basis of the possession of the number of Rekhas by each sign in the Ashtakavarga of each planet and the total value of the period so obtained is divided by half. The resultant value is called Ashtakavarga's Ayu. In this manner, one can determine the longevity of the native through the Ashtakavarga system. The periods as disclosed in the preceding Shlokas on the basis of a sign securing the number of Rekhas have been tabulated as illustrated below : 


TABLE - 37

Let us apply these basis of the working out the longevity on the Modle Example-89 which is being considered for the purpose of illustrating the various principles expounded by the Sage in the Ashtakavarga system. We would like to take the readers back to the tabulation of Ashtakavargas of various planets in Chapter-69 and would lift therefrom, the units of Rekhas secured by each sign in the Ashtakavarga of different planets. The Ashtakavarga Rekhaprada Chakra of the Sun is being taken up and the number of days etc. contributed by the each sign in this Ashtakavarga are drawn in the subsequent table showing the share of this planet in the total longevity of the native.

ASHTAKAVARGA'S AYU CHAKRA :

The contribution of Ascendant's Ashtakavarga is - 10 years, 1 month, 9 days, 12 hrs. 

In order to ascertain longevity of the native, as described earlier, the Ashtakavarga's Ayus of all the planets including the Ascendant are to be totaled up and half the value of this sum will denote the Ayu (Ilife span) of the native. 

The sum total of the value contributed by all the planets along with the Ascendant is tabulated as under : 

<table><tr><td>Planets</td><td>Years</td><td>Months</td><td>Days</td><td>Hours</td></tr><tr><td>Sun</td><td>12</td><td>-</td><td>10</td><td>12</td></tr><tr><td>Moon</td><td>10</td><td>1</td><td>17</td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td>Mars</td><td>6</td><td>-</td><td>21</td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td>Mercury</td><td>20</td><td>-</td><td>25</td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td>Jupiter</td><td>20</td><td>1</td><td>8</td><td>12</td></tr><tr><td>Venus</td><td>16</td><td>1</td><td>9</td><td>12</td></tr><tr><td>Saturn</td><td>8</td><td>-</td><td>14</td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td>Ascendant</td><td>10</td><td>1</td><td>9</td><td>12</td></tr><tr><td>Total</td><td>102</td><td>7</td><td>25</td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td>Half the Value</td><td>51</td><td>3</td><td>27</td><td>12</td></tr></table>

The Ashstakavarga's Ayu of the native comes to - 51 years, 3 months, 27 days and 12 hrs. 

# Aey SAmuudayayatekvagaiyay: | 9

# Chapter-74

# SAMUDAYA (COLLECTIVE)

# ASHTAKAVARGA

Hdavdhya r  lalok 

Sahvataeefekfklanayy 5aoyoooy pritimn yosot | 1911 

Sahmuyahayamaiahbaannoeaymaaee bavf: 1 

राल्ति: कृत्तानिन राल्तानों वर्षेयरानिन वर्षेयरानी ！||2 || 

1-2: Maharishi Parasara said - O, Brahmin! Draw out the twelve compartments (रेमिक्त) of the birth diagram (chakra) and fix all the houses including the Ascendant. Place sum total of the Rekhas of Ashtakavarga of each sign in the Chakra. This is called Samudaya (Collective) Ashtakavarga. Through this, the results for the native are delineated. 

Notes: The underlying idea of Maharishi is that the manner the birth chart is drawn, in the similar fashion this Chakra is also outlined and the sign of the Ascendant is placed in the first house and the rest of the houses are filled in with the signs in seriatim exactly on the pattern it is done in the birth chart. Thereafter, the 

total number of Rekhas obtained by the each sign through the Ashtakavarga of various planets be placed in their respective sign. In case of our Model Example-93, the sign of the Ascendant is Aries and the same should be fixed in the first house and accordingly a chakra of 12 houses is drawn. 

Count the contribution in the Aries sign, by all the planets, right from the Sun to Satum and Ascendant. These figures (Rekhas) obtained in the Model Example is 6,4,5,7,7,7,5 and 4 respectively and the sum total of which comes to 45. Similarly, the total number of Rekhas secured by the other signs shall also be figured out. The easy way of totalling up the Rekhas is to draw out a table of twelve columns for the twelve signs and eight horizontal lines for seven planets and Ascendant. Thereafter, place the number of Rekhas secured by the each sign from the each planet below them. The bottom total of the table will indicate Samudaya Ashtakavarga of each sign. Let us draw the table for the Model Example: 

<table><tr><td>Signs Planets</td><td>1</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>6</td><td>7</td><td>8</td><td>9</td><td>10</td><td>11</td><td>12</td></tr><tr><td>Sun</td><td>6</td><td>3</td><td>5</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>5</td><td>5</td><td>5</td></tr><tr><td>Moon</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>5</td><td>1</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>6</td></tr><tr><td>Mars</td><td>5</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>2</td><td>1</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>2</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>3</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Mercury</td><td>7</td><td>5</td><td>3</td><td>7</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>7</td><td>4</td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td>Jupiter</td><td>7</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>6</td><td>3</td><td>4</td><td>7</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td>Venus</td><td>7</td><td>5</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>6</td><td>4</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>6</td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td>Saturn</td><td>5</td><td>6</td><td>4</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>2</td><td>5</td><td>2</td><td>2</td><td>2</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Ascendant</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>4</td><td>6</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>3</td><td>5</td></tr><tr><td>Total</td><td>45</td><td>36</td><td>36</td><td>28</td><td>26</td><td>30</td><td>28</td><td>33</td><td>27</td><td>33</td><td>30</td><td>34</td></tr></table>

From the foregoing tabulation of total Rekhas obtained by each sign through the planets and Ascendant, Samudaya Chakra as described in the Shlokas could be drawn for the Model Example, like a birth chart. 

# SAMUDAYA CHAKRA

According to some other renderings on the subject, like the one expounded by savant Vaidyanathan, the totalling of the Rekhas secured by the each sign is not carried out. 

# Results of the Rekhas of the Samudaya Ashtakavarga:

JiHaaiaikpKefalra 8y Raaayasot yMprada: I

3-5: In the Samudaya Chakra, the sign which has secured more than 30 Rekhas will deliver medium results and in the same way, the signs holding less than 25 Rekhas, their results will be distressful. The planning of propitious work should be done in the signs capable of delivering auspicious results. O superior Brahmin! Auspicious work should be relegated during the distress giving signs. The planet posited in the superior signs is considered auspicious and in other signs as inauspicious. 

# Results of the houses compatible with the number of the Rekhas :

TnVahide-ayyepyehne 2e e 1 

Jabiokov shoopn shy hne hain binn 

M 5 

6: The results of the houses should be studied in consistence with the density of the Rekhas in each house. If the number of Rekhas are more, the results would be extremely good and should the Rekhas are less in number, it will deliver bad results. If the number of Rekhas are neither less nor more, the results of the same magnitude be predicted. 

Notes: From the erected birth chart of Samudaya Ashtakavarga which sign is holding more number of Rekhas, the native should undertake auspicious ventures during those signs because results of such activities will be of beneficial nature. 

In the Samudaya chart, the Ascendant of Aries sign is having 40 Rekhas and as and when the planets pass over the Aries sign in transit such planets in accordance to their Karakatwas will confer beneficial results for the houses they own. The planet or the house lord while transiting the Aries sign and whenever Aries sign rises in the east, the work related to the planet or the house lord will gain special benefit. Samudaya Ashtakvarga holds key days or time that would be most auspicious for the native. 

Many Astrologers follow some set pattern of predicting the results, such as, if any sign is having 40 Rekhas, the 1st, 13th, 25th, 37th, 49th, 61st, 73rd years of the native will prove fortunate for the native. Alternatively, suppose any sign is securing less number of Rekhas then the 8th, 20th, 22nd, 32nd, 44th, 56th and 68th years of the native will bring physical distress and mental anguish to the native. In this way, whichever house holds high Rekhas, in that year and the years followed at a gap of 12 years therefrom, will deliver benefic results. Similarly, the house which contains less number of Rekhas, in that year and in the subsequent years with the spacing of 12 years will be the distressful years for the native. 

# Spectral Results of Samudaya Ashtakavarga :

Makayalatul kaiyaihikke laamoi laaamai tkiyagangatotohay: | 10 |   
Lagnai kaiyaihikke ayeluy yogabanravahanr hui:   
Biparitton dhariiuy mavvleoy n sanyay: |C| 

7-8: If the 11th house of the Samudaya Ashtakvarga is having more Rekhas than the Rekhas of the 10th house and if the Rekhas of the 12th house are less than the Rekhas of the 11th house, and the Ascendant has more Rekhas, that native will be fortunate (he will enjoy comforts of all kinds) and wealthy. In the reverse situation (that is 12th house holds more Rekhas than the 11th house and the Ascendant also possesses less Rekhas), the native will definitely labour under poverty. 

# Trisectional results of the twelve houses :

Bhagabha: |   
Pahayatu Pahayatamahaegd  kghcokr  vdeetl|19   
Saojmyeovknt guym  yayawaninmasyirshyaphal t yaya!   
Kamadaa balaiaaayaaayaae u  o0 

9-10: Like the results of the Dasas, the results of the houses should also be predicted from the three sections (segments). The segment (Khanda) which is having the malefic planets, that part is considered distressful. The segment (Khanda) which holds benefic planets that part will deliver auspicious results and the segment possessing mixed planets (both benefic and malefic) will deliver mixed results. The result from the Ascendant to the 4th house will be of the childhood. From the 5th to the 8th houses, the result of the adulthood and houses between 9th and the 12th will produce the results of the old age. 

Notes : According to these Shlokas, the 12 houses of the nativity have been divided into three broad segments. The first part of the birth chart is represented by the house starting from the Ascendant up to the 4th house. The second part is consisted of the houses 

between 5th and the 8th houses. The houses from the 9th to the 12th form the third part of the horoscope. Correspondingly, the first segment will denote the early part of the life and the rest of the two parts of the birth chart, signify the adulthood and old age of the life span. The effects of the planets in these three distinct segments are connected with the disposition of benefic and malefic planets. The early life of the native will have distresses and destitutes, should this part the chart possesses malefic planets. Similarly, any part of which containts auspicious planets, the corresponding life span will be bestowed happiness and prosperity. Similarly, the placement of mixture of the benefic and malefic planets in any segment of the birth chart, there will be a mixed result in that part of the life of the native. In nut shell, it can be said that the life of a particular segment will be quite happy and comfortable, if the benefic planets are occupying it. It will be utterly miserable and distressful, if malefics alone are posited and it will give rise to happiness as well as misery, if there are benefic and malefic planets having the possession of such segment. 

From these principles, one thing has emerged quite clearly that the deficiency of the Rekhas could get compensated with the placement of benefic planets in a particular segment (Khanda) of the horoscope. Similarly, if the Khanda of the nativity holds least number of Rekhas and on the other hand it is occupied by the malefics planets, the corresponding part of the life of the native will become excessively miserable. Even otherwise, when the Rekhas are less in number, the native suffers from the sickness, mental worries, destitute and disappointments and on the top of it, if it is influenced by the malefics, the miseries will get compounded.

# yulabvabvabvaganni 1

# Briyata yghaanayaiotiritj 112C

11-28: Samudaya Ashtakavarga, if contains 7 or less than 7 rekhas, there will be fear of death of the native in the same month (that is, in the month the Sun passes through said sign). For the purpose of propitiation (मानिका) 20 Pala (Pala or Tola equivalent to 11.5 gms) Gold and 2 heaps of sesamum seeds (heap symbolizing mountain) should be given in charity. 

If a sign is holding 8 Rekhas, there will be the chances of the death in that very month. For the alleviation of this affliction, 'Tuladana' of camphor (weighting equivalent to natives weight be undertaken. 

If a sign is possessing 9 Rekhas, it will cause danger from the snakes in that solar month (Sun over such sign). To mitigate this ill effect, a chariot driven by four horses be donated in charity. 

If a sign is holding 10 Rekhas, it will produce danger from weapons in that solar month, in order to mitigate the evil effects, an armour alongwith Vajra (weapon) should be given in charity. 

O Vipra! when a sign secures 11 Rekhas, there will be possibility of getting disgraced in that (solar) month. For propitiation, an idol of the Moon (रितिम्) made out of 10 Palas of gold be donated in charity. 

The sign which is containing 12 Rekhas will cause danger of death by drowning in that (solar) month. The charity of (agricultural) land with the crop of the foodgrain to the Brahmins will confer auspicious effects. 

The sign having 13 Rekhas will produce danger of death by the tiger (wild animals) in the (solar) month. In order to ward off the evil effects, an idol of Lord Vishnu (ित्रधान्यू विषयु दिगवान) be given in charity. 

If a sign which is occupied with the 14 Rekhas, there will be danger of death in the solar month. For the propitiation, an idol of Varaha Moorti (रवाक्यु़) made in gold be given in charity. 

If a sign is containing 15 Rekhas, there will be danger from the king (authorities) in that Solar month. An elephant be donated 

in charity to get alleviation from this ill effect. 

The sign which possesses 16 Rekhas, in the solar month of that sign, a great misfortune will be caused. A replica of Kalpa Viksha (कूल्ति वर्षा - A legendry tree which fulfils desires) made in gold be given in charity which will ward off this evil effect. 

The sign which contains 17 Rekhas, in the (solar) month of that sign, there will be danger from diseases. In order to get this affliction alleviated, a cow and jaggery be given in charity. 

The sign which secures 18 Rekhas, the solar month of that sign will cause disputes (conflicts etc.). For propitiation, cow, gems, land and gold be denoted in charity. 

Positioning of 19 Rekhas in a sign will lead to residing in a foreign land (banishment from his homeland) in the (solar) month of such sign. Propitiations be performed with proper rituals to get relief. 

Occupation of 20 Rekha in a sign will cause loss of intelligence (prudence) in the (solar) month of that sign. To atone the ill effect, Lord Ganesha be worshipped. 

The sign with 21 Rekhas will produce disease and distress in the solar month of that sign. For the alleviation of such an evil effect, food grains in a heap form (structured as a replica of a mountain) be denoted in hairy. 

The sign with 22 Rekhas will cause miseries to the brothers (of the natives) in the solar month of that sign. For the propitiation, a mirror be given in charity. 

Positioning of 23 Rekhas in a sign will produce conflicts in the solar month of that sign. In order to mitigate this ill effect, an idol made in gold be given in charity. 

In the solar month of a sign which is possessing 24 Rekhas, there will be loss of co-borns (death of brothers etc.). For the alleviation of such an evil effect, 10 cows should be given in charity and for getting relief from all the diseases, worship (Japa - निर्णि) be performed. 

The sign with 25 Rekhas will cause loss of prudence (wisdom) in the corresponding (solar) month of that sign. For the propitiation of this malefic effect, the Japa (तिकत्) of Goddess 

Saraswati (Saraswathi) be performed. 

Thesign which is possessing 26 Rekhas, the corresponding solar month of that sign will cause loss of wealth. Japa of Shri Sookta ( शोर्थलि जन्द ) be performed for the alleviation of such evil effects. 

Positioning of 28 Rekhas in a sign will produce all round losses during the corresponding month (solar) of the sign. Havana (रियति) of the Sun with complete rituals be performed for seeking happiness. 

The solar month of the sign securing 29 Rekhas will cause anxiety over the worries. To ward off the evil effect, Ghee, clothes and gold be given in charity. 

The sign which is possessed with 30 Rekha, that solar month of the sign will be full of prosperity and happiness. 

Notes: The connotation prosperity used in the last paragraph for the word. "Dhana Dhanya" (प्रति रूध्य) embodied in the Shlokas is in the present context. 

# Results of more than 30 Rekhas in the Samudaya Ashtakavarga :

BriyAaiiikaii: 1 

29: Possessing more than 30 Rekhas [i.e., the sign having secured Rekhas in excess of 30, in the month (solar), Samvatsar (रान्दिल्ति) and Nakshatra] will confer accretion of wealth, children and happiness. If there are more than 40 Rekhas (in a sign), it will give boost to pious deeds, wealth (Laxmi - रूकी) and fame. 

# Commendable system of Ashtakavarga :

Ae 1 1 1 

the Dasa pattern will be in the reverse order. As the maxim goes after the first Dasa of Cancer the next Dasa will be of the third sign after skipping the two signs in the reverse order viz., Aries Dasa, next Capricorn and thereafter the Dasa of Libra will come. The Dasa period (years) are like Sthira Dasa system. The Cancer sign being a movable sign, each Dasa will be of 7 years duration. On the completion of first cycle of movable signs, the next round of Dasas will be of dual signs starting from Gemini, Pisces, Sagittarius and Virgo with the duration of 8 years each. Lastly, Dasa serial of dual signs will follow in the order; Taurus, Aquarius, Scorpio and Leo sign of 9 years duration of each Dasa. 

Maharishi Parasara has attributed 7 yrs; 8 yrs; and 9 yrs; to Movable. Fixed and Dual signs respectively while Rishi Jaimini reckoned 9 years identically for all the signs. 

# 21. Shoola Dasa

Ninayagamay Vibchavargay Kerehivcshcukuladhya Tmuyta I   
LagmnaSahatMatao MuTuMnayoyi BalaVanu MbovatI 1921   
TahadaViirjyme Vbpr ！KamaduLokmaat: Samae I   
Dvshara: SiyharvatLzhr BaJIimHaarKemoe MuJit: 1921 

181-182 : Some astrological Savants consider Shoola Dasa for ascertaining the timing of death. The eighth house from the Ascendant or the 7th house i.e. the 2nd house, which ever is stronger will lead the Dasa order into motion. The sequence of the twelve signs will be in the forward or in the reverse order depending upon the leading sign being odd or even. The Dasas years are identical to those in the Sthira Dasa system. Death possibly occurs in the Dasa of strong Capricorn sign. 

Notes : Shoola Dasa is also known as Niryana Shoola Dasa. MaharishiParasara has adopted an identical Dasa-span of the signs of the Sthir-Dasa even for the Shoola-Dasa system though Rishi Jaimini considered 9 years for all the signs of the Dasa. In the exampled horoscope, Rahu and Ketu are posited in the 2nd and the 8th house respectively and as such it is not possible to ascertain the comparative strength of these signs. On the basis of longitudinal difference, the lord of the 8th house, the Sun 

# Aey 104

# Chapter-75

# PLANETARY RAYS

जय ेर्षमीनु प्रवधियावपि प्रधाना तिकजसलम ! | 

[191] 

Niihe k chanlare prakatla ramaychanaaata: 

Niiioeon n 2u 4h h aahaiik k cokiaiaiaiaiaiaia 

Keeiayaraihmi 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 8e 

1-2½: Maharishi Parasara said, O Superior Brahmin! Now I will describe the planetary rays. In the state of deep exalation, the unit of the rays for the Sun etc. are respectively 10, 9, 5, 5, 7, 8 and 5 units. The unit of the rays at the deep debilitation point is nil and the unit of the rays for the point in between these two extreme points (the deep exaltation and the deep debilitation) is ascertained on a proportionate basis. In order to determine the rays of a particular planet, deduct the sign of debilitation from the longitude of that planet and if the resultant turned out to be more than 6 signs, it should be subtracted from the 12 signs (if the resultant is less than 6 signs, it should be kept unaltered). The resultant is then multiplied by the unit of the rays assigned to such 

planet. The product be then divided by 6. The resultant will denote the number of rays of that planet. 

Notes: The units of the rays for the Sun etc. planet at their deep exaltation points have been tabulated as under : 


TABLE - 38Rays of Deep exaltation point :


<table><tr><td>Planets</td><td>Sun</td><td>Moon</td><td>Mars</td><td>Marc</td><td>Jup</td><td>Ven</td><td>Sat</td></tr><tr><td>At deep exaltation</td><td>10</td><td>9</td><td>5</td><td>5</td><td>7</td><td>8</td><td>5</td></tr></table>

The procedure for determining the proportionate unit of the rays at a point falling in between the deep exaltation and deep debilitation points as narrated in the foregoing axioms is summed up as follows: 

i) The deep debilitation sign of the concerned planet is deducted from the longitude of the said planet. If the resultant figure in less than 6 signs $(180^{\circ})$ , it should be multiplied by the unit of rays of the deep exaltation point of the same planet (as shown in the above table). The product be divided by 6. The outcome will indicate the number of rays for the planet. 

ii) If the outcome happens to be more than six signs, it should be further deducted from the 12 signs $(360^{\circ})$ and the figures so obtained be multiplied by the unit of the rays of the deep exaltation point of the planet and as usual, the product be divided by 6 and the resultant will denote the number of rays for the planet. 

# Refinement over the determined number of the Rays :

Aeepreerree r sckkarvivrro: kchitot ywha I 11 11 

L

Jadivahgughe djjn: yvamkata: 

रानिकुको विना रागवागि अले तल्ल दियी || | | 

विरैयपी अधिकान्यो के जोर्था: समन्दिका तिज ! | 

Rahmuyagavshaaedev fcl vachy vihchau: | 10 | 

3-7: O Vipra! some of the other Acharyas have indicated further refinements over the determined number of the rays. If a planet is posited in its sign of exaltation, the determined number of the rays are trebled. If it is in the Moola Trikona sign, the determined rays are to be doubled. In case of being in its own sign, the determined number is multiplied by 3 and then divided by 12. If a planet is in the Adhinitra's (best friend) sign, the determined rays be multiplied by 4 and then divided by 3. In the event of friendly sign, the determined rays be multiplied by 6 and then divided by 5. In case of planet being in the enemy sign, the determined number of rays be brought down to half and if it is in the Adhishatru's sign (bitter enemy), the determined rays are first doubled and then divided by 5. If the planet is in the neutral sign, no correction is called far. 

Barring Mercury and Venus, the number of rays in respect of all other planets will get annulled on getting combust. The learned astrologers should delineate the results on the basis of the determined number of rays duly moderated (i.e. final net numbers). 

Notes : The deep debilitation degrees of the planets are as follows : 

# Planet Deep debilitation degrees

1. Sun Libra $10^{\circ}$ 

2. Moon Scorpio $3^{\circ}$ 

3. Mars Cancer $28^{\circ}$ 

4. Mercury Pisces $15^{\circ}$ 

5. Jupiter Capricorn $5^{\circ}$ 

6. Venus Virgo $27^{\circ}$ 

7. Saturn Aries $20^{\circ}$ 

Let us take an example and apply these rules to make the finer details understandable by the readers. 

# Example:94

Data: Date of birth, 6-7-1977; Time of birth 9-30 p.m.; Latitude, $28^{\circ}$ -43'(N); Longitude, $77^{\circ}$ -47'(E). 

The logitudinal details of the birth chart of the native are as follows: 

<table><tr><td>Planets</td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mint.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>Ascendant</td><td>Aries</td><td>21</td><td>35</td><td>46</td></tr><tr><td>Sun</td><td>Leo</td><td>20</td><td>23</td><td>27</td></tr><tr><td>Moon</td><td>Gemini</td><td>1</td><td>57</td><td>9</td></tr><tr><td>Mars</td><td>Gemini</td><td>9</td><td>55</td><td>36</td></tr><tr><td>Mercury</td><td>Leo</td><td>17</td><td>43</td><td>8</td></tr><tr><td>Jupiter</td><td>Gemini</td><td>9</td><td>7</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Venus</td><td>Caner</td><td>17</td><td>12</td><td>20</td></tr><tr><td>Saturn</td><td>Caner</td><td>29</td><td>59</td><td>00</td></tr></table>

Mercury is the only planet which is combust. As per the aphorisms expounded in the foregoing Shalokas, Mercury inspite of being combust, its number of rays do not get annulled. The correction enunciated in these Shlokas are entirely based upon the Panchdha Maitri (compound relationship) of the planets in the nativity and therefore, it is necessary to draw Pachdha Maitri Chakra (Chart) for the native. 


Panchdha Maitri Chakra :


<table><tr><td>Planets</td><td>Sun</td><td>Moon</td><td>Mars</td><td>Mere.</td><td>Jup.</td><td>Ven.</td><td>Sat.</td></tr><tr><td>Best Friend</td><td>Sun Mars Jup</td><td>Sun Merc</td><td>Sun</td><td>Ven</td><td>Sun</td><td>Merc</td><td>Merc</td></tr><tr><td>Friend</td><td>-</td><td>Sat</td><td>Ven Sat</td><td>Mars Jup Sat</td><td>Sat</td><td>Mars Jup</td><td>Jup</td></tr><tr><td>Neutral</td><td>Ven Sat</td><td>Ven</td><td>Jup Moon Merc</td><td>Sun Moon</td><td>Moon Mars Merc Ven</td><td>Sun Moon</td><td>Sun Moon Mars Ven</td></tr><tr><td>Enemy</td><td>Merc</td><td>Mars Jup</td><td>-</td><td>-</td><td>-</td><td>Sat</td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td>Bitter Enemy</td><td>-</td><td>-</td><td>-</td><td>-</td><td>-</td><td>-</td><td>-</td></tr></table>


Computation of number of Rays of the planets



The Sun :


<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mint.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of the Sun</td><td>4</td><td>20</td><td>23</td><td>27</td></tr><tr><td>Less : Deep debilitation point of the Sun :</td><td>6</td><td>10</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>10</td><td>10</td><td>23</td><td>27</td></tr></table>

Since the balance is more then 6 signs, reduction from 12 signs be carried out. 

<table><tr><td>12</td><td>00</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>(-) 10</td><td>10</td><td>23</td><td>27</td></tr><tr><td>1</td><td>19</td><td>36</td><td>33</td></tr></table>

The assigned number of rays to the Sun is 10 and therefore, the above balance figures be multiplied by 10 and divided by 6. 

$$
\begin{array}{l} \frac {(1 - 1 9 - 3 6 - 3 3) \times 1 0}{6} \\ \frac {(1 6 - 1 6 - 5 - 3 0)}{6} = \quad 2 - 2 3 \end{array}
$$

The Sun is posited in the Moola Trikona sign and therefore, this figure should be doubled. 

$$
(2 - 2 3) \times 2 = 4 - 4 6
$$

The net number of rays of the Sun $= 4 - 46$ 

# The Moon

<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mint.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of the Moon</td><td>2</td><td>1</td><td>57</td><td>9</td></tr><tr><td>Less: Deep debilitation point of the Moon</td><td>8</td><td>3</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>5</td><td>28</td><td>57</td><td>09</td></tr></table>

Since the balance is less 6 signs, these figures be multiplied by 9 and divided by 6 

$$
\frac {(5 - 2 8 - 5 7 - 9) \times 9}{6}
$$

$$
\frac {5 3 - 2 0 - 3 4 - 2 1}{6} = 8 - 2 8
$$

The Moon is located in a Adhinitra sign (best friend). Therefore, the above number of rays be multiplied by 4 and divided by 3 

$$
\frac {(8 - 2 8) \times 4}{3} = 1 1 - 2 7
$$

The net number of rays of the Moon $= 11 - 27$ 

# Mars :

<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mint.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of the Mars</td><td>2</td><td>9</td><td>55</td><td>36</td></tr><tr><td>Less: Deep debilitation point of the Mars</td><td>3</td><td>28</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>10</td><td>11</td><td>55</td><td>36</td></tr></table>

Since the balance is more than 6 signs, reduction from the 12 signs be carried out. 

<table><tr><td rowspan="3">(-)</td><td>12</td><td>00</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>10</td><td>11</td><td>55</td><td>36</td></tr><tr><td>1</td><td>18</td><td>4</td><td>24</td></tr></table>

The above figures be multiplied by 5 (rays of Mars) and divided by 6. 

$$
\begin{array}{l} \frac {(1 - 1 8 - 4 - 2 4) \times 5}{6} \\ = \frac {8 - 0 0 - 2 2}{6} = 1 - 1 0 \\ \end{array}
$$

Mars is posited in the neutral sign, hence no further moderation is required. 

The net number of rays of Mars = 1 - 10 

# Mercury :

<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mint.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of Mercury</td><td>4</td><td>17</td><td>43</td><td>8</td></tr><tr><td>Less: Deep debilitation point of Mercury</td><td>11</td><td>15</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>5</td><td>2</td><td>43</td><td>8</td></tr></table>

Since the balance is less than 6 signs, it can straight away, be multiplied by 5 (rays of Mercury) and divided by 6. 

$$
= \frac {\frac {(5 - 2 - 4 3 - 8) \times 5}{6}}{\frac {2 5 - 1 3 - 3 5 - 4 0}{6}} = 4 - 7
$$

the Dasa running at birth. 

From the table given below, the readers can early ascertain the planet, their Dasa years and Nakshatras represented by them.

# Balance of Dasa

davahamannn mayalatcln mghoogon n hult faklma I dhyaay ma uukvatvabg y oagm maanadu vshoohitam 1951 

16: Multiply the Dasa years of the planet, in whose Dasa the birth takes place by Bhayaata [The expired (elapsed) period of the Nakshatra] and divide it by Bhabhoga [The full period to be traversed by the Nakshatra]. The resultant value will represent the period consumed in the Dasa of the planet till the time of birth. By deducting this figure from the full duration of the Dasa of the planet, the balance Dasa of the said planet is arrived at. (For terms Bhagyaata and Bhabhoga please refer to page 65 of volume 1). 

# Note:

Example-1: Data: Date of birth 11/12-6-1981; Time of birth - 1-30 (night), Place of birth; Latitude $28^{\circ}$ -43' N, Longitude $77^{\circ}$ -55'E (The calculations are based on Vishwa Panchanga of B.H. University, Varanasi, India).

The balance be multiplied by 8 (rays of Venus) and divided by 6. 

$$
= \frac {\frac {(2 - 9 - 4 7 - 4 0) \times 6}{6}}{\frac {1 8 - 1 8 - 2 1 - 2 0}{6}} = 3 - 3
$$

Venus is also posited in the neutral sign hence, no moderation is necessary. 

The net number of rays of Venus = 3 - 3 

Saturn : 

<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mint.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of Saturn</td><td>3</td><td>29</td><td>59</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td>Less : Deep debilitation point of Saturn</td><td>0</td><td>20</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>3</td><td>9</td><td>59</td><td>00</td></tr></table>

The balance is less than 6 sign and hence, to be multiplied by 5 (rays of Saturn) and divided by 6. 

$$
= \frac {\frac {(3 - 9 - 5 9 - 0 0) \times 5}{6}}{6} = 2 - 2 3
$$

Satum is also placed in a neutral sign and hence, no further moderation is required.

wealth, idiot and cunning; with 13 total rays - enganged in steeling; with 14 total rays - looks after many families and religious, follows the family traditions, wealthy and learned; with total 15 rays - high education and qualities, head of the family, this is what Lord Brahma has stated; with 16 rays - most notable in the family; with 17 rays - will have many servants; with 18 rays - famous; with 20 rays - will have many co-borns.

20: Like, a native is born in a Kshatrya family (securing total number of rays more the 50), he will be an emperor. If the native is born in a Vaishya family (with the same count of rays), he will be a king. If the native takes birth in a Sudra family (under the same circumstances), he will be wealthy; and if the birth being in a Brahmin family, he will be highly learned, scholar and a religious person.

22-23: O Brahmin! The auspicious and inauspicious results are in accordance to the number of rays secured by any planet. The prognostication of the correct results without the knowledge of the number of rays will not be possible. Therefore, the scholars should determine the number of rays (of the planets) before predicting the results.

# SUDARSHANA CHAKRA

1-3 : Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! Now I divulge the secret of a suprior knowledge which Lord Brahma himself narrated for the welfare of the world at large. This is known as "Sudarshana Chakra" on the basis of which, the Astrologers will develop a clear insight of perdition from the Ascendant and the houses, for the persons and their related auspicious and inauspicious results from birth through death, for every year, every month and every day. 

# Structure of SudarshanaChakra :

Eeknneodmbrn 1 

aadadraar ane 1tut uayaii aee

<table><tr><td>Dasa Srl.</td><td>Dasa Lord</td><td>Days</td><td>Months</td><td>Years</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">1</td><td rowspan="2">Aries - Mars</td><td>2</td><td>6</td><td>1951</td></tr><tr><td>17</td><td>11</td><td>2</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">2</td><td rowspan="2">Taurus - Venus</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>1954</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>16</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">3</td><td rowspan="2">Gemini - Mercury</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>1970</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>9</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">4</td><td rowspan="2">Cancer - Moon</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>1979</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>21</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">5</td><td rowspan="2">Leo - Sun</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>2030</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>5</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">6</td><td rowspan="2">Virgo - Mercury</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>2005</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>9</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">7</td><td rowspan="2">Libra - Venus</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>2014</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>16</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">8</td><td rowspan="2">Scorpio - Mars</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>2030</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>7</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">9</td><td rowspan="2">Sagittarius - Jupiter</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>2037</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>10</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>TOTAL</td><td>19</td><td>5</td><td>2047</td></tr></table>

The apropos maxim could also be explained in this manner. The full value (in Ghatis & Palas) of one quarter of the Nakshatra encompasses the corresponding complete life span. Accordingly, the expired life span could be ascertained from the elapsed portion of the Nakshatra's quarter at the time of birth. 

Formula = Expired portion of quarter x full span (year) Full value of Nakshatra's quarter 

= Expired period of birth. 

One fourth of the Bhabhoga equals one quarter (Pada). Therefore, the value of the quarter (Pada) would be different for each native. 

In the aforesaid example, with a view to have an easy calculation, the Bhabhoga is taken as 60 Ghatis and 1/4th of which i.e. 15 Ghatis is 

# Sudarshana Chakra

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/30bb78adcd1ef41aacd5f5f451ffcea7a5db7651867c5c10691c597e5b781a27.jpg)


In the exampled horoscope, the Ascendant is Aries which is denoted by 1 and placed in the first compartment of the inner circle and rest of the planets are placed in their respective sign in this circle. The Moon is posited in the Gemini and therefore, Gemini (Moon's sign) is placed above the Aries in the middle circle and the other planets are positioned in their respective signs. Similarly, the Sun is in the Leo sign of the birth chart and accordingly, placed above the Gemini in the outer circle and others planets in the same fashion, have taken their respective positions in the outer circle. After this, Sudarshana Chakra is ready for delineation. 

# Special features of Sudarshana Chakra :

7-9: The peculiarity of Sudarshana Chakra is that, the Ist house, the Ascendant, the Moon and the Sun get combined. Considering this (Ist house in respect of all the three) as Ascendant, the house of wealth (2nd house there from) etc. be reckoned. Thereafter, in accordance to the positions of the planets in these houses, the results be delineated. The Sun be considered auspicious in the Ascendant and inauspicious in the other houses. Malefic planets if are in the sign of exaltation or own sign, may not be treated as inauspicious. In this way, after looking into the auspiciousness and inauspiciousness of the planets, the results should be predicted. 

yo bhav: kwhaimisotamayamaay auyukto  o 

papayofoyuyuoyo yoa yaa 100 

10: The house which is harboured by its lord or posited with or aspected by the auspicious planets becomes powerful while the aspects and positioning of the malefic planets in a house will make them weak.

11-13: The results of the combined houses, be considered on the basis of the presence of the planets over there. If a house is devoid 

of planet but receives the aspect, the results be stated accordingly. The house having the benefic planets will be auspicious and the house occupied by the malefics, will deliver inauspicious results. If a house holds mixed planets (both the benefic and malefic), the results will be auspicious, if the number of the benefic planets is more and if the malefic planets are more in numbers, the malefic results will prevail. In this manner, the combinations and the aspects of the planets to a particular house (i.e. the benefic aspects to a houses will deliver auspicious and malefic aspects to a house will cause inauspicious results) be taken into account and the result be stated in accordance to the factors which are more pronounced. 

Notes: The important principles propounded in the foregoing Shlokas are very useful from the point of view of predicting the results. They are summed up as follows: 

(i) The first and the formost principle is that the results of the house will depend upon the planets occupying the house. 

(ii) In the absence of any planet, the results are delineated on the basis of the aspects received by a particular house. 

(iii) The consideration of auspiciousness and inauspiciouness of the planets in a house is very important. If it is assessed that all the planets are benefic, the auspicious results will come in planty and easily. If malefic planets are influencing the house, such house will be deprived of the results connected with the Karatakwa of the house. 

(iv) If a house is holding both beneficial and malefic planets, the results will be influenced by the planets which are more in number. 

(v) Suppose the number of both beneficial and malefic are identical, in that case, the strength of the planets is to be ascertained. If the strength of the benefic planet is more, the results will be auspicious and in a reverse situation, the malefic planets will gain the strength. 

(vi) If a house is bereft of planets, the results will be on the basis of aspects. Benefic aspects will indicate auspicious and malefic inauspicious results. Should both types of aspects be in an equal proportion, the strength of the aspects will decide the result. 

y 1 

14: If a house is neither occupied by the planets nor receiving the aspects, the results then be judged on the basis of the auspicious and inauspicious Karkatwas of the lord of the house. 

Special effects of the auspicious and inauspicious planets.

15-16: If a benefic planet is having many inauspicious Vargas, it is considered delivering malefic results. If a malefic planets occupy more number of auspicious Vargas, benefic results be predicted. The placement of a planet in the sign of exaltation, own sign and in the Vargas of the benefic planets will make the planet as auspicious and positioning of a planet in the enemy or debilitation signs or its divisions (six fold), renders the planet as inauspicious.

17: Maharishi Parasara said - O Brahmin! In this manner, the auspiciousness and the inauspiciousness of the planets, and the houses be considered, for predicting the results of the concerned 

houses.

A malefic planet, posited in the debilitation or inimical sign will denote inauspicious Varga and benefic planet, posited in the exaltation or own sign will indicate auspicious Varga. It is on the basis of this crteria, each planet has been assigned the numbers shown in the last two rows of the Saptvarga's table appended below. It may be noted that more importance is given to the natural beneficence rather than the criteria of friendly relationship. 


SAPTAVARGA CHAKRA OF THE HOUSE

It is essesntial for the houses, first of all, to establish the auspiciousness and inauspiciousness of the planets connected with them. Let us take the position of the Sun in the nativity. In the Saptavargas Chakra, It has secured 5 Vargas of auspiciousness and 2 Vargas of inauspiciousness, because, the Sun has gone in the sign of debilitation in the Navamsa and in the rest of the Vargas, it is situated in the friendly or Vargas of the benefic planets. Although, in Trimsamsa, the Sun is in the Varga of Mercury, but the Sun is the enemy of Mercury and hence is considered to be in an inauspicious Varga (see Example-94, Chapter-75). 

The Moon secures 6 auspicious and 1 inauspicious Vargas. In the Panchdha Maitri Chakra, the Moon has become enemy of Mars. The lords of the other signs and Vargas are either friends of the Moon or benefic planets. Therefore, the Moon has secured 6 auspicious Vargas. 

Mars, in Trimsamsa has gone in the Varga of Saturn. The Vargas of other planets occupied by Mars are either friendly or benefic 

planets. 

Mercury obtained 5 auspicious and 2 inauspicious Vargas. Mercury is in the sign of the Sun which is a malefic planet. Besides, in Dawadasamsa Varga, It has gone in the sign of debilitation and hence 2 inauspicious Vargas. 

Jupiter has also secured 6 auspicious and 1 inauspicious Vargas. The Sun is the best friend of Jupiter and that is why, the Sun has been counted as auspicious over here. In the Panchadha Maltri Chakra (see Example-94, Chapter-75). Saturn is the friend of the Jupiter but in natural relationship, they are enemy to each other. It could have been considered an inauspicious Varga but for Jupiter securing auspicious Varga on the basis of compound relationship. 

On similar logic, Venus has 4 auspicious and 3 Inauspicious Vargas. Satum gets 5 auspicious and 2 inauspicious Vargas. Like wise, the auspicious and inauspicious Vargas for Rahu and Ketu are 4, 3 and 1, 6 respectively. 

From these analyses, it can be said that the Moon, Mercury, Jupiter and Venus are auspicious planets while amongst the malefic planets, the Sun is little above medium auspicious. Mars with 6 auspicious and one inauspicious Vargas can not, as well, be termed as truly inauspicious. Inauspiciousness of Ketu is the maximum but Saturn and Rahu are moderately inauspicious planets. 

The analyses of the houses are discussed here after. 

The First House: It does not possess any planet and hence it will not gain any strength on account of planetary combinations. Let us now see the position of the aspects on the Ascendant. The 1st house receives the aspect of Saturn only, which has acquired, 5 auspicious and 2 inauspicious Vargas and on this basis, Saturn becomes benefic. But it is a natural malefic planet. This will thus signify medium health (neither very sound not weak) of the native.. The effect of Saturn will also give him good height and black hair. At times, he will have complaint of headache. One important fact to be noted over here is that if, a malefic planet secures good number of auspicious Vargas, it is considered benefic in the financial matters but will not do good from the health point of view. It could thus be concluded that the birth of the native would have been in a well to do family. 

The Second House : This house is also devoid of any planet but it receives 9th aspect of Rahu (the lord of the 2nd house Venus has secured little more than medium auspiciousness). Therefore, the native will have weak eye sight and the native would have taken spectacle at an early age and will be possessing a peculiar white patch on his face. The 2nd house signifies the life span of adolescent after the childhood which might have passed through well by the native. 

The Third House: This house is occupied by the Moon, Jupiter and Mars and all of them have secured more number of the auspicious Vargas. This, therefore, will signify that the native is bestowed with the happiness of many younger co-boms. Besides, he will be an expert in handicraft and will perform his skill with great dexterity and precision. 

The Fourth House : This house harbours Venus and Saturn. Venus has auspiciousness little over medium and the status of Saturn has already been described while analysing the first house. The native is conferred with house, social status, fame but will not be having the benefit of the mother's comforts. 

The Fifth House : The Sun and Mercury are occupying this house. The Sun is generally clubbed with the other malefic planets except being in the Ascendant. The Sun, however, has secured 5 auspicious and 2 inauspicious points in the Saptavarga Chakra. Mercury has also obtained the same count. Therefore, it can be concluded that the education will run well but at an average level. 

The Sixth House : It is posited with Rahu which possesses medium inauspiciousness and therefore, the health will be mediocre. 

The Seventh House; This house also does not hold any planet. Therefore, it should be assessed on the basis of aspects. Jupiter with 6 auspicious and 1 inauspicious points is aspecting the 7th house. Therefore, the married life will be comfortable and the marriage alliance will also be with a good family. 

The Eight House : There is no planet in it and hence, the aspect on this house be considered. Ketu is aspecting the 8th house. In this nativity, Ketu is the most malefic planet. Therefore, the native will not cherish a long life. In the Chapter of Ashtakavarga, the native's age has been assessed as 51 years, 3 months, 27 days and 12 hrs. 

The Ninth House : There is no planet in it but it is receiving 

the aspects of the Moon, Jupiter and Mars planets. The Moon and Jupiter have been proved as most auspicious planets in their respective assessment carried out earlier. Although Mars is a natural malefic planet, it has secured 6 auspicious and 1 inauspicious counts. Hence the native shall be inclined towards religion, be famous and will receive blessings of big persons. 

In this manner, the rest of the houses may also be analysed.

18-18½: Maitreya said - O Maharishi! When the predictions for the natives could be made through Sudarshana Chakra why the other sages narrate the results from the Ascendant? I had only this much doubt and O-Lord, kindly clarify the same.

19-20: Parasara said - If the Sun and the Moon are posited in the different signs other than the sign of the Ascendant, the results from the Sudarshna Chakrabe only delineated. Between these three viz., the Ascendant (Lord), the Sun and the Moon, if two or all the three are posited in the Ascendant, the results be declared on the basis of the Ascendant.

21-23 : O Vipra! Now I will narrate the results of every year etc., from the Sudarshna Chakra in accordance to Dasa and Antardasa. Assigning one year to each house commencing from the Ascendant, assume every house as Ascendant for that very year and the following house as the 2nd house and soon. Thereafter, presume an Antardasa of one month in the every year in each house and then from the lst house of the Antardasa, the 12 signs in seriatim, be assumed as 12 months which should be perceived as the results giving months connected with the 12 houses. 

Similarly, commencing from the house identical with the month, each house be taken for the Pratyantar Dasa of two and a half days. Considering the sign of the Pratyantar Dasa as the Ascendant, the results of the 2nd, 3rd houses etc. be taken as the effects for two and half days each. In the same way, the Sookshima Dasa of $12\frac{1}{2}$ hrs. is worked out and its results may also be considered in the same manner 

# The method of prediction :

24-26: If the Angular, Trinal and the 8th houses from the house emanating the Dasa, are occupied with the benefic planets, it (Dasa) will deliver auspicious results. Rahu and Ketu, occupying the houses will cause loss to such houses. The house holding more number of malefic planets gets destroyed. Barring the 6th and the 12th houses, the benefic planets in other houses bestow auspicious results. The malefic in the 3rd, 6th and 11th house give benefic results. (That is benefic planets in the 6th and the 12th house will do harm while malefic planets in the 3rd, the 6th and the 11th house will do good). In this manner, the results of every year, month, day etc. be assessed right 

from the time of birth. 

Ten cycles of 12 houses from the Ascendant (12 years of each cycle) of Dasas or Antardasa should be considered. (The ten cycles of 12 houses make 120 years of Poorna Ayu, a full life cycle under Vimshottari Dasasystem). 

Notes : To have a proper insight of the appropriate principle embodied in Shlokas 21 to 26, we will take the support of the example. 

Yearly Dasas: Suppose we need to delineate the results of the first year, the Ascendant be taken as first year. In other words, the first house will have the first Dasa of one year. It may be seen from the Sudarshana Chakra that the birth Ascendant is the Aries sign and hence, the Aries sign will rule over the first year of the native. Since the Ascendant denotes first year, the rest of the houses of the Chakra will remain as it is. The third house is occupied by the Moon, Jupiter and Mars. As per stated principle, malefic planets in the 3rd, the 6th and the 11th houses confer favourable results. Here, Mars is the only malefic planet but vide Saptavargas Chakra, its inauspiciousness has been mitigated and has since acquired the auspicious qualities. 

The 4th house, with the position of benefic planet Venus over there, will deliver favourable results. Besides, Saturn is also posited over there but Saturn has also shed its malevolent traits as per Saptavarga Chakra and has since secured some element of beneficence. 

The 5th house is occupied by the Sun and Mercury. The Sun is in its own sign and Mercury is an auspicious planet. Hence, this will deliver favourable results. In the 6th house and the 12th houses, Rahu and Ketu respectively are posited. The rule relating to this is that, the house which harbours Rahu or Ketu, will cause harm to such house. 

Monthly Antardasas : The first year belongs to the Ascendant and accordingly, the first month of Antardasa will also go to the Ascendant (the 1st house). The 1st house is owned by Aries sign and as such the month will also be that of Aries. The second monthly Antardasa will pertain to the 2nd house i.e. of Taurus sign and it should be treated as Ascendant for the second month. It means that the physical comforts of the native will 

be identical to the status of the 2nd house (as Ascendant) and also the lord of the 2nd house in the nativity should by studied accordingly. In the same month, the 3rd house will show the results of the 2nd house i.e., for wealth etc. Likewise, the subsequent houses are studied. In the same manner, the third house be reckoned as Ascendant for the third month and for which the 4th house of the birth chart will denote the 2nd house and status of the wealth etc. should be interpreted therefrom. 

For thesecondyear, the 2nd house should be treated as the Ascendant. Taurus owns the 2nd house and as such, the Taurus Dasa will operate in the second year and for which the 3rd house will act as house of wealth, (2nd house) etc. The rest of the house will take their positions considering the 2nd house as Ascendant. In this Dasa, the first monthly Antardasa will also go to the 2nd house (as Ascendant) and so on and so forth. 

Under the same scheme, the 3rd house will assume the role of Ascendant in the third year and its yearly Dasa will operate. The 2nd, the 3rd house etc. will be represented by the 4th, the 5th houses respectively. In the same fashion, the 12 years results will be delineated from the 12 houses. 

In the 2nd cycle of the Dasas, the 13th year will again belong to the Ascendant and the Dasas of the succeeding years should be situated exactly on the lines explained earlier. 

Pratyantar Dasas : Commencing from the monthly Ascendant (of the Antardasa), the 12 signs will indicate the 12 Pratyantar Dasas of $2\frac{1}{2}$ days ( $2\frac{1}{2} \times 12 = 30$ days = one month). Let us suppose, we have to see the result of the first $2\frac{1}{2}$ days of the first month and the first year. Here, the Ascendant will act as the Ist house and following it, will be the 2nd house and so on. What, we mean to say is that the total effect for these $2\frac{1}{2}$ days should be interpreted from the corresponding houses, keeping the Ist house as the Ascendant. Accordingly, for the next $2\frac{1}{2}$ days, the 2nd house will assume the position of the Ascendant and the effects be interpreted from all the 12 houses in the same manner. 

In the Sudarshana Chakra system, 30 days are taken for a month which is identical to a solar month. For the benefic of the readers, the Dasa, Antardasa and Pratyantardasa Chakras representing the Ist twelve years cycle, the Ist twelve months cycle and the Ist twelve $2 \frac{1}{2}$ (2 days - 12 hrs.) 

cycle respectively are illustrated and could be applied on the example being considered for this purpose.

27-28: Judging the results for the year, month, day etc., from the Sudarshana Chakra system and also analysing the same through Ashtakavarga system, the net results be determined. When the same auspicious and inauspicious results are indicated from both these systems, then only the prognostications are declared. If there exist some difference in both these results, that is, one is showing auspiciousness and other is indicating inauspiciousness, then the results should be stated on the basis of the comparative strength of the case. 

sign is altogether on a different footing. In this scheme, the natal Moon has no relevance. In Apsavya, the allotment cycle of the Navamsa-sign is in the reverse order commencing from Scorpio, Libra, Leo, Cancer, Gemini, Taurus, Aries etc. 

There are twelve Navamsa-signs (the 12 zodiacal signs). As per the explanations rendered in respect of Savya and Apsavya Nakshatras and the distributions of the Navams-sign to their respective quarters (Padas), two tables are drawn i.e. Table-21 and Table-22 which will make the concept amply clear.

3-7: Features of Ruchaka Yoga : A person born in Ruchaka Yoga will have longish face, over zealous, radiant but polite, extremely powerful, beautiful eye brows, black hair, refined tastes, fond of battle fields, dark red complexion, conqueror, intelligent, head of the thieves, cruel temperament, slender thighs, devout of Brahamins, holding 'Veena', 'Yagra', 'Dhanush', 'Pasha' and 'Vrishabha' sign in his hands and having "Chakra-Rekha"; well versed in incantation, occultism and employing the spells with malevolent purpose, having the height of hundred 'Angulas' with equal proportion of length of the face and the waist, equal to one thousand "Tulas (तु़ला)", ruler of Vindya and Sahasya Pradesh and will attain Swarga (heavens) at the age of 70 years and will die by weapon or fire. 

Notes: If Mars being in the sign of Aries, Scorpio or in Capricorn and get posited in the Angular houses, it will form Ruchaka Yoga. This Yoga will, therefore, not be created in the Ascendants of Virgo, Sagittarius and Pisces signs. Besides, it is also not necessary that in which ever nativity this Yoga is formed, it will deliver the identical results. 

Infact, Sage Parasara has greatly generalised the results of this Yoga and if we deeply study these effects, we will notice some amount of contradiction. In Aries and Capricorn Ascendants, the described results would be more prominent in the native. Suppose Mars is posited in Capricorn sign in Aries Ascendant, the Mars apart from being in the sign of exaltation, it will acquire the directional strength and will cause direct 4th aspect on the Ascendant. In such a situation, it will gain abundant strength in bringing an equilibrium 

between mind and temperament. 

The position of Mars in the Ascendant of Capricorn is also considered quite powerful and the only difference would be that it will lose half the potency of directional strength. Here again the Ascendant (self) and 4th house (mind) would be under the influence of Mars especially so when Mars owns the 4th house. With these kind of disposition an illustration is cited here under : 

Example : 97 

Data: Date of birth 2/3-3-1943; Time of birth 5-00 hrs. (IST), Latitude: $28^{\circ} - 39'$ (N); Longitude: $77^{\circ} - 13'$ (E) 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/9b02d6468f81f9ddd63fab5782ca8923c0487b40bdc4348ae6e22b8e70a735f5.jpg)

This birth chart pertains to one I.A.S. Officer (Indian Administrative Service). Mars is exalted in an Angular house forming Ruchaka Yoga. This native has got longish face with dark red complexion, stout body, hot temperament but the position of the Moon and Mercury in the Ascendant protects him from being cruel. Having born in an elite family, he possesses literary and high class interests. He has special

# Results of the Antardasa of Ketu in the Mahadasa of Mercury :

Bghayyannatgaii kai 1 

yuyuyuyue yuyuueyue yuyuyue 

yogakarckasamshn 1 

रेशिकस्थान्य अनुल्ली दोत्तिकस्थान्यानिरूल ् ू ा ि 

Chutuayaaajaiyaihata: yaiatae baoergha gnagam: 

Vivahakaiitgprasagkhrh Sarnanrnnnnae 

moojnanamnraaahy aadai maohu knaaahm | 12 

6-8½: During the Antardasa of Ketu in the Mahadasa of Mercury, if Ketu is placed in Angle or Trine, conjoined with or aspected by the benefic planets or associated with the Yogakaraka planet or from the lord of the Mahadasa, if it is located at Angle or in the 11th house, it will confer results like bodily comforts. generation of abundant wealth, affection of brothers and friends; gain of animals, acquisition of wealth through travellings; educational attainments and opportunities for improvement of reputation; honourable meeting with the king; comforts of food and clothes in the beginning and also in the middle of the Antardasa. 

Notes: The Edition of Brihat Parasara Hora Sastra of Chaukhambha carried a word "प्रधान्यम्" which means influx of excessive wealth. In other words, during the Ketu's Antardasa of the Mahadasa of Mercury, one becomes wealthy. Looking into the other results of the 

weaponary, adorns black and curly hairs, having an independant nature but is the protector of the family. Shares his luxuries and enjoyments with his friends, heavy weight (20 Tulas). Spends happy and comfortable life with his wife and children and as a ruler of Madhya Pradesh, he lives for hundred years. 

Notes: If Mercury goes into an Angular house duly posited in the sign of Gemini or Virgo, it will cause Bhadra Yoga. Savant Mantreswara in Phala Deepika had made a special mention of this Yoga. It runs like this that a person who is born with this Yoga will be of sharp intellect, neat and honest living, praised by the learneds, of pleasant nature and is an eloquent public speaker, such native possesses imposing and kingly personality. 

The formation of Bhadra Yoga is restricted to Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius and Pisces Ascendants. In all these four Ascendants for giving rise to Bhadra Yoga, there would have maximum eight possibilities i.e. two in each Ascendant, on the basis of changing of sign. Therefore, prognostication should be based on the predominance of Mercury in these signs and houses. However, the other influences on Mercury should also be taken into consideration. It is pertinent to state that the full effect of Bhadra Yoga could only be obtained when Mercury is singularly posited in its own or exalted sign falling in the Angular houses and is also devoid of aspect from any planet and there should not be any kind of interference. This conviction becomes more true in the case of Sagittarius and Pisces Ascendants. In Gemini or Virgo Ascendant, if Mercury gets the conjunction of Venus, it may be construed as a compatible combination that too when Mercury is located in the Gemini sign and in which case this combination would be of Angular and Trinal houses and it will thus be backed by a Rajayoga. The conjunction of Venus with Mercury in Virgo will lower the effect of the Yoga on account of debilitation of Venus though Virgo is an exalted sign for Mercury and that would cause cancellation of debilitation. Nevertheless, it would affect the results of the Bhadra Yoga because it would certainly degrade the strength of Venus. The effect of connotation 'काग्यी' imbbed in these Shlokas is more pronounced in the event of the presence of Mercury and Venus in the Virgo sign because in this, Venus attains special overbearing effects and becomes a determining 

factor for a particular vocation.
