13-16: Features of Hamsa Yoga: The native of Hamsa Yoga will possess voice analogous to swan (hamsa), fair in complexion, charming face and well developed nose, phlegmatic nature, having honey like reddish brown coloured eyes, reddish nails and is an intelligent person. He is like a king with well moulded cheeks, round forehead and elegant feet. He possesses marks of fish (Matsya), iron hook (Ankusha), Bow (Dhanusha), Conch (Sankha) Lotus (Kamla) on his hands and feet. He will be of lascivious nature but his lustful urge of women will remain unsatisfied. He attains a height of 96 Angulas ( अनुलि ) and have interest in swimming and water sports. Being a contented ruler of the areas between Ganga and Yamuna, he after enjoying all comforts, leaves the world at the age of 100 years. 

Notes: Jupiter possessing the sign of Cancer, Sagittarius or Pisces, exalted and own signs, and posited in the Agular houses gives rise to Hamsa Yoga. Accordingly, this Yoga will be formed in Aries, Gemini, Cancer, Virgo, Libra, Sagittarius, Capricorn and Pisces Ascendants and the native, with the rest of the Ascendants, would be deprived of this Yoga. However, the results of these four Ascendants of movable signs and four of dual signs will be at variance. These divergent results would be on account of placement, conjunction and aspects of various planets on Jupiter. The results narrated by the Maharishi are obtained in a limited manner. In Aries Ascendant, the Jupiter will be in the 4th house which is a house of comforts and 

happiness and Jupiter is the Karaka of happiness. However, these results will only be felt if Jupiter gets support from a dignified planet giving happiness of that very level and if a planet of contrary nature is influencing Jupiter, this Yoga will render diminishing results. Here is an example, where such an effect has been vividly experienced. 

# Example : 98

Data : Date of birth, 8-6-1943; Time of birth 3-10 hrs. (IST); Latitude, $29^{\circ}$ -10' (N); Longitude, $75^{\circ}$ -44' (E) 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/c51a6a53a7c5822f53ad88d67139cf9902b554f7bdc400686a11de652d100ff5.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/a419f79b7fe066a861229583c74ff9090af4c9f31f353081430808a56b501d1d.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/ef0b8d4bcb1270cdc21e46f3804638f447f5e020b3ed8aeab7095ae4b48e0b7d.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/c7bc4c98b70925d6f58ab6163c5dc511ae0b4d55b6758d3581b9aa9cc1d5ddb1.jpg)


This birth chart demonstrates an excellent example of Hamsa Yoga. Here, lord of the 4th house (happiness) the Moon is dignified and Karaka of luxuries, Venus is also conjoining them. (Both Moon and Venus get directional strength in the 4th house). This native made tremendous gains through export business and also acquired sizeable property though he already had sufficient parental property. He is famous, fortunate and prosperous person. In this chart, the 2nd and the 7th lord Venus have a connection with the 12th lord Jupiter and that is why developed intimate relations with various young 

women. "Karaka Bhava" of 2nd house (speech), is Jupiter, Venus Karaka for the speech and Moon (humbleness) and all combined go to indicate that the native will be a soft spoken, a well composed person who would render imposing effect on an individual through his refined mannerism and captivating speech. This trait enabled the native to develop close relations with the young women in no time. The impact of his this force not only attracted the young women but also fairly grown up ladies fell for him. The underlying idea of narrating this effect is to show that the combination of the Moon and Venus with Jupiter, confers lustful nature as otherwise Hamsa Yoga would not have given rise to the nature of lascivious passion. The combination of the 7th and 9th lords in a nativity will indicate that after the marriage, the native will indulge with women other than his wife because 9th house represent the younger sister of the wife and the 7th house signifies the sex. Therefore, after wife, there would be a possibility of sexual relationship with the younger sister of the wife. 

In Aries Ascendant, the Yoga is more pronounced because libidinous planet Venus takes over the ownership of the 7th house. It is not intended to discuss Kala Sarpa Yoga but this Yoga is quite prominent in this birth chart because the Hamsa Yoga which delivered royal comforts and luxuriant enjoyments also destroyed its effects. This shows that malice Karakatwa (signification) of Kala Sarpa Yoga did come to play. 

The formation of Hamsa Yoga in Cancer Ascendant brings many ups and downs. No doubt, this Yoga gives rise to the fortune of the native and he is bestowed with opulent wealth and abundant property but Jupiter being the lord of the 6th house becomes cause for the disease and having posited in the Ascendant (body), the native will fall victim to some serious disease. Here, Jupiter's connection with any planet either through conjunction or aspect will land the native into litigation related to the house owned by these planets. This effect is quite evident in the horoscope discussed here after. 

# Example : 99

Data: Date of birth 18/19-7-1955; Time of birth, 4-50 hrs. (IST); Latitude, $28^{\circ}21'$ (N); Longitude $79^{\circ} - 23'$ (E) 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/9a8d838f915c74822215eea3f5f2e0b0b3feb6abd59ee59e9d13d91b934de5e7.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/4b3d096ad870a8ccac3886241835ae7ae61e6cb54d7810731a6269d48ecf6087.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/c0ad6000f1fa0a2a45577531088c5cb072c9ae5a0a06b3393512a4565e63213a.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/f3fd45782c5c9f29ab8f0110b79efb4964e443451ae06a577da149feef6925ea.jpg)


Jupiter is posited in the Ascendant thus gives rise to Hamsa Yoga. The native has been suitably benefited with this Yoga. Mars being the lord of the 10th house duly conjoined with the lord of the 2nd house (wealth) is placed with Jupiter in the Ascendant. On account of this combination, the native is a Sales Tax officer and amassed so much wealth that he could be counted as wealthiest in the strata of the middle class. One day, he was caught red handed while accepting the bribe. This incident gave such a rude shock that he became partially lunatic. Besides he was prosecuted by the Government and terminated from the service. The reason for all this malady is quite obvious. Mars is the lord of the 10th house which represents the Government. Jupiter being the 6th lord, depicts litigation and therefore, litigation with Government became evident and the Sun was the cause i.e. wealth which added fuel to the fire. 

In this horoscope, Saturn is aspecting Jupiter and other planets in the Ascendant. Although Jupiter does not aspect Saturn, the Jupiters' 

sign Sagittarius is under the aspect of Saturn and Saturn's sign Capricorn is aspected by Jupiter. Therefore, both these planets are asping the signs of each other and involving the 6th house (litigation) and the 7th house (wife). The native being under mental-tension, started harassing his wife who eventually deserted him and started living in her father's house. The father of the native's wife being a renowned lawyer got the native and his family entangled in a litigation and obtained divorce for his daughter. 

The idea of citing these cases is that the results of these Yogas should be delineated with due care and after a detailed analyses. Predicting results merely on the basis of the right planet at the right place as propounded by the Sage for these Yogas may land one into an embarrassing situation.

gagnbo nataiikatlaangfo n hfo naiitidoyfok: | 190 

Sahmukhun khrerod 

Mukki vikshvabuulr 111 

Maaalayo Maalalvaiay  ch d aati saiinukkum 

1991 

17-19: Features of Malavya Yoga : The native having the Malavya Yoga possesses beauteous lips, slim waist, splendour alike Moon, body emitting special fragrance, normal redish complexion, medium height, uniform and neat teeth, voiceressembling elephant's loud cry (trumpet i.e. deep thick voice), elongated arms, length of face 13 and breadth 10 Agulas (बागूल). After attaining the rulership of sindh and Malwa and experiencing comforts and happiness for 70 years, he departs from this world for heavenly abode. 

Notes: This Yoga is created when Venus duly conjoined with Taurus, Libra or Pisces sign is located in an Agular house. Malavya is a kind of Yoga which gets formed with every Ascendant. For delineating results of this yoga, certain precautions are necessarily to be observed. Some of these are appended as under : 

i) It is said that the lord of the 2nd and the 7th houses is posited in 2nd or 7th house becomes Markesha. This principle tends to cause some kind of doubt about the efficacy of this yoga in case of Aries and Scorpio Ascendants, because the Antardasa of Venus may inflict death and the native when having this thing in store, how can he enjoy material comforts? However, it is our humble submission that one should not rush through in declaring this result unless he has meticulously ascertained the longevity of the native. It is also necessary to know whether Venus is under the influence of any malefic planets or not. Even though Venus is being influenced by the malefic planets and if the native has a good longevity, this period will pass away merely causing some kind of physical distress which however, will not leave any telling effects. 

ii) Malavya Yoga in Aries Ascendant confers marriage or love with a woman, wealth, family comforts, prosperity, accretion of wealth, progress in the vocation of the children or business tours etc. While the Scorpio Ascendant no doubt, gives marriage or woman's love but it also causes heavy expenditure. The business tours are by and large, remain unsuccessful. The relations with the partner and relatives become estranged. Wife may contract some disease. Depletion of wealth may also be observed. 

iii) The Antardasa of Venus in Cancer Ascendant does cause sufferings but Venus being the lord of an Angular house, the effect gets neutralised and the results of the lord of the 11th house becomes more pronounced which according to Maharishi is of malefic nature. 

iv) The Antardasa of Venus in Libra Ascendant does cause some physical sufferings but of an ordinary nature. However, in Taurus Ascendant, the intensity of sufferings is more severe because with this Ascendant, Venus attains the lordship of the 1st house (body) and the 6th house (disease). However, good results relating to financial 

matters are not experienced in both these Ascendants. In Libra Ascendant, if Venus gets the association of the lords of auspicious houses, it may confer success specifically in the worldly matters but in Taurus Ascendant, with whatever house Venus establishes its connection, the native would get involved in litigation relating to the signification of that particular house. 

v) Malavya Yoga delineates specific results in Capricorn and Aquarius Ascendants provided Venus forms connections with the lords of auspicious houses. In these Ascendants, Venus becomes Yogakarakas being the lord of Angular and Trinal houses. 

vi) Formation of Malvya Yoga in Gemini and Virgo Ascendants, the signs of Mercury, its results are excellent. In this case if Venus has connection with the lord of any Angular houses through conjunction or aspect etc., the results would be par excellent because this relationship would be amongst the Yogakaraka planets. Malavya Yoga in Gemini Ascendant makes the native intelligent and he will have good earnings in instructional vocation (teaching, disseminating knowledge etc.) but in Virgo Ascendant, the native will get success in the business activities. 

vii) To get material happiness in Sagittarius and Pisces Ascendants by Malavya Yoga would be quite doubtful because Venus over here acquires ownership of auspicious houses. However, in Pisces Ascendant, when Venus forms certain particular connections with some lords of the houses, the results of some favourable events or attributes are observed. Here are two horoscopes to affirm these contentions. 

# Example : 100

Data: Date of birth 6-5-1934; Time of birth 11-05 p.m. (IST); Latitude, $30^{\circ}$ -19' (N); Longitude, $78^{\circ}$ -63' (E) 

In the instant horoscope, Venus is in Pisces sign and located in the 4th house hence Malavya Yoga. This Venus is also the lord

of the 6th house which signifies fights, litigations etc. thereby forming a combination of certain attainments through these traits. If the native makes his earnings in these vocations, it would be quite enterprising. Therefore, the native may pursue legal profession. Actually, he is a very famous, efficient and successful advocate. Jupiter which is a "Karaka Bhava" for speech is positioned in the 10th house free from any type of affliction. In natural horoscope, Venus owns the 2nd house which denotes speech, is the lord of the 6th house and in the sign of exaltation in the birth horoscope aspecting the "Karaka Bhava". These positional qualities of Venus in the nativity lended full support of making him a renowned advocate. The status and the educational level of a native could be identified by analysing the entitre qualities of the 5th, the 9th and 10th houses. Although this is a general principle but these houses and their lords are, in a way, very much inter-related in this horoscope and that is why the native could have good achievements in his life. 

Example:101 

Data: Date of birth 17-2-1964; Time of birth 9-35 hrs. (IST); Latitude, $28^{\circ} - 21'$ (N); Longitude, $79^{\circ} - 23'$ (N). 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/8b18ff50671a02c18ba9d86bbc33322ebe3dee8bed2d8cfbaa144e21070ba955.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/66a849bf60d9791d8682d29e758f67c02ad26467425f076cf10247a12d10cab5.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/e3ffc2e474ddd69bd9aeca455cc81c18d82cc86cefd1fd392fe1ba20d0b64589.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/b0513a65fbb245f5984be42fe1fde222ab7039c5f3413f6b61f2e4442dc73e5b.jpg)


In this birth chart, Venus being in the Pisces sign, is posited in the Ascendant. As such Venus is forming Malavya Yoga. In the Pisces nativity, Venus owns the 3rd and the 8th houses, hence becomes inauspicious. Every house may be auspicious and inauspicious does have some beneficial significations. Like, the 3rd house is connected with technical, sculpture, engineering type of education. In the same manner, the 8th house signifies minerals and materials found beneath the earth. Consequently, Venus would represent Mining Engineering. If this Venus creates any nexus with the house of education (5th house), the native will study these subjects and if Venus also forms connection with the house of vocation (10th house), he will pursue the same profession. 

In the instant horoscope, Venus does have linkage with both these houses (5th and 10th) as the lords of these houses being in 

conjunction with Venus are posited in the Ascendant. The native having passed his master of engineering in mining is employed in the same profession. Significator of intelligence Mercury is posited in the 11th house and aspecting the 5th house and Karaka of wisdom Jupiter being in its own sign as become powerful. The native remained a brilliant student and even after the completion of his education, he is still busy in academic and scholarly pursuits. 

Incidentally, it is necessary to clarify one fact over here, that is, if the Moon, Venus and Jupiter are conjoined with Mars, the native definitely gets selected in Indian Administrative Service (I.A.S.) because, in this format, Mars, the lord of the 9th house joins the 10th lord Jupiter. The native could not get success in I.A.S. even after three attempts because he did not get support from of the 9th lord Mars in his nativity.

20-22: Feature of Sasa Yoga : A person having Sasa Purusha Yoga will possess small teeth and soft faces brave but will not have small body. He will have narrow waist with weak loins, elegant thighs, intelligent and will have interests in forests, hills etc., powerful and be an army chief. Such natives do have protruding teeth to some extent, agile, will possess special knowledge in metallurgy, indulges in flirtation with women, plunderer of others wealth etc. This kind of native will have marks like garland (mala), Veena, Mridanga (percussion instrument) and weapons in his hands and feet. After having served the holy Earth and lived happily for 70 years, he departs for the heavenly abode. 

Notes : The position of Saturn in the Angular houses and in the sign of Capricorn, Aquarius or Libra will give rise to Sasa Yoga. The Yoga will not be formed in Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius and Pisces 

Ascendants because the required signs will not fall in the Angular houses with these Ascendants. This Yoga gives excellent results in Libra and Taurus Ascendants because here, Saturn will become Yogakaraka. 

One should delineate the results in Aries, Cancer, Leo, Scorpio Ascendants with great care, specially in the case of Cancer and Leo Ascendants an extra precaution is to be exercised because the position of Saturn will give the lordship of the 6th or the 8th house as well. In Scorpio Ascendant, due care is also required but with a deep study and proper insight, if the pit falls are located and kept in mind, one can avoid wrong and unmindful prognostications.

1-4: Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! Now I will narrate the results on the basis of Gunas (प्रधानी). If Satwa-guni (सन्दिकु़ि) planets are rising at the time of birth, the native will be Satwika (सन्दिकु़ि) and of sharp intellect. With the rise of Rajoguni planets at the time of birth, he will be Rajoguni (सन्दिकु़ि) and at the time of birth, if there is rise of Tamoguni planets, the person will be an idiot and of Tamsika (तमामिकु़ि) nature. With the predominance of the planets of all the 

Gunas at the time of birth, there will be a mixture of the qualities of all the three Gunas (प्रधान् रूध) in the native. 

O Vipra! a person born under these Gunas are classified as Uttama (बावप्त), Madhyama (सिकायम), Neecha (Adhama) (निर्त) and Udaseena (जोर्णीन). I am now narrating (the characteristics of) these Gunas as stated by Sage Narada (नार्तू़्ति).

The happiness or sorrows related to woman (wife) be considered from the Rekhas contained in the 7th house (sign) from the Venus duly multiplied by (total) Shodya Pinda etc. 

Notes : The status of the Ashtakavarga of Venus in the Model horoscope (Example-93) showing the reductions etc., istabulated hereafter: 

<table><tr><td>Planets</td><td>Ven Sat</td><td>Sun Mer</td><td>Rah</td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td>Ket</td><td></td><td></td><td>Mar Jup Mon</td></tr><tr><td>Signs</td><td>4</td><td>5</td><td>6</td><td>7</td><td>8</td><td>9</td><td>10</td><td>11</td><td>12</td><td>1</td><td>2</td><td>3</td></tr><tr><td>Number of Rekhas</td><td>4</td><td>4</td><td>3</td><td>6</td><td>4</td><td>2</td><td>3</td><td>6</td><td>4</td><td>7</td><td>5</td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td>Trikona Shodhana</td><td>0</td><td>2</td><td>0</td><td>2</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>2</td><td>0</td><td>5</td><td>2</td><td>0</td></tr><tr><td>Ekadhipatya Shodhana</td><td>0</td><td>2</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>2</td><td>0</td><td>5</td><td>0</td><td>0</td></tr></table>

The Rashi and Graha Pinda are calculated on the lines of Moon's Ashtakavarga and the final position so emerged is as follows: 

$$
\begin{array}{l l l} \text {R a s h i P i n d a} & = & 7 7 \\ \text {G r a h a P i n d a} & = & 2 0 \\ \text {S h o d y a P i n d a} & = & 9 7 \end{array}
$$

Venus is posited in the sign Cancer and the 7th sign therefrom is Capricorn which has 3 Rekhas and the product after multiplying these Rekhas with the total Shodya Pinda (3x97) comes to 291. The remainder after dividing this product by 27 is obtained as 21. The 21st Nakshatras from Aswini Nakshatra is Uttara Ashada and the trinal Nakshatras (10th and 19th) thereferom are Kritika and Uttar Phalguni. As and when Venus passes through these Nakshatras, the wife will get distressed. 

Now, divide the product (291) by 12 and we get remainder as 3. The 3rd sign from Aries is Gemini and the trinal signs therefrom is Libra and Aquarius. The transit of Venus over these sign will bring about distress to the wife of the native. 

19: The Uttam creature in Uttam limb, Madhya creature in the Madhya limb, the Udhaseena creature in Udhaseena limb and the Adhama creature in Adhama limb (of the Lord Kala) are created. 

UeTnMaang G 1 1 

20: The Uttam limb (of Lord Kala) is the head, Madhyama limb, the chest; Udhaseena limb, both the thighs and Adhama limb denote the feet. 

EeV gugavghaadeev kalmae: gajayat Jaiitmeekutu  tuee eaeaujayatoe  r 

21: Gunas govern various segments of 'Kala' (time-frames showing some pronounced tendencies of nature), which creates distinctions (or classifications) in the various forms of the living or the non-living (nature). 

Eev 1 1 

22: In this way, in line with its own nature of Chaturvida (composed of four elements;) lord 'Kala' has created this universe with four characteristics. 

Note : The Indian Yoga philosophy essentially teaches us to be the master of self as well as the elements of the process of elevation of soul to its final goal of sublimation with God. 

Nature has been conceptualised to be composed of three inseparable cardinal states of existence (qualities निका) namely, Sat (स्ति), Raja (रागा) and Tama (तमा). This trinity of cardinal states of nature is always in equilibrium and its composition determines the thoughts and actions. 

Even Vadic/Hindu God represent the same trinity. Lord Brahma (the creator) Vishnu (the protector) and Mahesh (the destroyer) symbolise the Raja, Sata and Tama Gunas governing nature. 

The three Gunas (प्रधिक) are contemporary and also inseparable roots of the powers (प्रधिक्य) of the nature (प्रधिक्य) which mutually interact and reach the equilibrium. As a matter of fact, the Sato Guna is the balancing power and owing to this very quality, this guna is known for its adjustment, joy and light (प्रधिक्य). Rajo Guna is the power (प्रधिक्य) of motion and denotes struggle, efforts, endeavour, enthusiasm and action. Tamo Guna is the power (प्रधिक्य) of inertia (lethargy) and stands for lack of consciousness and in its qualitative form it symbolises ignorance, incapability and lack of action. These Gunas form the foundation of the study of metaphysics and are generally used for self analysis as they are also active in the physical nature. Besides, this type of power is conferred upon every object existing in the nature. The mode of action and mobile form of physical nature, is the out come of the interaction of the qualitative powers. 

The equilibrium of the variety of the conscious and the unconscious in the world is protected by the active natural powers of these qualities (प्रति) though, it all depends upon the persisting flow of the supportive, obstructive or destructive contacts which are secured by them through the kinds of powers encampassing them. The man's own mental, spiritual or physical (bodily) nature is nothing but the synthesis of these creative powers and the fusion of these trinal balancing qualities. The man is surrounded by the environment, and its assimilation and interaction decides the nature of behaviour and reaction, to be adopted from these three types of qualities. 

Constantly being faced with the conditions (good or bad) without any efforts of self-defence or capacity to absorb or turning it favourable, if one bears it by remaining inert and powerless, such will be the symptom of Tamo Guna. In fact a lethargic person behaves in this manner only. Accordingly, the Tamo Guna manifests itself ignorance, disconcertment, in-capacity, imprudence, laziness, languour, lack of intelligence, mechanical behaviour, mental inertia, dullness, obscurity and dormant soul. The impact of this quality (Guna) breaks the very system of the nature and disbalances it. If a man alters the system, any other form of balancing power or new force of creative developments would not be able to replace it. 

On the other hand, there is Rajo Guna, and with its support, 

a man would be able to counter act these evil contacts, conditions or impacts which tend to take him towards the destructive course of Tamo Guna quite effectively. The planets representing Rajo Guna manifest the permission, encouragement and inspiration to endeavour, resist and master over the circumstances or situations and have it absorbed in oneself and establish a determined will to fight and win it over. The characteristics of Rajo Guna indeed, are eagerness, action and desire. The struggles, changes and new creations, victory and defeat, joy and grief, hopes and disappointments emanate from Rajo Guna. However, Rajo Guna obstructs the way of spirituality and happy life (happy life as perceived by the Indian sages). Its knowledge is incomplete as well as untrue. The excessiveness of Rajo Guna causes imprudent, forgetfulness, imbroglio, confusion, perpetual action maladjustment, agony on account of attachments, frustrated desires, unhappiness over losses and failures. 

Rajo Guna manifests strength of mobility, vibrant action, desire to work, power of creativity and efforts leading to success. However, Rajoguni (the one who is imbued with Rajo Gunas) is certainly touched with demonic tendencies. Although the presence of Rajo Guna is absolutely essential, yet this type of quality does make the man suffer from confusion, intoxication, pride, over ambition, cruelty, brute anger, extremism, selfishness, prejudice, deception, envy, jealousy and ungratefulness, savagary, greed and plundering etc. These qualities of the man are responsible in deforming the nature of the Earth. If one man is born with the exceedingly excessive qualities of Rajo Guna, the Earth has to endure the threat of a world war. The Indian masses have not forgotten the exceptionally exuberant quality of Rajo Guna having been possessed by one man of ancient period known as "Duryodhana" who was responsible in taking Kauravas and Pandavas into the great war of Mahabharta. The names of the great warriors of the old and present times viz., Ashoka, Taimur, Changez Khan, Napoleon and Hitler could as well be clubbed with the name of Duryodhana. 

Besides these two qualities (Gunas) of the native, there is yet another Guna saturated with the Divine light which is known as Satwa Guna or Satoguna. Satwa Guna represents that trait of nature (प्रधिक) which is imbued with light (प्रधिक्य) and equilibrium and is drawn 

towards the truth, knowledge, blissfulness, ecstasy, beauty, happiness, right mindedness, perfection, composure and steadiness. The characteristics of the Satwa guna are the pleasure of the bright affluence of knowledge, burning enthusiasm of compassion, spontaneous zeal, perfect minuteness and enlightened wisdom. Besides, restraining the power, hormonal and equalitarian existence are the other attributable factors of the Satwa Guna. 

Satwa Guna having endowed with the characteristics of purity is the foundation of keen knowledge (तिका) and light (प्रधान्या). The very strength of purity of Satwa Guna does not permit any disease, deformity or distress to have it set into the nature (प्रधान्या). When body receives the light of awakening, self learning and plentiful glitters of knowledge, it appears as if, the doors and the windows of a closed room have opened up towards the sun and the sunlight gushes into the room brightening it all over. When righteous qualities are awakened, intellect gets brightened, all the senses are sharpened, entire mind is enlightened and contented, life on getting calm and steady becomes glowing, satisfying and pleasurable, it would then be understood that Satwa Guna has since arisen and grown excessively. 

It is not correct to say that a native possesses only one of these Gunas (qualities) exclusively and is completely devoid of the other two qualities. In fact these Gunas have interchangable relationship and inter-flow of mutual influences which keep on joining and separating. There is always a struggle going on at the back of the mind of the native (conscious and unconscious mind) to win over other Gunas dormanted in the native i.e. one of these qualities will be more evident than the another in the native. In other words, all the natives do possess all these qualities but it varies widely in content. 

All the natives do possess Satwika tendencies in a greater or lesser measures. Consequently, it is seen that all of them have some light (enlightenment) different and distinct mode of tenderness and happiness or undeveloped attitudes. They also have some amount of hold over the quality of subtle adjustment with the circumstances, sympathetic nature, basic intellect, wisdom of judgement, right kind of thinking, fair amount of determination, weak 

emotions, true excitements and sense of adherence to the rules and regulations. 

Similarly, all the natives also possess the Rajo Gunas (qualities) and as a result of which a greater amount of zeal, desire, excitements, tendencies to struggle, dejection, falsehood, confusion and unbalanced joy and grief are evident from them. However, all the natives with this quality (Rajo Guna) do show eagerness to act or react, and undertake tough and adventurous pursuits at the face of lots of oppositions and pressures from all the comers. 

In the same way, every native has Tamsika tendencies as well, because, all the natives do have some elements of ignorance, moments of unconsciousness, poor tolerance, natural weaknesses and also have the feel of tiredness, laziness and mental lethargy. Besides, the ignorance, incapacity and grief, fear, subjugation by the circumstances, exertation of pressure by the other persons, events, powers etc., are generally faced by all the natives in some form or the other. 

All the natives in some directions of natural powers or in some corner of their mind or nature, are found to be Satwika. Similarly, they are Rajsika in some other direction or part of their mind and nature and they are, simultaneously, Tamiska in yet some other directions or portion of their mind and nature. 

A native is said to possess either Satwika, Rajsika or Tamsika nature depending upon the impregnation of the strongest Gunas (quality) amongst the three in respect of general nature, particular mind and mode of action of the native. However, a person holding exclusively one type of quality is a impossibility. No one possesses an absolute purity. Acknowledgeable person does not dominate an absolute knowledge. Similarly, a learned person always remains partially leamed. Infact, many bad, wicked or brute qualities remain dormant in him due to predominance of good and virtuous qualities. 

With the same analogy, a bad person is not a thoroughly bad person. Even the most stupid person does have some latent and underdeveloped capabilities. A timid person also has a facet of courage which can precisely be judged after proper analysis of the 

planetary and Dasas (Mahadasa, Antardasas etc.) position in the nativity. Besides, for a particular point of time, an astrologer can safely ascertain which of the quality (Guna) and of what measure is available and how long it will last, in the native, merely by observing and analysing his behaviour at that point of time. However, only those astrologers can acquire this analytical capability who is leading the life in a Satwika manner. When an astrologer himself is indulging in the activities contrary to spiritual discipline and virtous living, his nature and innerself could never gain this ability.

1-2: Maitraya said - O Maharishi! You have since explained the results on the basis of the time of the birth but how to find out the results of the one whose time of birth is unknown. Kindly describe if there exist any method through which auspicious and inauspicious results modelled by the past birth, could be ascertained. 

रसाथु पूधत वया विक्र ！लोकानुप्रधामान्ता | 

克喀哈拉里 莱巴 斯诺哈拉 费尔曼哈拉达兹纳蒙纳阿乌 | | 3 | |

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克拉夫·哈达克

44-49: The periods of 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) astride the setting of the semi-disc of the sun and like wise, 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) proceeding and 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) succeeding the Sun rise i.e. a span of 10 Ghatis (4 hrs.) each is termed as the twilight period (Sandhya) of evening and morning respectively. As such both these Sandhyas are constituted of 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) the 20 Ghatis period (4 hrs.) of the day (i.e. the gap between morning and evening Sandhyas) is called POORNA (पूपाद) and similarly, 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) of the night is named as MUGDHA (मुध्या). 10 Ghatis Sandhya of the sunrise and 10 Ghatis Sandhya of the sun set are called KHANDA (सनिका) and SUDHA (सनिका) respectively. 

If the birth occurs in Poorna or Mugdha, multiply the period (Ghatis) consumed at birth in the said portion by 2 and divide it by 15. The product be expressed in years, months etc. and reckoned as a constant factor. Multiply this factor by 1 to 9 in natural order, the Kala Dasa of the corresponding planet starting from Sun to Rahu, again in normal planetary order is achieved. In case the birth is in the span of Khanda or Sudha, the expired period upto the birth be multiplied by 4 and then divided by 15. This product also be used as a constant factor in calculating the Kala Dasa of the planets in the same manner. 

Notes : The technical terms used in these Shalokas are explained further for the proper comprehension of the readers. 

(i) Khanda (राग्या) : Day and night consist of 60 Ghatis (24 hrs). The period of 10 Ghatis i.e. 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) prior and 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) later to the Sun rise is called morning twilight (Sandhya). The Sandhya of the morning is called Khanda. 

(ii) Sudha (Ghatis): In the identical fashion 10 Ghatis of the evening i.e. 5 Ghatis (2 hrs.) astride sun set time constitute the evening twilight (Sandhya). 

(iii) Poorna (v): The period of 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) between both the Sandhyas i.e. Khanda to Sudha is known by the name Poorna. 

(iv) Mugdha $(\text{贡} \text{贡})$ : Similarly, the 20 Ghatis (8 hrs.) portion from Sudha to Khanda is termed as Mugdha. 

<table><tr><td>Mercury</td><td>=</td><td>Summer (Greeshama)</td></tr><tr><td>Moon</td><td>=</td><td>Spring (Vasanta)</td></tr><tr><td>Jupiter</td><td>=</td><td>Winter (Shishira)</td></tr><tr><td>Mars</td><td>=</td><td>Autumn (Sarad)</td></tr><tr><td>Venus</td><td>=</td><td>Rainy (Varsha)</td></tr><tr><td>Saturn</td><td>=</td><td>Winter (Hemanta)</td></tr></table>

<table><tr><td>मावर्वो ूलकागपुरवाकिकेपूरवाकान्तु परारधोँि
अनुपातित् िसियूर्षा ूलकागर्षाशमा डिहा ! || 12
लहर्षाशवाकिकेरालो ाो जन्सकागर्षाशमा डिहा : |</td></tr><tr><td>ल्रयप्रधान्तु एँरवाकान्तु रालो काथले देश्यया || 19</td></tr></table>

8-9: The first half part (5 Deg.) of the Drekkana will denote the first month of the season (There are six seasons and each season is of two months) and the second half part will be represented by the second month of the season. The Tithi (Rik) could be determined on the propotionate basis of the part of Drekkana Degrees (Amsas) of the Sun. The Istakala (Kur) denoted by the Sun's degrees will be the time of birth of the queriest. After calculating the degrees (Spasta) of the other planets and the houses, the predictions for the queriest should be (from the time of birth thus obtained) pronounced. 

Notes : The Hindu Astronomy has reckoned six seasons (Ritus) in a year. They are as follows : 

<table><tr><td>Seasons</td><td>Months</td></tr><tr><td>i) Vasanta (Spring)</td><td>Chaitra and Vaisaka (April and May)</td></tr><tr><td>ii) Greeshma (Summer)</td><td>Jyeshta and Ashada (June and July)</td></tr><tr><td>iii) Varsha (Rainy)</td><td>Shravana and Bhadra (August and Sept.)</td></tr><tr><td>iv) Sarad (Autumn)</td><td>Aswini and Kritika (October and November)</td></tr><tr><td>v) Hemanta (Winter)</td><td>Mrigasira and Pausha (December and January)</td></tr><tr><td>(vi) Shashira (Winter)</td><td>Magna and Phalguna (February and March)</td></tr></table>

If the first half of the Drekkana rising at the time of query, all the 30 days of a month will be represented by the first month of that season and should the latter part of the Drekkana be rising, it will indicate that the birth has taken place in the second month of 

the season. A Drekkana is of 10 degrees and half of the Drekkana will be of 5 degrees. The 30th part of this 5 degree of the Drekkana will constitute just 10 minutes. In other words, every 10 minutes of rising of half Drekkana will represent a 'day'. 

There are two Ayanas viz. Uttarayana. Three seasons each are covered by these two Ayanas. The seasons Shashira, Vasanta and Greeshma are in the fold of Uttarayana while, the rest of the three seasons namely Varsha, Sarad and Hemanta, will goto Dakshinyana. 

If the Ritu does not agree with the Ayana, the season of the alternative planet shall be taken as the season of the birth of the querist. The first, the Moon and Venus the second, Mercury and Mars and the third, Jupiter and Saturn are the three pairs of alternative planets.

10: Maitraya enquired - O Maharishi! Since Jupiter turns around to the same sign after 12 years, which of the cycle (of Jupiter) be considered the year of the birth.

11-12 : Maharishi Parasara said - O Virtuous Brahmin! In the event of doubt about the birth year, it should be judged from the approximate age of the querist at the time of query and the birthtime (year) be fixed by adding, 12 years for each Jupiter's cycle to arrive at the estimated number of years of the age and the year of the birth. Inspite of this, if any difference inthe age is indicated, then presuming the position of Jupiter in the trinal sign from the Ascendant (Prashna Lagna), the age of the querist is estimated and through which the time of birth on the basis of Lagna and Ritu, is fixed as per the procedure described earlier. 

# Jahalvata Maaat Suhuayyagkakalalshar an khyg bvei |

13: Maitreya contended, O Maharishi! After finding out the month, degrees (Amsas) of the Sun etc. as stated by you, how to ascertain the time of birth, kindly explain to me for the advantage of the world at large. 

Sakkaanotoreeepuayagauuloyesdih hiajssatam ！丨

14-16 : Maharishi Parasara said, O Virtuous Brahmin! On determining the sign, Amsa etc., of the Sun, the longitudinal (Amsas) details of the Sun be ascertained on the basis of number of days expired after the Sun's ingress into a sign identical with the consumed degree (Amsas) etc. at the sunrise. Thereafter, convert the difference between the Amsas (degrees etc.) of the sunrise and the Sun's Amsas (longitude) at the time of birth into Kala (तिका) and multiply it by 60. The product be then divided by the day's motion of the Sun. The resultant Ghatis, Palas etc. will denote "Ishta Kala" of the birth either before or after the sunrise. If the longitude of the Ishta Sun is less than the longitude of the sunrise of the Sun, that much before the sunrise, and if the longitude of the Ishta Sun is more than the longitude of the sunrise of the Sun, that much after the sunrise would be the Istakala of the birth (Time of birth). (This principle will be well understood once the readers go through the example coming up in the note portion of this chapter). 

Notes: In this chapter, Maharishi has expounded the principles of creating the birth chart of a person whose horoscope has either been destroyed or has not been made earlier and also that person does not remember the time of birth or year of birth etc. or, if time 

is known, but year is not known or vice-a-vara. The priciples could best be comprehend through an example. 

# Example : 102

Let us suppose, a querist whose birth details are not known, approached an Astrologer on 11-4-1991 at 11-40 hrs. The Ishta Kala of the sunrise was 14 Gh. 9 Pal. 40 Vipal. The longitude of the Sun was $11^{\circ} - 27^{\circ} - 7^{\circ} - 38^{\circ}$ and Ascendant $2^{\circ} - 26^{\circ} - 37^{\circ} - 56^{\circ}$ . The Ayanamsa was $23^{\circ} - 44^{\circ} - 22^{\circ}$ . The age of the querist was estimated to be between 18 and 22 years. The chart at the time of query is shown below: 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/ac2485695f67e2c346203a272ac2118286722775c58c16d0725c9e2764527d57.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/e0dddec96399251af52239a8a6fe010e38583c09d6a1a01a1b7a60d55ce01b3e.jpg)


The Aries is the Ascendant for the Dwadasamsa Chart. Therefore, Jupiter will be posited in the Aries sign at the time of birth, meaning thereby, when Jupiter was passing through Aries, the native had birth in that year. Jupiter remains in one sign for about one year. In the query chart, Jupiter is posited in the Cancer sign and Jupiter was transiting the Aries sign 4 years prior to it. If we take into account one full zodiacal cycle of Jupiter, the native would be around 16 years $(12 + 4)$ . Considering another cycle of Jupiter, the age of the native will go in the vicinity of 28 years but we have estimated the age between 18 and 22 years. Therefore, reckoning one cycle of Jupiter will take us nearer to the estimated age and we take off from this point. 

By subtracting 16 years from the year of query i.e. 1991, the rough year of birth comes to 1975. It is confirmed, from the Panchanga (Almanac or Ephemeris) that the transitory position of Jupiter during 1975 was in the Aries sign. It is important to note here that we should 

stick to the year of birth on the basis of the deductions and should there be some difference in the actual transiting position of Jupiter at the time of birth, the same could be over looked. 

Assessment of Ayana : The Ascendant at the time of query is $2^{s} - 26^{o} - 37^{r} - 56^{e}$ which is in the latter part of the Gemini sign and hence the birth was 'Dakshinayana'. 

Assessment of Ritu (season): The query Ascendant is in the Drekkana of Saturn which denotes Shishira Ritu. This Ritu falls between 20 January to 19 March when the Sun transits Capricorn and Aquarius sign. Here we have seen that the compatibility between Ayana and Ritu does not exist. Therefore, we need to take shelter of the special rule embodied in Shloka 7 and according to which the Ritu connected with Jupiter should be adopted. Jupiter denotes Hemanta Ritu which takes place between 20 November and 19 January corresponding to lunar months Mrigshira and Pausha. 

Assessment of the month: The longitude of the query Ascendant is $2^{\circ} - 26^{\circ} - 37^{\circ} - 56^{\circ}$ which is the latter part of the 3rd Drekkana which indicates the 2nd month of the pair of month. Therefore, Pausa lunar month (from 19 December to 19 January) would be the month of the birth. 

Assessment of the Amsas of the Sun : The third Drekkana of the query Ascendant is $1^{\circ}$ -37'-56" ahead of the 1st half of this part of Drekkana (after $25^{\circ}$ ) i.e. $1^{\circ}$ -38' (say). Therefore, the expired position in the degrees (Amsas) etc. is 1 Amsa and 38 Kalas $(60 + 38) = 98$ Kalas. The 5 Amsas of the latter part of the Drekkana will be 300 Kalas. $(5^{\circ} \times 60)$ . The latter half of the Drekkana is equal to 30 Amsas (degrees) of the Sun (i.e. month) and thus we can find out the expired Amsas of the Sun by applying rule of proportion. 

$$
\begin{array}{r l r} 3 0 \text {A m s a s} & = & 3 0 0 \text {K a l a s} \\ 9 8 \text {A m s a} & = & \frac {3 0 \times 9 8}{3 0 0} = \frac {9 8}{1 0} \\ & = & 9 \text {A m s a s a n d} 4 8 \text {K a l a s} (9 ^ {\circ} - 4 8 ^ {\prime}) \end{array}
$$

The Sun between 19 December and 19 January remains in the Sagittarius sign. Therefore, the longitude of the Sun at the time of birth will be $8^{\circ} - 9^{\circ} - 48^{\circ} - 00^{\circ}$ . 

Assessment of time of birth : From the longitude of the Sun, it has become evident that the native was born 9 days after the solar ingress inthe Sagittarius sign. Therefore, we need to find out the longitude of the Sun on the sunrise of the 10th day. The longitude of the Sun at the time of sunrise on the 25th December, 1975 was $8^{\circ} - 9^{\circ} - 11'$ and, day's motion of the Sun was $1^{\circ} - 1^{\prime}(61^{\prime})$ . These data are less than the above Sun's longitude. Therefore, the native was born after the sunrise. On the basis of these details, we now proceed to fix the time of birth of the native. 

Subtract the longitude of the sunrise from the reckoned longitude of the Sun. 

<table><tr><td>Longitude of the Sun :</td><td>8°</td><td>9°</td><td>48&#x27;</td></tr><tr><td>Less longitude of sunrise (-)</td><td>8</td><td>9</td><td>11</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>0</td><td>0</td><td>37</td></tr></table>

The daily motion of the Sun is 61' and there are 60 Ghates in a day. Therefore, the expired Amsas (degrees) after the sunrise will be. 

$$
\frac {3 7 \times 6 0}{6 1} = \begin{array}{l l} 3 6 & \text {G h . 2 3 P a l . , 3 6 V i p a l} \\ 3 6 & \text {G h a n d 2 4 P a l . (s a y)} \end{array}
$$

Therefore, the native was born 36 Gh. 24 Pal after the sunrise. 

The birth time could also be worked out in terms of hours and minutes by taking 24 hrs in place of 60 Ghatis for the day. It will come to : 

$$
\frac {3 7 \times 2 4}{6 1} = \frac {8 8 8}{6 1} = 1 4 \text {h r s .} 3 3 \text {m t s .} 2 5 \sec .
$$

Alternatively, divide Ghatis etc. by 2.5 or 5 to get the position of hrs. etc. 

$$
\begin{array}{l} (3 6 - 2 4) \quad + \frac {5}{2} = \frac {(3 6 - 2 4) \times 2}{5} \\ = \frac {7 2 - 4 8}{5} = 1 4 \text {h r s .} 3 4 \text {m t s .} \\ \end{array}
$$

This expired hrs. after the sunrise should be added to the 

time of sunrise on 25th December 1975 which was 7 hrs. 16 mts. and we will arrive at the birth time of the native. 

<table><tr><td></td><td>hrs.</td><td>mts.</td></tr><tr><td>Time of sunrise</td><td>7</td><td>16</td></tr><tr><td>Add expired time</td><td>14</td><td>34</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>21</td><td>50</td></tr></table>

Therefore, the time of birth of the native was 9 hrs. 50 mts. at night on the 25th December 1975. Based on this birth time, the birth chart of the native could be erected and analysed for the predictions. 

# Jy prayjyayoyogay: | |9

# Chapter-81

# YOGAS CAUSING ASCETICISM

Aey 1 1 

預約約定の効zeitを提供予

1: Maharishi Parasara said - O Brahmin! Now I will expound the Yogas which will cause asceticism (Pravrajya) and during which the persons recluse himself from his home and adopt some other religious tenets (discipline). 

Chuurayravirnirneerree: 

रेयँ रिकल्तानी नयकाली ेरेयँ रिकल्तानोति || ू || 

EeKdavad  yatiiehckksharre nirnirn: kma 

Jyayy aaiyafifikcysay  sabaloeuy yuayyayiyi | 13 | 

2-3: If one house contains four planets duly strong, it will give rise to a Yoga which will cause asceticism (abandonment of 

temporal concerns). Amongst these planets, if the Sun is strong, the native will be a Tapasvi (लाक्ली), a contemplative ascetic; should the Moon be strong, the person becomes a Kapali (कपाली) - a man with begger's bowl; if Mars being strong, the person will adorn red clothes; with Mercury being strong, he will possess a staff (रवम्या); incase Jupiter gets stronger, he will be a Yati (योति) - and ascetic (Pravrajya) abstinent and severe in self-discipline; should Venus be strong, he will be the possessor of Chakra (धिकामारी); in case Saturn be strong, he becomes Naga (नागा) - the one who moves about in nude forms. In the event of many planets come out to be strong, the native will embrace asceticism on the basis of the strongest planet amongst them. 

Notes: Infact the inclination towards asceticism is nothing but the mental make up of a person or a particular state of mind. For the fructification of Pravrajya Yoga, the 4th house becomes more relavent. The 4th house and its lord, the Moon that signifies the mind and if malefic planets like Saturn or the Sun or the lord of the 12th house (मोशा विर्ति) have the overbearing influence in the nativity, the Yoga of asceticism (सन्दागा) gets fructified. Some learned Astrologers may not agree for the involvement of the 12th house and the lord of the 12th house and this disagreement may due to the fact that the 12th house signifies bed comforts (sexual connections), but this is a traditional interpretation and which is not derived etymologically. If we understand this disposition in a broad sense, it stands to mean as Yoga (मोया) for combining, meeting, making pair or for that matter, mating. Our ancient have also described this house as निकाया विर्ति - final emancipation. 

Maharish Parasara's contention of having four or more planets in one house is confined to the fact that they should be within the space (orb) of one degree. If they are apart by more than one degree in one sign, it will not form Pravrajya Yoga. 

B 

4: A weak Pravrajya Yoga : The planets forming the Yoga 

causing asceticism may be strong but if they are combust, the native will not turn (take initiation of) ascetic even though he may develop deep adoration in the religious tenets. 

Araaansranaananae ananae aannnnae ananae 1 

5: If the powerful Pravrajya causing planets are weak and also get combust, the Sun becomes the signicator for the Pravrajya Yoga and such native tum ascetic (तु़क्ति) of the type denoted by the Sun. 

Janaaahnnae aee 5yae e 2: 1 

6: Other Pravrajya Yogas : The dispenser of the Moon if receives no aspect from any planet but, in turn, aspects Saturn, the native get initiated (रिति) into religious tenets signified by the planet who is stronger between the two of them. 

Ninberlato JnnaMnBnogAorBovt KevvLoeNnAiknOgokalat: I

7: If the dispositor of the Moon (lord of the sign where the Moon is posited) is weak and is only aspected by Saturn, the native will embrace the religious tenets (precept) duly signified by Saturn. 

y 

8: If the Moon be in the Drekkana of Saturn and also in the Navamsa of Saturn or Mars and aspected by Saturn, the native will be an ascetic of the type signified by Saturn.

9: Should there be planetary war between Mars etc. planets and if Venus happens to be in the North or in the South, it become victorious. The conjunction of the other planets within one degree causes planetary war and the planet only in the north -i.e. having higher celestial longitude is reckoned as stronger and becomes victorious.

# Chapter-82

# FEMALE HOROSCOPY

藥藥藻 藻藥藻 造藥對 藻藥對 藻藥對 藻藥對

tanaa aannnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa aannnnnnaa 

1: Maitreya enquired - O Maharishi! You have narrated various kinds of results connected with the native. Kindly now explain as to how to ascertain the results for the female native. 

saguy yuot  luya vipr 1  y 

sraa aee: 1 

Veeepaee  e  e: 

Laganeeepa: 

5 1 

1 1 

2-4: Maharishi Parasara replied - O Vipra! You have posed a very good question. I will explain the same as well. What all results relating to the male horoscopy which have been narrated so far, be also considered for the female horoscopy. However, what are 

special to the female horoscopy, the same are briefly, being described over here. The attributes related to her Deha (physical traits, body etc.) should be reckoned from the 1st house (Ascendant), the birth from the 5th house, husband from the 7th house and widowhood be ascertained from the 8th house. Those results which are not possible to occur for a female, the same be stated for her husband. 

Notes: The fortune (रिस्यान) of the husband and widowhood of a woman should be analysed from the 7th and 8th houses respectively from her nativity. This is what, Maharishi has indicated in the above Shlokas. The 7th house denotes the husband. This house is reckoned as the Ascendant for the husband. Besides, the Ascendant not only signifies the body as expounded in these Shlokas but also stands for general happiness, sorrows, knowledge (prudence), place of birth, fame, strength, glory, honour, kingship, politeness, longevity, solace, general appearance, prestige, behaviour, sound health, vocation, childhood, adulthood, old age etc. The learned Astrologers have also reckoned the other Karkatwas of the Ascendant as to forsake things (रिस्यान), character of the native, shortcomings, various Avasthas (dignity of planets) etc. 

Therefore, from the 7th house in the woman's nativity (taken as the Ascendant for her husband), it is quite appropriate to analyse the characteristics of the husband of the native. Positioning of benefic planets in the 7th house, makes the woman as most fortunate. 

In the birth chart of a woman, if the dignified Sun is posited in the 7th house, and it (the Sun) does not form any kind of connections with the malefic planets like Rahu, Satum etc., such female native gets married in a high class family. 

In Chapter-1 of the Shloka 14 of Phaldeepika, it is stated that the Sobhagya ( शिकाया )of the woman (husband's fortune), whether he is alive or not, should be delineated from the 8th house. Further, in accordance to the Shlokas 5 and 6 of Jataka Parijata (Chapter XVI), the longevity of the husband of a woman should be judged from the 8th house only. Therefore, to analyse the widowhood of a female from the 8th house is rather more appropriate. This is the 

only place where the position of Mars causes obstacles in the happiness and-comforts of the husband. Placement of Mars in the 8th house denotes that the native is Manglika (रमानी). The 8th place from the 7th house is the 2nd house. This 2nd house is considered as the house of longevity or death of the husband. If the 8th house of a female holds two or more than two malefic planets, the 2nd house of such nativity will have the aspect of these malefic planets and that will indicate an adverse situation for the longevity of the husband of the native. In the 28th and 47th Shlokas of this very Chapter have stated that the position of Mars in the 8th house makes the native responsible for the husband's death. Here are two illustrations to affirm this contention. 

# Example : 103

Data : Date of birth, 3-3-1968; Time of birth, 18-06 hrs. (IST); Latitude $25^{\circ}$ -34'N; Longitude $84^{\circ}$ -01'E. 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/0c2bf4c341765a64cb0caab1db27b2c0279841e45c7348493710aaacc81e1841.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/8ca84315587f6ff1ea1f3b28729bca4f7a06bbd4e161483754ae3689884609c6.jpg)


The husband of this native died due to throat disease after one and half years of the marriage. In the birth chart, Rahu, Saturn and Mars are posited in the 8th house forming a direct aspect over the 2nd house. This 2nd house is owned by Mercury. The signs belonging to Mercury always react swiftly. Therefore, in a short time after the marriage, this tragedy took place. The placement of malefic planets in the 8th house are therefore, not welcomed especially in context with the happenings of the life. 

# Example : 104

Data: Date of birth, 19-11-1951; Time of birth, 13-20 hrs (IST); Latitude, $27^{\circ}$ -13' (N); Longitude $77^{\circ}$ -29' (E). 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/769c968b77f854d8a2ec2c64350f0591024b05cf4d15439124e9d10f2e039236.jpg)

Sun</td><td></td><td>Sat
Mar
Ven</td></tr></table>

In the instant horoscope, the 8th house is harbouring Saturn, Mars and Venus of which first two are first rated malefic planets. In the 2nd house, Jupiter is posited in its own sign. The lord of the house of longevity of the house, Karaka of the husband and the Bhava Karaka (2nd house) all the three factors related to the husband, are being represented by Jupiter. The 2nd house is also badly afflicted. Therefore, the husband died after 7 years of the marriage. Besides, this woman never experienced the lascivious comforts of the marriage. The reason being that firstly, the Karaka for such traits of life is Venus which is in the sign of debilitation and is also badly afflicted through the conjunction of two malefic planets and secondly, the lord of the spouse house (7th) Sun is receiving a malefic aspect of Saturn. Therefore, her husband, most of the time in this space of 7 years, remained sick and the obvious reason was the aspect of the Saturn over the Sun. This maleficence became prominent due to the fact that Saturn is the lord of the 12th house which is 6th (house of disease) from the 7th (house of husband) and when ever any connection between the Ascendant (body) and the 6th house is formed, the body contracts some disease. Both these horoscopes demonstrate the ill effects of the positioning of the malefic planets in the 8th house which resulted in the shortening the life of the husbands. 

# Laganeenu Lhamsa: 5a Naar Prukutilikvulat

5-5½: If the sign of the Ascendant and the sign in which the Moon is posited, are even signs, that woman will be conjoined with the feminine characteristics and will have the feminine qualities. She will be submissive and possessed with auspicious attributes. Should there be aspects of the benefic planets, she will be exceedingly beautiful and will have sound health and bodily comforts. 

Notes : The characteristics of even and odd signs are of different types. Even is also known as Somaya which is synonymous to calmness, serenity, silence, tranquillity, auspiciousness, beautiful, attractive etc. In other words, these qualities are more compatible with the characteristics of the Moon. Probably, with the evolution of the Earth, the word Somaya would have been coined as an expression for the Moon. Therefore, both the even (रेम्य)sign and the Moon are connected with attraction, feelings, tenderness etc. As a matter of fact, the word Somaya is also akin to Mercury who is considered to be the son of the Moon. Therefore, lovely and attractive features should also be studied from Mercury. 

Venus exclusively is the Karaka for beauty. It is said in the Deva Keralam "प्रधान रूधिका वर्षयस्थल" that is, in a female nativity if Venus is posited in an even sign and the Ascendant is neither occupied or aspected by the malefic signs, that woman will possess calm, composed and quite nature and will also be adomed with delicate womanish qualities. If the sign of the Moon also happens to be of an even sign and is bereft of malefic aspect and also occupied by the malefic planets, such female qualities will get more pronounced. 

Sage Mantreshwara in his treatise has affirmed this aspect in Shloka which runs as under : 

"Jyokmnyo 10 gnnn 

2015年全国春季学业考试卷1

It means that if a woman's horoscope have both Ascendant 

and the Moon in the even signs and are aspected by the benefic planets that woman will have a good son and husband and imbued with calmness and adorn plenty of ornaments. 

Sage Varaha Mihira has stated in his Brihat Jataka some thing like this : 

"gurwannnnaaahnnnne nnnnne 

s 

It states that if the Ascendant and the Moon both are in the even signs, that woman will bear feminine nature. In other words, such women will have glowing lustre and will possess submissive, blushing and innocent looks. If these Ascendants are aspected by the auspicious planets, that woman will have decent behaviour and adorn elegant clothes and ornaments. 

Saravali contains the following Shloka in this context: 

"Kahsikshayat kahsaneo: 

藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥

It says that if the Ascendant and the Moon be in the even sign in a woman's nativity, that woman will be faithful to her husband, beautiful and will possess a pleasant nature. If these Ascendants are aspected by the benefic planets, such woman will have long beautiful hair and imbued with many qualities. 

An idol of beauty and carved in accordance to the descriptions expounded by the sages, for the body and behaviour of a woman, an appropriate horoscope of a woman is illustrated over here. 

Example : 105 

Data : Date of birth, 17-2-1964; Time of birth, 20-59 hrs

IST); Latitude, $23^{\circ} - 09^{\prime}(\mathsf{N})$ ; Longitude $79^{\circ} - 57^{\prime}(\mathsf{E})$ 

In this horoscope, the Ascendant is of Virgo sign while the Moon is in the Pisces sign (Ascendant of the Moon) and hence both are of even signs. The Karaka of beauty Venus is also in the sign of exaltation and that too is in an even sign. Again, Venus owns the 2nd house in the nativity which signifies the 'face' and as such it gains further importance. The Ascendant is receiving the direct aspects from the most auspicious planets, Jupiter, Venus and the Moon. Besides, the Ascendant is owned by a benefic planet Mercury. No doubt, the Ascendant is also being aspected (8th aspect) by Mars, but with so many other benefic factors in favour, this malefic aspect will not have much bearing over the auspiciousness. 

The features of this woman are very much akin to these exceptionally auspicious combinations, like she is fair in complexion, possesses a well proportionate body. She is soft spoken, polite, well behaved and adorns a pleasant smile. Her face is imbued with freshness and possessed with the quality of understanding. She is having a most comfortable married life.

6-7: If the Ascendant and the Moon are in odd signs, that woman will possess manly attributes (both in shape and bearings) and will have bad temperament. If these (the Ascendant and the Moon) are being influenced by the malefic planets, she will be bereft of good qualities. If they receive a mixed (auspicious and inauspicious) influences, the mixed results be predicted. Amongst the Ascendant and the Moon whosoever is the stronger, its results will be pronounced. 

Notes : In this Shlokas, Maharishi's revelations are contrary to what has been divulged in the earlier Shlokas. It states that if the Ascendant and the sign in which Moon is posited in a female nativity are the odd signs, that woman will possess manly features and behaviour. The word Vishama (विषयम odd) over here stands for (कन्ला diminish or 

less) or $\text{रेय़}$ (dust). Here both stand for the similar meaning. Infact, the quality of dust $\text{रेय़}$ over here symbolizes the power of virility, efforts and exertion. The odd $\text{(विकाम)}$ signs are also called as signs of energy or power (बूड़ विकामी). These signs bear great power of virility and masculine vigour or strength. Therefore, manly traits to be signified by the odd signs, are but natural. 

When the Ascendant and the Moon (significator of mind and portrays nature of a person) bear the odd signs in the birth chart of a woman, such woman will bound to acquire masculine nature and will also reflect manly features from her face and body. If the strong Sun, Mars and Jupiter influence the Ascendant and the Moon in the female horoscope, that native will be a valorous woman like Rani Jhansi, Joan of Arc etc. However, if in addition to these planets, other malefic planets also cast their influence over the Ascendant and the Moon, that woman will be rude and uncivilised. All other Astrological treatises (Jataka Shaestras) also give the similar account. Phaladeepika states : 

A

It means that when the Ascendant and the Moon both are in the odd signs and are aspected by or conjoined with malefic planets and the female possessing such dispositions in her birth chart will be of a fraudulent mind, haughty and behaves with her husband in, furious and impolite manner. She will have manly traits, will not remain under the control and will face poverty. 

Saravali also speaks in the tone of Phaldeepika and the matching renderings are as follows : 

yuyuanyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuy 

v 

It says that if, in the horoscope of a female, the Ascendant and the Moon are in the odd signs, that woman will have the manly appearance, and behaviour and will possess maleficent nature and undergo miseries. If the odd sign of the Ascendant and the sign housing the Moon are conjoined with or aspected by the malefic planets, she will be sinful, wretched and bereft of good qualities. 

According to Brihat Jataka :

It states that if odd signs herbour the Ascendant and the Moon in the female nativity, she will possess masculine features and behaviour. Should the Ascendant and the Moon receive malefic aspects and conjunction, she will perform sinful act and devoid of good qualities. If the Ascendant in the even and the Moon in the odd signs or if it is in other way round, she will posses the combination of the mixed qualities.
