9-16: The stronger between the Ascendant and the Moon (the lord of the Ascendant and the sign of the Moon) if is posited in the signs of Mars and in the Trimsamsa of Mars of a woman nativity, that woman will have sexual union with a male before her marriage. If it is in the Trimsamsa of Venus, she will be an adulterous woman even after her marriage. Should it be in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, that woman will be involved in conjuration and incantation (occultism). If it being in the Trimsamsa of Jupiter, she will be a saintly and virtuous woman. In case, it is in the Trimsamsa of Saturn, she will be a maid servant or a mistress. 

If the Ascendant or the Moon as the case may be, is posited in the signs of Mercury and had gone in the Trimsamsa of Mars, she will be a cunning and guileful woman. If in the Venus Trimsamsa, she will be of lascivious character; if in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, a virtuous woman; in case, it is in the Trimsamsa of Saturn, she will be hermaphrodite and if in Jupiter's Trimsamsa, she will be a chaste woman. 

If the Ascendant or the Moon as the case may be, is posited in the signs of Venus and gets located in the Trimsamsa of Mars, the female native will be of a wicked nature; if in its own Trimsamsa (Venus), she will become popular on account of her good qualities; if in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, she will distinguish herself in various kinds of arts (having an artistic flair); should it be in the Trimsamsa of Jupiter, she will be full of qualities and in case of Saturn's Trimsamsa, she will marry for the second time. 

If the Moon being in the sign of Cancer (own sign) in the nativity and get located in the Trimsamsa of Mars, she will be of an independent nature; if it is in the Trimsamsa of Venus, she will be characterless; in Mercury's Trimsamsa, she be an expert in sculpture art; in the Trimsamsa of Jupiter, she will be endowed with many qualities and should it be in the Trimamsa of Saturn, she will attain widowhood. 

If the Ascendant or the Moon as the case may be, is posited in the Leo sign in the birth chart and is situated in the Trimsamsa of Mars, she will be of a talkative nature; if in Venus's Trimsamsa, she will immolate herself (Sati-ṣṭādī) if in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, 

she will behave like a man (possessing manly qualities and nature); if in the Jupiter's Trimsamsa, she will be a queen and if in the Trimsamsa of Saturn, she will go astray (or go against) from her family's traditions and customs. 

If the Ascendant or the Moon as the case may be, is placed in the signs of Jupiter in the nativity and then has gone in the signs of Mars in the Trimsamsa, she will possess many qualities; if in the Trimsamsa of Venus, she will be deviating from a good conduct; if in Mercury's Trimsamsa, she will be acquainted with sciences; in the Trimsamsa of Jupiter, she will be conjoined with many qualities and if it is in the Trimsamsa of Saturn, she will be averse to an intense sexual relationship. 

If the Ascendant or the Moon as the case may be posited in the signs of Saturn in a female horoscopy, and get placed in the Trimsamsa of signs of Mars, that woman will be a maidservant; if in the Trimsamsa of Venus, she will be a learned; if it being in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, she will be of a low moral values; in case of the Trimsamsa of Jupiter, she will be devoted to her husband and should it be in the Trimsamsa of Saturn, she will be involved in the adultery with the low caste persons. 

Notes: In these Shlokas, Maharishi expounded the theory of the qualities depicted by the Trimsamsa Varga of the nativity. From these renderings and also other such scriptures, it is revealed that the Trimsamsa in the female horoscopy portrays the characteristic of good and bad qualities connected with the women. In order to comprehend these priciples, it is, first of all, required to establish as to who between the Ascendant and the Moon (Ascendant) is the stronger one in a particular nativity and these principles will only apply on that Ascendant (i.e. the stronger one). Secondly, it is necessary to ascertain the sign of the stronger Ascendant and where exactly this sign is placed in the Timsamsa Varga and the prognostications are made accordingly. 

If the stronger Ascendant is in the sign of Aries and Scorpio, barring the Trainsamsa of the Jupiter, the results will not be of a good quality. This is what Maharishi has revealed. The reason being, 

if it is in the Timsamsa of Mars, the woman enjoys the sexual union even before her marriage. The reason for this is quite obvious because Mars is also the lord of the Ascendant and owns the Trimsamsa of the Ascendant which is imbued with the qualities of independent nature and to act in ones own will. It also gives rise to passion and to control over the passion is rather difficult, especially by the one who possesses such a nature. Phatdeepika, and Saravali used the word 'sul' (sinful) and in Jataka Parijata called it as suuwarai (ill tempered) for such combinations. These connotations lend full support and confirm the statement of Brihat Jataka. 

With the Trimsamsa of Venus, the woman is also stated to be of ill tempered but in this situation she maintains sexual union with the other persons even after the marriage. Venus is the significator of the marriage. This kind of disposition does form some kind of connection between Mars and Venus. While Mars denotes passion, Venus attributes sex. This combination tends the native to cross the limits of wedlock, customs and sensitivity of the precious relations. 

If it is in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, she will pursue occultism. This is also stated by the savants Varaha Mehira, Kalyana Varma, and Mantreswara apart from Maharishi Parasara. Such women are the great believer of occultism (प्रधान्यति) and incantation. Even though they acquire good education and have modren out look, this particular quality will be deep seated in them. Jataka Parijata contains "प्रधान्यति" instead of "प्रधान्यति" which connotes that in the event of Mercury's Trimsamsa, the woman will be of dirty nature. It is also experienced that in such a situation, the woman will be lethargic and lives in an apathetic state. 

In case of Jupiter's Trimsamsa, the intensity of the passion of Mars is checked by Jupiter. As such the woman of such nativity will be sober and moderate. The Trimsamsa of Saturn will give rise to subservientence to the native because the nature of Saturn (service) influences Mars to give such results. 

The Ascendant or the Moon (Ascendant) who soever is stronger, if is in the sign of Mercury and in the Trimamsa of Mars in a female horoscopy, she will be a cunning woman, according to Maharishi Parasara. 

Here an important principle takes birth that the combination of the Ascendant and the Trimsamsa takes priority because Maharishi has stated earlier that if the Ascendant being Mars falls in the Trimsamsa of Mercury then only the woman will believe in occultism. Here, if the Ascendant that of Mercury is in the Trimsamsa of Mars, the native will be a cunning women. 

In the event of Venus trimsamsa, she will be hard working type. Saravali also states some thing like this "प्रधान रैगण्ति---" which means that she will believe in working hard thereby lending full support to Maharishi. Varaha Mihira in Brihat Jataka expounded the same aphorism while Jataka Parijata gives a different version. According to which, if there be the Ascendant or Moon (Ascendant) of Mercury and Mercury be in the Trinsamsa of Venus, she will adorn beautiful clothes and ornaments and also be the keeper of many cows and female buffaloes besides being wealthy and reputed. The concerned Shloka runs as follows: 

# “”

The version of Phaleepika is all together different which states such woman will be an important woman. As a matter of fact, the results depicted by Phaldeepika and Jataka Parijata appear to be more relevant in this context. The basis of this statement, in addition to the experience, are some technical reasons which are as follows: 

(i) Mercury signifies handicraft especially work of minute nature such as making ornaments etc. 

(ii) Venus, on the other hand, is fond of ornaments and finances are also controlled by it 

(iii) Mercury is considered as hermaphrodite planet 

(iv) In a natural horoscope, Venus owns the 2nd house which denotes apart from other things, mouth and due to this fact, the eatables like food, milk products etc., also being signified by it 

(v) Here, Mercury being a hermaphrodite planet and Venus being a planet of passion and lust, it will not give less indulgence in sex because this combination carries conflict within itself. It is here, Maharishi has stated, that if there 

be Trimsamsa of Saturn, the nativewill be hermaphrodite which is quite true because both the planets involved in creating this situation in the Vargas are hermaphrodite planets. 

In the Mercury's Ascendant and also in the Trimsamsa of Mercury in the birth chart of a female, she will possess many qualities. Infact, these qualities are related to the sculptural trait. Such woman is also good in house holds. She would be expert in all kinds of works and carries out her duties most diligently. 

In the event of Ascendant or Moon, whatever is the case, is in the sign of Venus and in the Trimsamsa of Mars, she will be an unchaste woman. This expression is in line with the Shloka-9 ibid. 

Should it be in the Trimsamsa of Venus, she will possess great qualities. Infact, such a woman will be highly civilised, having a sweet voice and loves music. Her work will have an artistic touch. She organises herself well and keeps a perfect equilibrium in every sphere of her work. According to Phaldeepika, such female is a reputed woman. In this context, Saravali has this much to say: "रिको रागल्यु़ ात्तम् निकी अधिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका वस्थिका 

# “藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥藥

It implies that with such a combination, she will be the wife of a learned person(πιν) , will be good looking and reputed. 

In the Trimamsa of Mercury, she will be expert in arts. These renderings are rather quite appropriate. However, here some kind of distinction could be drawn between the two signs of the Venus viz., Taurus Libra. If the Ascendant being Taurus and Trimsamsa of Mercury, it will give subtle and intellegent touch to her art and even the finer details would not escape her notice. In case of Libra Ascendant and Mercury is the Trimsamsa, her art will be infused with a special quality and her reputation will spread beyond the frontiers of the country. 

In the Jupiter's Trimsamsa, the woman will be possessed with many qualities. This is also contended in Brihat Jataka and 

Saravali but Jataka Parijata states : 

# "zaiy juyi yuyuoyuyuy

That is, such a woman is like an epithet of Lord Indra, loyal to her husband, bestowed with children and saturated with many good qualities. 

The Trimsamsa of Saturn will cause remarriage for the native. If such Saturn is the lord of the 7th house or posited therein, the remarriage may take place at an old age. 

If the sign of the Moon is in the Ascendant and be in Trimsamsa of Mars, she will be of an independent nature. Infact, this state of nature is confined upto the age of adolescent. Thereafter, the mental excitements and anger will come up in a great measure. 

In the event of Venus Trimsamsa, she will be an unchaste woman and her conduct will be contrary to her family's traditions. It should also be termed as characterless. This contention of Maharishi will probably, in the state of fall of the Moon and Venus because with the loss of strength of the Moon, the level of the native goes down (in respect of family, society and education) and in the grip of her intense feelings, the Karkatwa of Venus takes over and its related attributes bring diminuation in her behaviour, character etc. If both the planets are strong, the expression contained in Phaladeepika in this respect, be kept in mind, which states that in the Cancer Ascendant and Venus Trimsamsa, she will possess a good conduct. 

In the Trimsamsa of Mercury and Jupiter, the results will take place in according to the said priciple. With regard to Satums' Trimsamsa, she will attain widowhood. This effect is noticed primarily in those horoscopes in which there is an exchange between the Moon and Saturn. 

If the Ascendant being of Leo sign with the Trimamsa of Mars, the native will be talkative and loquacious as stated by Maharishi. However, Phaladeepika contended that with such a combination, she will act as wicked wife. According to Brihat Jataka, she will be of manly nature. Jataka Parijata has this to say: 

# "nto gurqgurqur qur

That is, such a woman will have qualities befitting to a man and unchaste in character. However, the contention of Saravali falls 

in line with Maharishi. 

Infact, woman with this combination would be of aggressive nature, loud mouthed, commanding voice and of a dominating kind. The other results may be read in this context. 

If the Ascendant is in the sign of Jupiter and Trimsamsa of Mars, she will have many qualities. If the 5th house of the nativity of such woman is well fortified, she will undertake research work on some topic. She will be commended for her work at least once in her life time and her reputation will be acclaimed by the world. The results of the Trimsamsas of Mercury, Jupiter and Saturn will take effect in the same manner. However, in the Trimsamsa of Venus, bad character is stated. This possibility will be in the event of Venus being in the sign of debilitation. 

When the Ascendant is in the sign of Saturn or the Moon is also in the Saturn's sign and in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, the stated results that such woman is cruel and will be involved in adultery with the low caste persons, needs to be analysed alongwith other expositions on the subject which would be useful. 

According to Jataka Parjata; 

“ 

which means that such woman is omniscient: and her sharp intellect will have many facets but those will be of unchate nature. 

Phaldeepika, Brihat Jataka and Saravali used the connotation of characterless, sinful, evil respectively. It may, therefore, be seen that except Jataka Parijata, the point of views of all others are identical. It has also been observed through experience that such a woman undertakes deceitful and cunning works for the financial gains. This is also possible, in the event of planets lending support to such an act.

17-21: If the 7th house is devoid of any planet and also without any benefic aspect, she will have a coward husband. If the sign of the 7th house being a moveable sign, the husband will be out of the house for most of the times. If Saturn or Mercury is posited in the 7th house, the husband of the native will be impotent. In the event of the Sun getting posited in the 7th house, she will be deserted by her husband. With the position of Mars in the 7th house, that woman will become widow at childhood or she dies immediately after the marriage. If Saturn is posited in the 7th house duly aspected by the malefic planets, she will remain unmarried right up to her old age. If the 7th house contains malefic planets, she will attain widowhood and if the planets being benefic she will devoted to her husband who will live long. Should there be planets of mixed category, she may remarry and will have mixed results (about her married life). 

If the Moon duly conjoined with Venus is posited in the Ascendant or in the signs of Venus or Saturn, such woman will certainly be a mistress alongwith her mother.

22-25: If the sign of the 7th house and also the Navamsa, be of Mars, her husband will flirt with other women and will be an angryman. If it be in the sign and the Navamsa of Mercury, her husband will be intelligent, skilled and a well composed person. If it be in the sign and Navamsa of Jupiter, her husband will be virtuous and will be able to exercise control over his sensing organs. If it be in the sign and Navamsa of Venus, her husband will be fortunate, handsome and loved by the women. If the 7th house be in the sign and Navamsa of Saturn, her husband will be old (much older than his wife) and stupid. If it be in the sign and Navamsa of the Sun, the husband will be a stone hearted and hard working type. If it be in the sign and Navamsa of the Moon, her husband will be handsome, libidinous and delicate. If there be different signs and different Navamsas of the 7th house, the result will also be mixed one and it should be so stated.

# Yogas of barrenness :

Knnaa a naiit sna nae uutne paauguyue 11 

34: If the Moon and Venus are posited in the Ascendant and get conjoined with Saturn or Rahu and the 5th house is occupied or aspected by the malefic in the female nativity, that 

woman will be barren. 

Notes : The Chaukhambha edition has also included the method of propitiation which is as follows : 

"kekknannr knnknnnnae 

Meaning thereby that one begets a child by performing RurdraAbhisheka and singing (of sacred hymns in devotion to Lord Vishnu. 

When the Moon and Venus duly conjoined with Saturn or Mars are posited in the Ascendant, they will certainly get deeply afflicted. The out come of the affliction of Venus in a female birth chart would be that the productive parts of the woman would get greatly diseased. Some kind of disorder in the generating organs will set in. Added to it, if the Moon is also in a state of distress, the effect will be in a greater degree. 

Besides, formation of any adverse Yoga or malefic aspects over the 5th house in female nativity would mean a straight assault on the capability of giving birth to a child because, this house signifies wombs which is directly connected with the progeniture and such a woman is considered as barren (रविक्या). Therefore, such disposition of the planets will naturally get affected with Bandhya Yoga (combinations of barrenness). In support of this aphorism of causing Bandhya Yoga of Maharishi, two practical examples are illustrated over here : 

Example : 106 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/bc42fd8f34c606d1808e1e87b5d12a3c2b6b173229f2bb770f2d5c1c3fbcc77f.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/07285f615c019795d92344f65f71afe0f46507791c9c6d50aee8b786872a7cc0.jpg)


Data : Date of birth, 10.4.1921; Time of birth 5-40 hrs (IST); 

Latitude, $26^{\circ} - 27^{\prime}(\mathsf{N})$ ; Longitude, $79^{\circ} - 02^{\prime}(\mathsf{E})$ 

In the instant horoscope of Aries Ascendant, the Moon, Venus duly conjoined with Mars are posited therein. Further, the 5th house is occupied by Saturn. As such, all the conditions of the preceding aphorism are getting satisfied. This woman could never bear a child. Besides, the occupation of the 7th house by Rahu, Karaka of offspring Jupiter is having association with Saturn (retrograde), the Sun the lord of the 5th house conjoining with Mercury lord of the 6th house (disease) and getting posited in the 12th house (Dushtha Sthana), are such important and nagative factors that lend active support in causing barrenness conclusively. 

# Example : 107

Data: Date of birth, 2.10.1902; Time of birth 6-30 hrs (IST); Latitude, $26^{\circ} - 55^{\prime}(\mathbf{N})$ ; Longitude, $75^{\circ} - 49^{\prime}(\mathbf{N})$ . 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/200192fd13450ca96721fbf2a0873873bf5fe1313018ea73abaa6148e063ee58.jpg)


![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/356bf4204ae55acb7559c6ac489c88eef1e9ce2f3e554dc58b5dafacabce0df2.jpg)


This horoscope of a female of Virgo Ascendant holds Venus and the Mcon in it and receiving a malefic aspect from Saturn which is also the lord of the 6th house (disease). The 5th house is under the direct aspect of Mars and the Karaka for progeny (Jupiter) is also getting engulfed by this malefic aspect. Besides, Jupiter is weak being in the sign of debilitation and this has also weaken the 7th house owned by Jupiter. In this nativity, the Moon is totally weak. This woman, accordingly, was never blessed with a child. 

In the Shloka in question, there is close involvement of the Ascendant. When ever any discease giving planet gets affected in the Ascendant, the effect of such affliction causes great miseries. This is a distinctive principle which has great bearing in delivering the adverse results and is a very important angle of the whole gamut. 

# Fortunate and unfortunate Yogas :

# kuuagauoosotlalgatse 50e-2e nearoy kugunnna

35: If the 7th house be in the Navamsa of Mars and is aspected by the Saturn in the female horoscopy, that woman will be unfortunate (she will possess diseased vagina). In the 7th house, if there is Navamsa of an auspicious planet, such woman will be fortunate and is loved by her husband. 

# Yoga for the attainment of happiness in father's house :

# y

36: If the Ascendant contains the sign of Mercury, and it is occupied by the Moon and Venus in the female nativity, she beholds abundant comforts of her father's house. 

Notes : The very fact that there are auspicious planets in the Ascendant explains itself that the native had a very comfortable childhood. The Shloka reveals that if the Ascendant be of Mercury's sign which harbours the Moon and Venus in it in a female nativity, such woman enjoys abundant comforts of her parents house (before marriage). The signs of the Mercury represent adolescent stage of the native. The Moon and Venus denote childhood and comforts respectively. Besides, on account of Mercury possessing special qualities, Maharishi has given preference to Mercury in the maxim. 

# Multibeneficial Yoga :

Lagne 1 

Jaiyee  वर्षातिलतसम्यना ूर्षानिकल्तुर्षारानिन्वति | | ूरूू | | 

37: If the Mocn, Mercury and Venus are posited in the Ascendant in the horoscope of a woman, she is endowed with many qualities. If Jupiter happens to be in the Ascendant, she will be bestowed with great satisfaction, children, wealth and happiness. 

Notes : This Shloka is very important because in this, Mars and Satum are also implicated in causing affliction to the Ascendant. In a nativity of Aries Ascendant, the lord and the Bhava Karaka of the 4th house is the Moon. As it is stated earlier that the sensibility of motherhood is infused in the Moon. Here, the position of Venus in the Ascendant is as a lord of the 7th house. In the event of progeniture, this house plays an important role. Therefore, placement of the Moon and Venus in the Ascendant becomes exceedingly important in connection with the motherhood. 

Secondly, in the Capricorn Ascendant, the Moon and Venus acquire due importance related to progeny because in such a nativity, they own the 5th and the 7th houses respectively. It could be stated that amongst the four Ascendants of Mars and Saturn, Aries and Capricorn are the only two Ascendants in which if the Moon and Venus get afflicted, they will become responsible for the denial of progeny. The other two Ascendants are of different level. Between them, Scorpio Ascendant is again important than the Aquarious Ascendant in which Venus get the ownership of 7th house and the Moon takes over the 9th house. Incidentally, the 9th house is also connected with the progeny. 

In Aquarious Ascendant, the lord of the 9th house is Venus and the same planet own the 2nd house as well. Therefore, Venus has an edge over the Moon in this case. In fact the conjunction of the Moon and Venus in the Ascendant will produce an opposite effect because the Moon being the lord of the 6th house will acquire malice prepense through disease. Therefore, the positionof the Moon in the Ascendant with Venus will not only be harmful for the Venus but also for the Ascendant. 

The Yoga of barrenness (4) is also formed in other Ascendants 

but the combination of Venus and the Moon in these Ascendants is not that important. The other planets also perform the similar function but it is also true that these planets are not capable of producing such damaging affects as in the Ascendants of Mars and Saturn while harbouring the Moon and Venus. The following planets will be the source of causing barrenness in different Ascendants : 

# Sign of Ascendant

# Planets of Barrenness

1. Taurus Mercury, Mars 

2. Gemini Venus, Jupiter 

3. Cancer Mars, Saturn 

4. Leo Jupiter, Saturn 

5. Virgo Satum, Jupiter 

6. Libra Saturn, Mars 

7. Sagittarious Mars, Mercury 

8. Pisces Moon, Mercury. 

In these Ascendants, the lord of the 5th and the 7th houses are taken as the basis. Besides, the lord of the 9th house and Jupiter also play an important role because, as stated earlier that 9th house is also, in a way, connected with the progeny and Jupiter is the significator (Karaka) for the progeny. Here are a few horoscopes to demonstrate the effect of the combinations of the barrenness. 

# Example:108

Data: Date of birth, 17-5-1939; Time of birth 4-30 hrs (IST); Latitude $29^{\circ}$ -27'(N); Longitude $77^{\circ}$ -19'(N). 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/70264c6b-03db-420c-873f-bdaced8dc4d5/84360bac79856535479b2c459aad1104ffe5939a10a56493d5f19f8fa73e0607.jpg)

In the horoscope of Aries Ascendant, Venus, the Moon, Saturn, Ketu are posited therin and are afflicted through the aspect of Mars from the 10th house. Besides, the planet which is greatly malefic in the Aries Ascendant is Mercury and it is also occupying the Ascendant. Therefore, the Moon and Venus are deeply afflicted. Further, here Mercury is the lord of the 6th house which has also aggravated the situation of barrenness. The aspect of Mars over the 5th house (progeny) has also diminished the chances of motherhood. Owing to these adverse factors, this woman inspite of performing so many propitiatory measures and undertaking series of treatments, remained barren.

Mercury being the lord of 8th and the 11th house will be considered as a malefic planet over here. The Karaka of progey Jupiter is in the sign of debilitation. Therefore, this woman could not be blessed with a child in her entire life due to heavy affliction on the Moon and Venus and with so many other adverse factors. All her attempts to bear a child turned futile and she remained barren.

kartari Yoga. Such native will face an untimely death. One should not fix any Muhurta for the marriage, with such an afflicted Ascendant.

Maharishi Parasara replied -O Brahmin! In the manner the results of the characteristics of the parts of the woman's body propounded by Lord Shiva to Goddess Parvati, I will unfold the same before you. Please listen.

45-47: A woman whose palm shows clear lines which are pink in colour, circular, smooth, complete (without cuts) and deep, such woman enjoys all comforts. If the lines of the palm indicating marks like a fish, she will have good luck and Sawbhaya (will have pleasure of the love of her husband). If the lines showing marks like a swaslika, that woman will be wealthy; if like lotus, she becomes wife of a king. If palm contains a sign of whirl traversing from right to left, she will be the queen of the king of the kings. If a woman's palm possesses sign (mark) of couch, umbrella, tortoise, she will be the mother of the king.

73-75: If the weak Moon is placed in the 6th, the 8th or the 12th house, it will cause fear of evil spirits, tiger, other ferocious animals; loss of house and agricultural fields. Fear of theft during journey, deep injury and other serious diseases will also get erupted. 

If the Moon be the lord of the 2nd on the 7th house, premature death may cause. Donation of white cow and buffalo for Brahmins will mitigate the ill effects and bestow sound health and happiness with the blessings of the Moon. 

Notes: Results of the Moon's Antardasa in the Mahadasa of the Rahu have also been enunciated by savant Manteshwar, in Shloka-37 chapter-21 of Phaldeepika. 

Vnunnnnaa: knnnne  nannanae

dniy i dya aaii t  eevniv 

Meaning thereby that in the Mahadasa of Rahu and the Antardasa of the Moon, there will be loss of wife; quarrel with the people, mental distress; misfortune befall on friends; danger from water; loss of crops, wealth, animals and children. 

The underlying idea of citing this stanza from the other classical works on Astrology is, that one should analyse the effects of this Antardasa with great caution, because this sub-period does not pass through smoothly and without giving any trouble. 

the value (Zodiacal factor) of such planet. The sum total of these products (multiplied figures) is called Pinda. 

Multiply signs Taurus and Leo by 10, Gemini, Scorpio by 8, Libra and Aries by 7, Capricorn and Virgo by 5 and the rest of the signs viz., Cancer, Sagittarius, Aquarius and Pisces be multiplied by 4,9,11 and 12 respectively. (These are the value of the signs or the multipliers of the signs). 

Now, I will explain the values of the planets. Jupiter has 10, Mars-8, Venus-7, Mercury-5 and the rest of the planets viz., the Sun, the Moon and Saturn have identical value as 5. These are the multipliers $(\pi_{\mathrm{Jup}})$ for the planets.

73-74: If a woman has evenly round noise with small nostrils, it is considered propitious. In the event of nose being thick in its front portion and middle part is flat, it is not admirable. 

If the front portion of the nose is reddish in colour or shrunken, it indicates widowhood. If nose being flat, she will work as maid and if nose happens to be too small or too long, she will be 

quarrelsome.

75-77: If the eyes of a woman are reddish in its corners with black pupils, eye balls are white like cow's milk, longish and smooth eye lashes, it will be auspicious. 

A woman with raised eyes will have short life and with round eyes will indicate loose morals. Honey kind reddish - brown colour eyes denote good-luck and happiness. A woman being blind from her left eye will have bad conduct and being blind from her right eye will be a barren woman. A woman with pigeon like eyes will be of a vicious and sinful character and if a woman has eyes analogous to elephant eyes, she will be unfortunate.

84-85: Soft (silky), black, thin and long hair brings auspiciousness. Hairs of reddish-brown colour, hard, dry, scanty and short are inauspicious. 

However, a woman possessing golden-yellow coloured (honey) or black coloured hairs is propitious.

1-3: Maitreya said - Hey Maharishi! you have since enlightened me with the results severely for women and men. Hey Vedagye (पिकार) (scholar of Vedic scriptures) I am yet inquisitive to hear more from you. O Sage! I learnt from the Shaestras that the soul of a childless person haunts after his death. O Lord! Kindly explain - what sin leads to childlessness? How an astrologer will delineate its effects from the Ascendant and what propitiatory penance a childless person should undertake to have a child? 

Sahayu Puotc Luya Vipr! Kzoyato h Tuya Muyu   
yohomayu Hpuoetn kivoeon kuyu 

4: Sage Parasara replied - Hey Vipreya, you have posed a good question. I will certainly reiterate the facts as described by Lord Shiva when beseeched by Goddess Parvati.

6: Lord Shiva replied - Hey Devi! you have asked a very good question. Now I will describe the combinations for the loss of children along with its propitiatory measures.

8: If the Sun, Mars, Rahu and Saturn duly strong are posited in the 5th house and Jupiter Karaka for children is weak, it will give rise to Yoga for childlessness. 

# Yogas of childlessness due to the curse of the Snake (Naga Raja):

9-16: If Rahu is posited in the 5th house and aspected by Mars or similar combination is formed in Aries or Scorpio sign, it will cause childlessness due to the curse of the 'Snake' (नाग). 

When lord of the 5th house conjoins with Rahu and Saturn forms conjunction with or aspects the Moon in the 5th house, it will cause loss of children attributed to the curse of the 'Snake'. 

Should Jupiter conjoin Rahu, lord of the 5th house is weak, lord of the Ascendant is conjunct with Mars, there will be loss of children owing to the curse of the 'Snake'. 

Conjunction of Jupiter and Mars, Rahu's position in the Ascendant, the 5th lord being in a Trik house (6th, 8th, 12th) will also give effect to the childlessness. 

If 5th lord Mercury is in the Navamsa of Mars and conjoined 

with Mars, the ascendant contains Rahu and Mandi, the curse of 'Snake' will destroy the children (progeny). 

If the 5th house is of the sign of Mars and the 5th lord is in conjunction with Rahu and is also under the aspect or conjunction of Mercury, it will denote loss of children as a result of the curse of the 'Snake'. 

When the 5th house contains the Sun, Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Mercury, Jupiter and the 5th and the Ascendant lords are weak, the childlessness will be ascribed to the curse of the 'Snake'. 

Conjunction of the lord of the Ascendant with Rahu and the lord of the 5th house with Mars or Jupiter with Rahu, will also effect the destruction of children attributed to the curse of the 'Snake'. 

# Propitiatory measures :

17-19: After ascertaining childlessness on the basis of planetary combination's enunciated in the previous Shlokas and to propitiate the curse, Naga Raja be gratified through worship. An idol of Naga Raja (नागा निका) be made in gold and have it glorified as per the traditions of the family and shall be worshipped by the scholars and cow, land, sesamum, gold etc. be denoted in charity as could be afforded by the family. By performing these rites, with the blessing of Naga Raja, there will be prolongation of the family. 

Yogas of childlessness due to the curse of the forefathers (पिकर) :

flowers or clothes; will undertake pious deeds, construction of pond, cow shed etc., will have festivity of marriage, happiness from wife and children, co-operation from the parents, blessings of Goddess Laxami (wealth), completion of ambitious tasks with the blessings of the king etc. If it is full Moon, the result will be total and in case of weak Moon, diminishing results will be experienced.

74-76: Debilitated, in inimical sign and placed in the 6th or the 8th house from the lord of the Mahadasa, the Moon in its Antardasa will produce death, distress to wife and son, loss of land, destruction of animals and agricultural produce, danger from the thieves and war (encounter). If the same Moon is the lord of the 2nd or the 12th house, it will cause early death, danger to body, mental distress etc. Recitation of Mantras (Japa) of Goddess Durga, Laxami, giving away white cow, buffalo in charity will ward off the evil effects. 

deprivation of children. 

A weak lord of the Ascendant is posited in the 5th house, the lord of the 5th house is in conjunction with the Sun and malefic planets are posited in the Ascendant and the 5th house will attribute childlessness consequential to the curse of the forefathers. 

If there is an exchange between the lords of the 10th and the 5th houses and malefic planets are posited in the Ascendant and the 5th house, there will be no issue following the curse of the forefathers. 

Mars being the lord of the 10th house conjoins with the lord of the 5th house and there are malefic planets posited in the Ascendant, the 5th, the 10th houses an annihilation of the children will take effect. 

If the lord of the 10th house is positioned in the Trik house (6th, 8th, 12th), Jupiter being in the inauspicious sign, the lords of the Ascendant and the 5th house are associated with the malefic planets, the curse of the forefathers will destroy the children. 

If the Sun, Mars, Saturn are posited in the Ascendant and Rahu and Jupiter are in the 8th and the 12th houses, the curse of the forefathers destroys the children. 

When the Sun is located in the 8th house, Saturn in the 5th house, the lord of the 5th house is conjunct with Rahu and malefic planet is posited in the Ascendant, there will be loss of children. 

If the lord of the 12th house is posited in the Ascendant, 8th lord be in the 5th house and the 10th lord is placed in the 8th house, it will cause childlessness. 

The 6th lord be in the 5th house, the lord of the 10th house is posited in the 6th house and the Jupiter being in conjunction with Rahu, there will be loss of children due to the curse of the forefathers.

31-33: To get set free from these afflictions, Shrada at Gaya be performed and unlimited or one thousand Brahmins be fed. Besides, Kanya Dan (to undertake the marriage of a girl) and donate cow in charity be also done. 

By performing these propitious deeds, one certainly gets released from the curse of the forefathers and his family will get perpetuated with children and grand children. 

Therefore, it is desirable of the learned Astrologers to delineate the results on the basis of planetary combination (Yoga) in the nativity.

34-50: The Moon being lord of the 5th house, in debilitation and hemmed by the malefic planets and the 4th and 5th houses also contain malefic planets, will cause destruction of children due to the curse of the mother. 

The position of Saturn in the 11th house, the 4th house containing a malefic planet and the Moon be in the sign of debilitation will destroy the children. 

If the lord of the 5th house is posited in a Trik house (6th, 8th, 12th) duly debilitated and the Moon be in conjunction with the malefic planet, there will be loss of children attributed to the curse of the mother.

75: Sookshmadasa of the SUN in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS: Weary heart, wavering mind, wandering, sometimes profit and some time loss (period of uncertainty) etc. 

Aarayegy dghnssmnyi: kayelalao gatangati: 

Bukudivahavibu: 

76: Sookshmadasa of the MOON in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS : Sound health, accretion of wealth and property, gain sales and purchases, advancement in education and intelligence (mental faculty) etc. 

77: Sookshmadasa of MARS in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS : Sluggishness, differences with the enemies, abandoning the country, great fear, eruption of disease and sorrow. 

78: Sookshmadasa of RAHU in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS : Danger from the king, fire and snake, loss of co-borns, severe conflicts, dislodgement from place or position (demotion) great fear etc. 

79: Sookshmadasa of JUPITER in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS: Gains from all activities, increase in wealth and crops, copious gains from the business etc. 

80 : Sookshmadasa of SATURN in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS : Affliction by the enemies, great anguish, destruction of animals, tremendous loss due to the death of an elderly person in the family etc. 

81: Sookshmadasa of MERCURY in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS : Increase of property amongst the brothers (co-boms), accretion of wealth from business, pleasure of wife and children. 

82: Sookshmadasa of KETU in the Pratyantardasa of VENUS: Fire incident, deep distress, disease affecting face/mouth, eyes and forehead, loss of amassed wealth etc. 

Notes: As it is apparent from the very name of 'Sookshmadasa' that it has a 'little' duration. In order to pinpoint the occurrence in the 

Virgo-Amsa-pleasure of wife, in the Dasa of Libra Amsa-ministership, in the Dasa of Scorpio Amsa-apprehension of death, in the Dasa of Sagittarius Amsa-acquisition of wealth. 

O-Brahmin! These are the results of the nine parts of Ariesthat is, 'Navamsa'. Similar results be reckoned in one's own Dasas.

# 4-5½: Results of the Dasas of Taurus Amsa:

In the Dasa of Capricorn Amsa - inclination towards sinful acts, in the Dasa of Aquarius Amsa - gain of wealth in business, in the Dasa of Pisces - accomplishment of all tasks, in the Dasa of Scorpio - danger from fire, in the Dasa of Libra-eminence from the king, in the Dasa of Virgo-danger from the enenies, in the Dasa of Cancer Amsa - trouble from wife, in the Dasa of Leo - eye trouble, in the Dasa of Gemini-danger from poison. These are the results of the Dasas of Taurus Amsa.

51-61: Now I will narrate the Yogas arising out of the curse of the brother and on knowing the same, the learned Astrologers should make efforts to protect the children from the curse. 

If the lord of the 3rd house is posited in the 5th house duly conjunct with Mars and Rahu, the lords of the 5th house and the Ascendant are in the 8th house, there will be loss of children due to the curse of the brother. 

If Mars and Saturn are respectively posited in the Ascendant and the 5th house and the lord of the 3rd house is located in the 9th house and Karaka for the brother gets posited in the 8th house, it will cause destruction of the children attributed to the curse of the brother. 

If Jupiter duly debilitated is posited in the 3rd house and Saturn goes to the 5th house and the Moon and Mars are posited in the 8th house, there will be loss of children ascribed to the curse of the brother. 

When the lord of the Ascendant be in the 12th house and Mars gets into the 5th house and the lord of the 5th house duly conjunct with a malefic planet is posited in the 8th house, then children will be destroyed owing to the curse of the brother. 

If the Ascendant and the 5th house are hemmed between the malefic planets and the lords of the Ascendant and the 5th house are posited in the Trik houses (6th, 8th, 12th), the brother's curse will

168-169 : Maharishi Parasara said : O Brahmin! Now I divulge Sthira type of Dasa. The movable signs are ascribed 7 years, fixed signs 8 years and dual signs 9 years of Dasas. Since the Dasa years are of fixed nature, this system is called Sthira Dasa system. The sequence of twelve sign Dasas commences from Brahma-Graha Sign. The order of counting for odd signs is forward and for even signs is backward.

65-68: If Mercury, Jupiter, Mars and Rahu are posited in the 5th house and the Ascendant contains Saturn, it will cause deprivation of children due to the curse of the maternal uncle. 

Should the lords of the Ascendant and the 5th house duly conjunct with Mercury, Mars, Saturn are posited in the 5th house, the childlessness will be attributed to the curse of the maternal uncle. 

When the lord of the 5th house on combusion gets posited in the Ascendant, Saturn be in the 7th house and lord of the Ascendant conjoins Mercury, even then there will be loss of children. 

Positioning of the lord of the 4th house duly conjunct with the lord of the 12th house in the Ascendant and the Moon, Mercury, Mars are posited in the 5th house will indicate the destruction of the children.

69-70: In order to get released from the curse of the maternal uncle, the idol of Lord Vishnu be installed and water pond, well and embankment be constructed. These measures will help accretion of children and property. The scholars should alleviate the curse in this manner.

71-78: The one getting proud and overbearing of ones power, if insults the Brahmins, this affliction (रेथप) or the curse of the Brahmins destroys the children (The kind of pride could be due to excessive property, physical strength, status, wealth etc.) 

If Rahu is posited in the sign of Jupiter, the 5th house contains Jupiter, Mars and Saturn and the lord of the 9th house is posited in the 8th house, the children get destroyed due to the curse of the Brahmins. 

When the 9th lord is in the 5th house, the lord of the 5th house be in the 8th house and gets conjunct with or aspected by Jupiter, Mars, Rahu, it will attribute childlessness due the curse of the Brahmins. 

Should the lord of the 9th house get debilitated, the lord of the 12th house is posited in the 5th house and either gets conjunct or aspected by Rahu, it will cause loss of children as a result of the curse of the Brahmins. 

If Jupiter being debilitated, Rahu is situated in the Ascendant or in the 5th house and the lord of the 5th house is in a Trik house (6th, 8th, 12th), it will also denote destruction of children following the curse from the Brahmins. 

If Jupiter being the lord of the 5th house and posited in the 8th house duly conjunct with the malefic planet or the lord of the 5th house alongwith the Sun and the Moon is in the 8th house, there will be loss of children due to the curse of the Brahmins. 

When Jupiter being in the Navamsa of Saturn and Saturn conjoins Mars and the lord of the 5th house is posited in the 12th house, the Brahmins' curse destroys the children. 

Should the Ascendant contain's Jupiter and Saturn and Rahu being in the 9th house or Rahu joins Jupiter in the 12th house, even then it will cause deprivation of children.

79-81: In order to alleviate this affliction (रेथाम), the native should observe Chandrayana fast and three fasts to do penance against the sinful act against the Brahmins and charity of a cow with donation (रेथामा) be given in charity. O Brahmin! Five gems alongwith gold be also denoted in charity and thereafter the Brahmins shall be fed with devoutness. In this manner, the native will certainly gain seven children and the native will get released from the curse of the noble soul.

95-105: By non performance of Shradha of the parents (previous birth) that gets into an evil spirit (soul) and with the curse of this spirit, there will be deprivation of children. Yogas of these kinds in relation to the Ascendant are described over here. 

If Saturn and the Sun are posited in the 5th house, the weak Moon in the 7th house and Rahu and Jupiter being in the Ascendant and in the 12th house respectively, the curse of evil spirit will destroy the children. 

When Saturn being, the lord of the 5th house is posited in the 8th house and Karaka (Jupiter) is also placed in the 8th house, 

it will cause deprivation of children due to the curse of the evil spirit. 

Positioning of malefic planet in the Ascendant, the Sun being in the 12th house and Mars, Saturn, Mercury in the 5th house and the lord of the 5th house is in the 8th house will cause destruction of the children as a result of the evil spirit. 

Should Rahu be posited in the Ascendant, Saturn in the 5th house and Jupiter being posited in the 8th house, loss of children will be the result owing to the curse of the evil spirit. 

If Venus, Jupiter and Rahu are posited in the Ascendant, Saturn is in conjunction with the Moon and the lord of the Ascendant is posited in the 8th house, the curse of evil spirit will destroy the children. 

If the Karaka (Jupiter) and lord of the 5th house being in the signs of debilitation and also aspected by the debilitated planets, it will also cause loss of children. When Saturn is in the Ascendant, Rahu in the 5th house, the Sun in the 8th house and Mars being in the 12th house, the evil spirit will destroy the children. 

If lord of the 7th house is in a Trik house (6th, 8th, 12th), the Moon is posited in the 5th house and Mars and Gulika are in the Ascendant, it will bring about the loss of children following the curse of the evil spirit. 

Positioning of the lord of the 8th house, Saturn and Venus in the 5th house and Karaka Jupiter being in the 8th house will cause the destruction of the children due to the curse of the evil spirit. 

# Propitiatory measures :

from the curse, the native should perform Shradha (śrādā) at Gaya, Rudra Abhisheka (śrādā अनिकोका) and charity of idol of Lord Bramha etc., should be done. Besides, cow, silver vessel and Neelamani (प्रति सिति) be also given in a charity to the Brahmins. After feeding the Brahmins, they should be paid Dakshna (सिति). By performing these rituals, the native will get released from the curse and birth of a child (son) will take place bestowing prolongation to the family.

109-111: In order to get blessed with a son (child), propitiation of the concerned planets to mitigate the affliction be performed. Worshipping of Lord Shiva will alleviate the affliction and curse of Mercury and Venus. Adoption of Mantras, Yantras and administering certain medicine (रूयागिका) will ward off the affliction (curse) of the Moon and Jupiter. Performing Kanyakada (कन्यागार्वा) will alleviate the Rahu's affliction. By recitation of Hari Bhajna (मित्रधाना), the affliction of the Sun gets mitigated. By giving cow in charity will effect release from Ketu's affliction. Japa of Rudriya will mellow down the affliction of the Mars and Saturn. All the afflictions will get extenuated by performing devout worship and listening to Harivansha Puma (प्रतिविका प्रतिगा) with earnest devotion and thus one will be blessed with a son (child).

2-3: Maharishi Parasara replied - O Brahmin the great! Whatever characteristics and attributes of the planets like the Sun etc., I have since narrated, all griefs and happiness of the living 

beings are under the control of these planets. Therefore, for the attainments of placid peace, perpetual prosperity, immaculate and virtuous knowledge, adequate rainfalls, good longevity, sound health and righteous power, propriatory measures and different Yagnas (प्रधिक) [sacrificial rites and an act of worship] of these planets be performed devotedly.

4-5: The idol of the Sun be made out of copper, the Moon in Sphatika (a kind of stone), Mars in red sandal, Mercury and Jupiter in gold, Venus in silver, Saturn in iron, Rahu in lead and Ketu in brass. Alternatively, the features (figure) of the various planets are sketched on a silk cloth of the colours ascribed to them as stated earlier, with the paste of sandal (मिकन्दि) and installed in the directions assigned to each planet as already explained.

stick to the year of birth on the basis of the deductions and should there be some difference in the actual transiting position of Jupiter at the time of birth, the same could be over looked. 

Assessment of Ayana : The Ascendant at the time of query is $2^{s} - 26^{o} - 37^{r} - 56^{e}$ which is in the latter part of the Gemini sign and hence the birth was 'Dakshinayana'. 

Assessment of Ritu (season): The query Ascendant is in the Drekkana of Saturn which denotes Shishira Ritu. This Ritu falls between 20 January to 19 March when the Sun transits Capricorn and Aquarius sign. Here we have seen that the compatibility between Ayana and Ritu does not exist. Therefore, we need to take shelter of the special rule embodied in Shloka 7 and according to which the Ritu connected with Jupiter should be adopted. Jupiter denotes Hemanta Ritu which takes place between 20 November and 19 January corresponding to lunar months Mrigshira and Pausha. 

Assessment of the month: The longitude of the query Ascendant is $2^{\circ} - 26^{\circ} - 37^{\circ} - 56^{\circ}$ which is the latter part of the 3rd Drekkana which indicates the 2nd month of the pair of month. Therefore, Pausa lunar month (from 19 December to 19 January) would be the month of the birth. 

Assessment of the Amsas of the Sun : The third Drekkana of the query Ascendant is $1^{\circ}$ -37'-56" ahead of the 1st half of this part of Drekkana (after $25^{\circ}$ ) i.e. $1^{\circ}$ -38' (say). Therefore, the expired position in the degrees (Amsas) etc. is 1 Amsa and 38 Kalas $(60 + 38) = 98$ Kalas. The 5 Amsas of the latter part of the Drekkana will be 300 Kalas. $(5^{\circ} \times 60)$ . The latter half of the Drekkana is equal to 30 Amsas (degrees) of the Sun (i.e. month) and thus we can find out the expired Amsas of the Sun by applying rule of proportion. 

$$
\begin{array}{r l r} 3 0 \text {A m s a} & = & 3 0 0 \text {K a l a s} \\ 9 8 \text {A m s a} & = & \frac {3 0 \times 9 8}{3 0 0} = \frac {9 8}{1 0} \\ & = & 9 \text {A m s a a n d} 4 8 \text {K a l a s} (9 ^ {\circ} - 4 8 ^ {\prime}) \end{array}
$$

The Sun between 19 December and 19 January remains in the Sagittarius sign. Therefore, the longitude of the Sun at the time of birth will be $8^{\circ} - 9^{\circ} - 48^{\circ} - 00^{\circ}$ . 

K

26-27: At the time when any planet is turning inauspicious for the native, the said planet be propitiated with proper worship and oblations because lord Brahma has given boon to these planets that who so ever would worship them, they (the planets) should do good to him. The growth and the down fall of the mankind, the existence and the destruction (प्रधान) of the world are solely dependent upon them. Therefore, the planets are the supreme amongst the divine Gods (प्रधान्योरूली).

1-4: Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! Now I will narrate the causes of the inauspicious births despite the fact when Ascendant (रिकन) and other planets being auspicious and well disposed. If a birth takes place during the Amavasya (रिकन्दावय), or Chaturdasi of the darker half of the lunar month. (14th tithi of Krishna Paksha), in the prevalence of Vishti Karna (रिकन्दावय), having the birth Nakshatra that of brother, in the birth Nakshatra of mother and father, during the solar ingress, during the Pata (रिकन्दावय), at the time 

of Solar or Lunar eclipses, during in the prevalence of inauspicious Yogas like Vyatipata (बारीपाति) etc. in all the three types of Gandants, during Yamaghant (मियान्वागत), Tithikshaya (तिकिकागय), in the Yogas of Dagdha (बारा) etc., Tritara (बारा) birth (son's birth after the birth of three daughters and vice-versa), a freak birth (some degree of disability) are considered inauspicious births. Performing of propitiatory measures will be beneficial and therefore, I now explain these measures (rituals) in the succeedingly narrations.

1-3: Maharishi Parasara said - O Maitreya! The birth of a child on Amavasya causes poverty for both the parents. The learned persons should undertake propitiatory measures for the alleviation of such affliction (रेम्य). 

To begin with, install a Kalasha (कलार) - a metal pitcher and put Goolar (पूरार) - a wild fig, leaves of Banyan (मित), Pipal (पिक्ल), 

mango and margo (रिया) trees and five gems (प्रधान्दी), and have its mouth covered with a cloth.

4-9: Worshipping amidst chanting of Mantras " निके त्रयपी रूधोति . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . ... ." and "अवस्थलम् रेश्या रूधोति ......." , the Kalasha (कलागा) be established in the south west corner (बावन सर्णा). The idol of the deities of the Sun and the Moon being the lords of Amavasya be made out of gold or the idol of the Moon made of silver and that of the Sun in Copper. Both these idols be installed with the prescribed rituals by worshipping amidst recitation of Mantra "अवस्थलम् रेश्या ...." for the Sun and "अवस्थलम् रेश्या ....." for the Moon for 16 or 5 times and perform the Havana (हिकाना). 

The learned Priests (पिकि) should perform Havana (अवन्या) giving oblations of wood pieces (अवन्या) and cooked charu (अवन्या) while reciting Mantras with devoutness "अवन्या...." and "सोलो रूधु़ अयत्तर्व अयत्तर्व......" respectively for 108 or 28 times. Thereafter, water be sprinkled (seemblance of a water bath - Abhisheka - कागिकेका) on the parents and 

the son (born on Amavasya). This be followed by offering of gold, silver and black cow as charity (रिलिका) and the Brahmins be fed (as many one can afford). These propitiatary ceremonies will extirpate the affliction and bring happiness and welfare to the child.

1-3: Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! The result of this affliction will be in accordance to the birth taking place in six different parts of Krishna Chataurdashi. If birth is in the first part, it be considered auspicious. The birth in the second and the third parts, will cause death to father and mother respectively. The birth in the fourth part, will attribute ruination of the maternal uncle and in the fifth part, will cause destruction of the family. There will be down fall of self and loss of wealth, should the birth be in the sixth part. For 

getting released from this affliction, propitiatory measures be taken in line the with the prescribed rituals.

6-13: The Brahmins knowing the ccorrect substance of the Mantras, should make the beginning with Varuna (बावया) Mantra after fully invoking themselves and then commence the worship with the Kalasha (कल्तिा) duly installed in the south-west corner while chanting Mantras viz; "अविकायक ...", "सोत्ते अधिका...", "पिकायमी...", "रूधनो अधिका..." and "अविकाय़ रैगा...". Thereafter Japa (बावया) of Sukta (बावया) "अविकाय़ रैगा..." etc., and "अविकाय़ रै�ा...". be performed followed by the recitation of "सोत्ते अधिका...". etc., Mantras. Subsequently, the idol of Lord Shiva be given water bath (बावया) and worship of Nine-Planets (न्देश्या) be carried out. This be succeeded with Havana (बावया) in a prescribed manner giving oblation of, Ghee (बावया), Charu (बावया), sesamums seeds (बावया), Urda (pulse - वाना), mustered oil, wood pieces (बावया) of Pipal (बावया) Pakar (बावया), Palas (बावया), Khaira (बावया), amidst chanting Mantras for 108 or 28 times severally for each Planet. Next, as per the precept, while chanting the Mantras of "अविकाय़ रै�ा...". Havana (बावया) with the oblations of Sesamums seeds be performed for the nine planets with full rituals. Finally, the water of the Kalasha be sprinkled (बावया) over the child and the father and then Brahmins, as many one can afford, be fed. This will bring welfare (बावया) to the child.

transactions be caused. 

In the last Shloka, the Sage has emphasised the transit positions of the planets. Although the Dasa may be unfavourable, if the said planet is having a favourable transit in the signs of auspicious houses, it will not produce unfavourable results as long as it remains in that transit. Conversely, if the planet is having a favourable Dasa but the transit is not good, in that case, the favourable results gets forestalled till the time the planet is passing through such a phase of the transit. 

![image](https://cdn-mineru.openxlab.org.cn/result/2026-04-29/a930488b-0e06-48f4-96b5-0619f72326f3/727989c2b2cfdca6818efca37c0ab499ee681fc6b680f3531c7696125ec90222.jpg)


9-13: The results of the Dasa of the 10th lord will be auspicious if conjunct with the 5th or the 9th lord. The Dasa of the other planets will also be favourable if posited with the 5th lord. Conjunction of the 9th lord with the other planets will cause exceedingly good results during the Dasa of such planets. The Dasa of the 9th lord if conjoined with the 4th lord and the Dasa of the 10th lord if posited in the 5th house are also the benefic Dasas. The Dasas of the Trine lords occupying Angles (kendras) position or the Angle lords located in the Trine houses, also render good results and these Dasas according to the Astrological Savants are said to be extremely auspicious. 

Notes: The apropos Shlokas unfold the most important maxims relating to the effects of Vimshottari Dasas which are elaborated in the following notes : 

i) The Dasa of the 10th lord duly conjunct with the 5th lord brings favourable results. This Dasa will boost the career

# Chapter-92

# PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING SOLAR INGRESS (SANKRANTI)

- 1 

Mikshar a ch rassai 11 

sakana 1 

Shaanayaa Suukamwabapotit lal:  shaonitivibib 

1-2: The name of the solar ingress (सिकानिति) falling on the weekdays - Sunday etc., are respectively - Ghora (मोरा), Dhuvankshi (धागी), Mahodari (मानोर्वी), Manda (मन्या), Mandakini (मन्यारकिनी), Mishra (मिया) and Rakshasi (रायारी). The native who have birth on the day of the Solar ingress will face poverty and miseries. The propitiation of planets this affliction to alleviate, confers happiness. Now I will state the method of propitiation. 

emotions, true excitements and sense of adherence to the rules and regulations. 

Similarly, all the natives also possess the Rajo Gunas (qualities) and as a result of which a greater amount of zeal, desire, excitements, tendencies to struggle, dejection, falsehood, confusion and unbalanced joy and grief are evident from them. However, all the natives with this quality (Rajo Guna) do show eagerness to act or react, and undertake tough and adventurous pursuits at the face of lots of oppositions and pressures from all the comers. 

In the same way, every native has Tamsika tendencies as well, because, all the natives do have some elements of ignorance, moments of unconsciousness, poor tolerance, natural weaknesses and also have the feel of tiredness, laziness and mental lethargy. Besides, the ignorance, incapacity and grief, fear, subjugation by the circumstances, exertation of pressure by the other persons, events, powers etc., are generally faced by all the natives in some form or the other. 

All the natives in some directions of natural powers or in some corner of their mind or nature, are found to be Satwika. Similarly, they are Rajsika in some other direction or part of their mind and nature and they are, simultaneously, Tamiska in yet some other directions or portion of their mind and nature. 

A native is said to possess either Satwika, Rajsika or Tamsika nature depending upon the impregnation of the strongest Gunas (quality) amongst the three in respect of general nature, particular mind and mode of action of the native. However, a person holding exclusively one type of quality is a impossibility. No one possesses an absolute purity. Acknowledgeable person does not dominate an absolute knowledge. Similarly, a learned person always remains partially leamed. Infact, many bad, wicked or brute qualities remain dormant in him due to predominance of good and virtuous qualities. 

With the same analogy, a bad person is not a thoroughly bad person. Even the most stupid person does have some latent and underdeveloped capabilities. A timid person also has a facet of courage which can precisely be judged after proper analysis of the 

if it is in the Timsamsa of Mars, the woman enjoys the sexual union even before her marriage. The reason for this is quite obvious because Mars is also the lord of the Ascendant and owns the Trimsamsa of the Ascendant which is imbued with the qualities of independent nature and to act in ones own will. It also gives rise to passion and to control over the passion is rather difficult, especially by the one who possesses such a nature. Phatdeepika, and Saravali used the word 'sul' (sinful) and in Jataka Parijata called it as suuwarai (ill tempered) for such combinations. These connotations lend full support and confirm the statement of Brihat Jataka. 

With the Trimsamsa of Venus, the woman is also stated to be of ill tempered but in this situation she maintains sexual union with the other persons even after the marriage. Venus is the significator of the marriage. This kind of disposition does form some kind of connection between Mars and Venus. While Mars denotes passion, Venus attributes sex. This combination tends the native to cross the limits of wedlock, customs and sensitivity of the precious relations. 

If it is in the Trimsamsa of Mercury, she will pursue occultism. This is also stated by the savants Varaha Mehira, Kalyana Varma, and Mantreswara apart from Maharishi Parasara. Such women are the great believer of occultism (प्रधान्यि) and incantation. Even though they acquire good education and have modren out look, this particular quality will be deep seated in them. Jataka Parijata contains "प्रधान्यि" instead of "प्रधान्यि" which connotes that in the event of Mercury's Trimsamsa, the woman will be of dirty nature. It is also experienced that in such a situation, the woman will be lethargic and lives in an apathetic state. 

In case of Jupiter's Trimsamsa, the intensity of the passion of Mars is checked by Jupiter. As such the woman of such nativity will be sober and moderate. The Trimsamsa of Saturn will give rise to subservientence to the native because the nature of Saturn (service) influences Mars to give such results. 

The Ascendant or the Moon (Ascendant) who soever is stronger, if is in the sign of Mercury and in the Trimamsa of Mars in a female horoscopy, she will be a cunning woman, according to Maharishi Parasara. 

of Mrityunjaya Mantra and of the devoted deity be performed and thereafter Brahmins be fed. It is after these rituals, the pacification of Deva (रेग्य) is complete.

# Chapter-93

# PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING ECLIPSES

1-4: Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! Whosoever is born during the solar or lunar eclipses runs the risk of sickness, sufferings, poverty and death. Therefore, for the benefit of mankind, I wish to narrate the method of propitiation for the alleviation of such 

afflictions. An idol of the lord of Nakshatra in which eclipses of solar and lunar take place be got made in gold. In case of solar eclipse an idol of Surya Deva (प्रधान विर्ति) be also got made in gold, of affordable size. For the lunar eclipse, silver idol of Chandra Deva (धन्दिका) should be got made and the learned should make the idol of Rahu in lead. 

$\text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} \text{哈} 1$ 

5-11: A sacred and even place be cleansed by smearing cow dung paste and a neat and decent cloth is spread over it and then install all the above three idols. In the event of solar eclipse, make oblation's of things favoured by the Sun viz. red rice, red sandal, red rosary, red clothes etc. and in the case of lunar eclipse, the Moon's favourable things like while sandal, white flower, white rosary, white clothes etc. and for Rahu, black flower and black clothes etc. and for the lord of the Nakshatra, offer white and fragrant things like flower etc. 

The Sun be worshipped with Mantras "jahann" and the 

Moon with "हम्ति वर्षा ....". The worship of Rahu be performed with Doorva (हिन्यी) and with Mantras "कम्पानानानिक्रा ....". Then, for the Sun and the Moon, Havana (हिन्यी) be performed with the wood pieces of Aak (हिन्यी) and Palasha (पूलाना) respectively. And O-Brahmin the great ! for the favour of Rahu, the Havana (हिन्यी) be performed with Doorva (हिन्यी - a kind of grass).

12-14: The native (the child) be given bath with the water of the Kalasha (कल्तिया) and then after calming down the senses (धनिकाया), worship the priest (अवृपाया). The Brahmins, as many one can afford, are fed. 

In this manner, the propitiatory measures for the child born during the eclipses be executed and after winning over all kinds of precepts, the person becomes fortunate.

# Chapter-94

# PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING GANDANTA

Ttivih- nakn- 1gnaanai gnnn aian 2hivsh mgtu Jnma- yarra- -vibwahadoe 0eovetaninwnnndm u | 19 

1: Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! There are three types of Gandanta (प्रधान्यि) namely - Tithi, Nakshatra and Ascendant (सिकन). They render death during the birth, travelling and auspicious ceremonies like marriage etc. 

# Tithi Gandanta :

Purpananandarayoyoetahoyo: 5nnoi  nanaohuyu 

2: O Maitreya! The starting Two Ghatis (प्रति) of Poorna Tithis viz., Panchami (5th), Dashami (10th) and Puranmashi (15th) 

and ending two ghtis of Nanda Tithis viz. Pratipada (1st), Shashti (6th) and Ekadashi (11th) totally four ghtis are called Gandanta. 

# Nakshatra Gandana :

Reyevatiabadakayo: Saaymamayo: yakmulayo: 1 

3: The four Ghatis of the Junction point (मादिक्ति) of Nakshatras Revati - Aswini, Ashlesa - Magha and Jyehta - Moola denoted by the last two Ghatis of the first and first two Ghatis of the second Nakshatra, are called Nakshatra Gandanta. 

# Ascendant (Lagna) Gandanta :

Moinanajayo: kafikshoyolalgnnyo-ralilachayo: 1 

4: Half Ghatis astride the junction point (रागिक्त) of signs Pisces - Aries, Cancer - Leo and Scorpio - Sagittarius totally one Ghati is known as Lagna Gandanta (ending half Ghatis of the first sign and starting half Ghatis of the second sign in each case) 

Euy  yuyyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuyuy 

5: Amongst these Ghandatas the ending five Ghatis of Jyeshta and Starting eight Ghatis of Moola Nakshatras are called Abukta Moola (बूधान्यम् रति). This is reckoned as highly trouble giving period.

6-8: Now I will narrate the method of propitiation for the alleviation of the affliction to the birth of the Gandanta Nakshatra. The father of the child should see the child only after the end of Janma Shouchaka (बन्दागीव - the period of ten days after the birth) or any auspicious day after performing rituals of propitiation. 

By giving charity of a bullock for the birth in Tithi Gandanta; a cow with calf in Nakshatra Gandanta and gold in Lagna Gandanta, will mitigate the evil effects. If birth took place in the first half part of the Gandanta, the Abhisheka (बिक्लेय़ - bath) of the child be performed alongwith father and in case of birth being in the second half part of the Gandanta, it should be alongwith mother. 

Bn 111 

9-11: On making idol in gold of the lord (deity) of the Tithi (for birth in Tithi Gandanta) or of the Nakshatra lord (for birth in Nakshatra Gandanta) or of the Lagna lord (for birth in Lagna Gandanta) and of weight 16 or 8 or 4 Masas as one can afford and Pooja be performed on placing over Kalasha (कून्ति) as per the prescribed rituals and with complete devoutness. Towards the end of the Pooja, Havana (बाना) be performed with the pieces of wood, food grains and Ghee (ी) and finally on feeding Brahmins with devotion as many as one could afford, the evil effects get alleviated.

# PROPITIATION OF PLANETS FOR BIRTHS DURING ABHUKTA MOOLA

1-2½: Maharishi Parasara Said - The lords of Nakshatras Jyeshta and Moola are Indra Deva (प्रधानोति) and Rakshasa (प्रधानोति) and both being highly inimical to each other, the birth taking place in their Gandanta (Junction) will suffer from severe affliction (रेमिका). A son or a daughter born during Abhukta Moola should be deserted or else, the father should not see the child upto eight years from the birth. For the purpose of getting relieved from this evil effect, I now narrate the method of propitiation. 

Lahzaa 1 1 1   
Jnnaa 1   
Jalatay 1 

3-4: Since these afflictions at birth are having high malice prepense, I will first explain the process of Moola Shanti (प्रति रिक्ति). The propitiatory rites be performed on the 12th day from the birth or on the birth Nakshatra or any auspicious day or during the 8th year of the native (child). 

JUULAMU 11411

5-8: Make a sacred place which is neat and even and have it purified with the paste of cow dung under a Mandapa (प्रधान्यम् - an open place covered with temporary covers etc.) having four gates duly decorated with arches in the east or north side of the house. Prepare a pit (पिकोपि) for performing Havana (पिकोपि) for the planets etc. Then get made an idol of Rakshasa (पिकोपि) being deity (lord) of the Nakshatra of 16, 8 or 4 of Mashas of gold as per the economic position of an individual. The idol should have two heads of blackish colour with sword and shield and mounted on a dead body and showing terrifying face alike goat.

is very dear to all deities (रेशलालो) as has been stated in the religious scriptures.

10-15: Select a learned and intelligent Acharya (priest) and establish Kalasha (कलागा) as per the prevalent religious traditions of the family and put Shataushadhi (सलागा), water of holy river and Panchagavya (पन्यागा). Then put the leaves of Bamboo over the Kalasha of 100 small holes and while facing towards west, place the idol over it and perform the worship by giving oblation of white clothes, rice etc. After drawing figures of Adhideva Indra and Pratyadhideva Jala (बल) in their respective Shakha (मारेशा), perform worship of the prime (प्रधान) deities (रिवराताको) with Mantras. Thereafter as per the laid down procedure, perform the Havana (होमा) for the happiness of the Deva and Adhideva. For the pacification of death affliction, after calming down the senses (मिकिको), the Japa (जपा) of Mantra " निमिको अयत्ति के......", be recited 1008 or 108 times and then the priest be honoured with the gifts with all humbleness.

16-18: The priest well conversed with the Mantras be made seated at a sacred place, and the father (रावस्थल), the wife and the child should take bath from the water of the two Kalashas wrapped with cloth. Thereafter, they should get dressed with white clothes and put fragrant paste of sandal and the father should offer milk bearing cow to the priest as well as gifts, as he could afford, and feast the Brahmins.

19-20: Recite the Mantra embodied in Shloka 19 i.e., "प्रधान रूधोति अन्यम् रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रॄवति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति रूधोति वस्थल्ली सिकर्षयपी का, "Destroy what ever sin and short comings contained in my limbs and accept oblation of Ghee (प्रधान) and grow my wealth (लकानी) and invigoration" 

While chanting the above Mantra, they (father, mother and son) should clearly see the reflections of their faces in the Ghee (in melted form). With these alleviatory measures, all the afflictions (evil effects) arising out of the birth of the native (son) in the Moola gandanta (बागूविक्य जूला) will get destroyed.

1-2½: Sage Parasara said - O Vipra! The birth in Jyeshta Gandanta proves disastrous for both native (son) and his father. Now I narrate the methodology for the purpose of alleviation of all the afflictions (obstructions - निल्ति). Like in the case of Moola Gandanta, erect Mandapa (रागम्यप) for the pacification of Jyeshta Gananda as well. Here Indra Devta be the prime Devta (प्रधान), Agni Devta (बांन) as Adhideva and consider Rakshasa (राकासा) as Pratyadhideva.

3-6: Have the idol of Indra Devta made of gold as one can afford holding Ankusha (बूधागा) and seated over Airavata (रेशाका) and put it on Kalasha (कृत्तागा) filled duly with paddy and rice. Worship be performed amidst chanting of Mantras as prevalent in the family and with fragrance, flowers, rice and take Abhisheka (sprinkling of holy water) and do Havana (बूधागा) and arrange food for the Brahmins. Recite Indra Sukata (बूधागा) and Mrityunjaya Mantras with complete devoutness. Lastly, do the prayer of Indra Devata. These rituals will bestow peace.

In the event of non availability of cows, the cash amount equivalent to the cow(s) be given to the Brahmins.

11-13: The birth of a girl or a boy in the last three quarters of Aslesha Nakshatra and their birth being in the first three quarters of Moola Nakshatra will become the cause of death of mother-in-law and father-in-law respectively. A birth of a daughter after three sons or a birth of a son after three daughters brings misfortune to his both paternal and material families. In that case the rituals of propitiation be undertaken countifully.

5-9: At the time of Havana (बवना), one Brahmin after purifying himself should undertake Japa (बारा) for Rudra Suktas (बर्षपूर्वति) and the entire Shanti Sukta (बारिको कृक्ति). Acharya, thereafter should perform the Havana with wood pieces (बारिको अयमी सिया), Ghee (बूा), Sesamum seeds (बूला), Charu (बूहा) for 1008, 108 or 28 times amidst the recitation of Mantras relating to Bramha, Vishu, Mehesha and Indra and the final oblation (बूगो अयमी सिया) be given in favour of devoted deity (बूहा अयमी सिया). 

Thereafter, the child be given Abhisheka alongwith his entire family followed by presenting of gifts to the priest and Brahmins be fed. After this, they should see there reflections in the Ghee kept in a copper vessel and gifts be distributed amongst the poors. With these propitiatory measures, all kinds of afflictions will get alleviated.

1-3: Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! Now I narrate the abnormal birth (delivery) on account of which the entire village and family get afflicted. If there is an early or delayed birth or the child is born with less or more times or without the head or with two heads or female showing appearance of an animal or male of animal, these kinds of abnormal births signify destruction.

In order to get relieved from such affliction, a proper procedure of propitiation should be carried out vigoursly or the concerned woman or animal viz., horse, cow etc. should be given up.

6-9: If a woman get pregnant or give birth to a child in the 15th or 16th years from her birth, it will be inauspicious. If cow gives birth when the Sun is posited in Leo sign, or a buffalo (female) gives birth when the Sun is in Capricorn, it will cause destruction to their keeper and also to their possessor (owner) and as such they should be donated in charity to a Brahmin or else, propitiatory measures be taken as per the prescribed procedure. All the rituals like Havan (रेमन्द) etc. be carried out as per the sequence as described earlier in a peaceful manner and with which the entire family will be get released from the affliction and bestowed happiness.

4-5 : Sage Parasara said, O Brahmin! As desired by you, now I would explain how to forecast every thing through queries. Kindly listen with full attention. The savants first of all, should know the subject matters related to the houses and thereafter, they should explain auspiciousness and inauspiciousness about the houses.
