6-11: The Assendant (1st house) denotes features of the querist, grief, happiness etc. and consider gain or loss of gems (wealth) from the 2nd house; valour, brothers (co-borns) and comforts of servants from the 3rd house; friends, house, village or town, mother and comforts of vehicles from the 4th house; pregnancy, wisdom, scholarly pursuits from the 5th house; enemy, maternal uncle, disease injury from the 6th house; grief, wife, business, travellings from the 7th house; death, battles (conflicts), disease, frightfulness from the 8th house; digging of pond, well and temple from the 9th house; kingdom, happiness of father, king's or government affairs from the 10th house; agricultural produce and vehicles from the 11th house; hindrances by the enemies, sexual relationship, expenditure should be considered from the 12th house. 

The assigned number of rays to the Sun is 10 and therefore, the above balance figures be multiplied by 10 and divided by 6. 

$$
\begin{array}{l} \frac {(1 - 1 9 - 3 6 - 3 3) \times 1 0}{6} \\ \frac {(1 6 - 1 6 - 5 - 3 0)}{6} = \quad 2 - 2 3 \end{array}
$$

The Sun is posited in the Moola Trikona sign and therefore, this figure should be doubled. 

$$
(2 - 2 3) \times 2 = 4 - 4 6
$$

The net number of rays of the Sun $= 4 - 46$ 

# The Moon

<table><tr><td></td><td>Sign</td><td>Deg.</td><td>Mint.</td><td>Sec.</td></tr><tr><td>Longitude of the Moon</td><td>2</td><td>1</td><td>57</td><td>9</td></tr><tr><td>Less: Deep debilitation point of the Moon</td><td>8</td><td>3</td><td>00</td><td>00</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>5</td><td>28</td><td>57</td><td>09</td></tr></table>

Since the balance is less 6 signs, these figures be multiplied by 9 and divided by 6 

$$
\frac {(5 - 2 8 - 5 7 - 9) \times 9}{6}
$$

$$
\frac {5 3 - 2 0 - 3 4 - 2 1}{6} = 8 - 2 8
$$

The Moon is located in a Adhinitra sign (best friend). Therefore, the above number of rays be multiplied by 4 and divided by 3 

$$
\frac {(8 - 2 8) \times 4}{3} = 1 1 - 2 7
$$

The net number of rays of the Moon $= 11 - 27$ 

of Vedic knowledge), spiritual knowledge, study of physics, disciple, religious, practicing Matra, Yantra and Tantra, spaculation, lottery, diplomacy, consullations, etc., be answered. 

The Sixth House: rules thief, hindrances, mental and physical troubles, death through weapons, (Prasna Marga); ill health, destitution, servant, evil deeds, aggressive actions, fatal assault of weapons by the enemises, suspicion, battle, animals like buffalo etc. (Dalvagya Vallabha). In addition, the queries of killing, attraction, disliking etc., employing spells with malevolent purpose, dispute opposition, thief, litigation, fire incidents, accidents, injury, captivity, bull, donkey, camel, explosion, conspiracy, arson, etc., and also house of maternal grand parents be considered from the 6th house. 

The Seventh House : denotes marriage, sexual relationship between man and woman, bed, house, loss of wealth through theft (Prasna Marga); health, purchases, servant, return from the journey (Daivagya Vallabha). The other queries like, business disputes, daily business engagements, love, attraction, romance, works related to film, lost item, love disputes, girl's first entrance into the house after marriage, sea voyage, selection of trade, business transactions, theft articles, oblivion, excitement, enjoyment of co-habitation etc., also be considered from the 7th house. 

The Eight House : governs destruction, calamity, dispute, cause and place of death, Imprisonment, monastery or student hostel, disease, obstacles and hindrances (Prasna Marga); longevity, opposition, secrets of the kingdom (Government), weaknesses of relatives, difficult places, fort, killing of enemy and wife, crossing the river (Daivagya Vallabha). In addition, the queries related to obstruction in sea voyage, snake, scorpion or dog bite, sudden accident, haunted by evil spirits, debts, wound through weapon, bad omen, back biting, foul language, about the deceased bequest, ups and downs, quarrel evil acts etc. also be considered from the 8th house. 

The Ninth House : signifies fortune, religion, sympathy, pious deeds, penance, father, charity, austerity, preceptor (Prasna Marga); imitation, temple of devoted deity, pilgrimage, coronation, religious pursuits, digging of water tanks, brother-in-law (Davagya Vallabha). From the same house, queries relating spiritual teachings, religious 

and social works, trust, progress, fame, favour of the King and Goddess, etc., be also tackled. 

The Tenth House: rules temple, town, auditorium, rest house enroute, drinking and eating places, permission, livelihood (Prasna Marga); attraction, rain, commendation of father's work and position, plous deed, fall in authority, currency and change of position (Dalvagya Vallabha). Besides, from this house, the queries related to climate, social and public places, capital, social status, honoured with title, reception, decoration, foreign travelling, etc., could as well be answered. 

The Eleventh House : denotes accomplishment of all the desired objectives, elder brother and sisters, infant, left ear (Prasna Marga). Besides, progress in the business, gain from the investments, elephant, horses, conveyance etc., clothes, theft of gold and wealth, scholarly conversation on worldly matters, logic, etc., be considered from the 11th house. 

The Twelfth House : governs evil act, losses, left eye, fall from the position, failures, (Prasna Marga); enjoyment, marriage, charity, loss of crops, uncle, witnessing the battle and the thought of defeat in the battle (Dalvagya Vallabha). From this house matters like, punishment, imprisonment, donation of animals, maternal aunt (mother's brother's wife and sister), sea voyage, etc., should also be considered.

12: A house, if occupied or aspected by its lord or benefic planets will possess strength and a house, if occupied or aspected by the malefic planets is rendered weak. Should a house be occupied or aspected by both benefic and malefic planets, it will produce madium results. 

# Deceitful or misleading queries :

Praananaa 1 

y 

13: In a query chart, if Saturn and the Moon, are posited in the Ascendant, and Mercury is combust and the Sun be in the Aquarious sign, it should be construed that the querist has come with dishonest intentions (deceptive motives). 

Notes : Some persons by their sheer negative approach, put question to the Astrologer just to test his knowledge. They visit the Astrologer with dishonest intentions. However, should the Astrologer feel through the analysis of the query chart that the queriest has posed the question mere for the fun sake, he should refuse to entertain and decline giving any prediction. The combinations for deceitful queries have been given in the foregoing Shloka through which it could easily be assessed whether the query is genuine or not and then only he should proceed to analyse the Horary Chart.

14-15: When (i) the lord of the ascendant (Lagnesha) the Ascendant and the significator (Karyesha) related to query, aspects the concerned house, (ii) the lord of the Ascendant aspects the concerned house and significator (Karyesha) aspect the Ascendant or duly posited in their respective houses, (iii) Lagnesha and Kareysha are in a mutual aspect, the success will be achieved after making efforts. Should the Ascendant (Lagna) and Kareysha are aspected by the full Moon, the success is achieved all of a sudden.

16: If the Ascendant (of the query chart) is conjoined with 

the auspicious planets or in the Vargas (Divisions) of the benefic planets and the Ascendant is in Sirshodaya sign (sign rising from the head), it will cause an early success.

PRAHNAOJADOTJIATRIKAKOENI 111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111 

17-18: A query held in the fist or thought of, will relate to Dhatu (article of the metal, mineral origin), if the planet (in a query chart) occupying its own Navamsa and aspects its own Navamsa in the Ascendant or in the trinal house. But, if the planet (in the same case) occupies the Navamsa of another planet and then aspects its own Navamsa in the Ascendant or in the trinal houses, the query will relate to Jeeva (matter connected with living beings). If the planet be in another's Navamsa and also aspects another's Navamsa in the Ascendant or in the trinal houses, the query will refer to Moola (some roots or vegetables kind). 

In the case of even signs, if the Ascendant is located in the 1st Navamsa, the query will relate to Jeeva; in the 2nd Navamsa, Moola; in the 3rd Navamsa, it will concern Dhatu. Again, the 4th, the 5th, the 6th Navamsas will relate to Jeeva, Moola and Dhatu respectively. (Further, the 7th, the 8th and the 9th Navamsas will repeat the order). In the case of Ascendant be in the odd sign and is located in the 1st Navamsa, the fist query will relate to Dhatu, the 2nd Navamsa, Moola and the 3rd Navamsa, will refer to Jeeva. Similarly, the remaining six Navamsas of the odd sign will repeat twice. 

Notes : The fist or thought of queries were generally used to test the skill of the Astrologers by the kings inthe ancient time. The foregoing stanza contains the method of delineating of the three types of queries which relate to (i) Dhatu, (ii) Jeeva and 

(iii) Moola. From these methods, it is possible to determine whether the object or subject matter held in the fist or thought of, is of a Dhatu, Moola or Jeeva kind. The principles, enunciated in this Shloka, are reiterated hereafter for the benefit of the readers. 

# Principle-1

If any planet occupying a Navamsa of its own, aspects a Navamsa of its own in the Ascendant, the 5th or the 9th house, it should be stated that the querist is thinking about Dhatu such as gold, silver, steel etc.

50-52: Should the lord of Antardasa be at the 6th or the 8th house from the lord of the Mahadasa and in conjunction with or aspected by the malefic planets, in that case there will definitely be the loss in the venture through the enemies and befall of sudden calamity. The same Ketu if posited in the 2nd or the 7th house, physical ailment, great fear will be the effects. In this critical period, the recitation (Japa) of Mrityunjaya Matras (Vedic hymns), the evil effects will get mellowed down with the blessings of Lord Shiva.

21-24: If from the Ascendant of query, the Moon is posited in the 7th house and the lord of the 9th house is in the first half of the sign, the traveller will be on his way back. If from the Ascendant of query, Jupiter, Venus or the Moon is posited in the 4th house, the traveller is about to return home. If from the Ascendant of query, Jupiter and Venus are occupying the 2nd or the 3rd house, the traveller is almost at home (that is, he would be reaching home at any moment). If between Venus, Mercury and Saturn if any one posited in a movable sign, the traveller will return home quickly but these planets should not be in retrogression.

32: From the query Ascendant, if the 5th house is occupied by the auspicious planets or conjoined or aspected by the own lord and also the lord of the month (the Sun) is also well fortified, the pregnancy will be safe. 

Note: In the reverse situation when the 5th house is occupied or aspected by the malefic planets and is neither conjoined with nor aspected by its lord and also the lord of the month (the Sun) is also weak, the pregnancy gets terminated.

33-35: If the lords of the Ascendant and the 5th house are 

in the odd signs, the son and if are in the feminine sign, the girl will born. The position of Saturn in the odd sign or in odd Navamsa, will also give son. The same Saturn happens to be in the even or in even Navamsa, the birth of girl will take place. The Sun, the Moon and Jupiter be in the odd sign or odd Navamsa will also give son. If the 5th house is aspected by Venus, Mars or the Moon, the birth of son will take place.

Should these planets duly dignified and get posited in the 11th house, it will give birth to a child.

1-4: Maharishi Parasara said - O Vipra! The Shastra expounded by Lord Bramha for Mahatma Narad and he (Narad) a with all humbleness narrated the same to Rishis Shaunaka etc. and I have unfolded the same Shastra as it is, before you. This knowledge should never be imparted to a person who is Jealous, backbiter (slander), dull, arrogant and unknown. This supreme and pious shastra amongst all Vedangas (बिरागा) should be taught well to 

the humble, devoted, truthful, intelligent, submissive and known persons.

5-6: A person having the knowledge of time (रागलम्ति) and accurate positions of planets and Nakshatras only can understand Hora Shastra and not others. A person knowing the subject matter of Hora Shastra should be truthful and able to exercise control over his senses. The auspicious and inauspicious predictions of such a person will come true.

7: A person who reads or listen devotedly this excellent Hora Shatra, his longevity, strength, wealth, fame will go on increasing.

8-9: In this manner, Maharishi Parasara narrated to Maitreya, the Brahmin, the new supreme Hora Shatra containing different remarkable chapters keeping in view the welfare of the world at large. Thereafter, it was spread to the entire world and was received respectfully by one and all.

10-25: The foregoing stanzas contain only the headings of all the preceding chapters. 

Notes: Besides 97 chapters, three more chapters have been added. These chapters have been adopted from the other treatises (Shastras) of Maharishi Parasara for the benefit of the readers. These Chapters are, Chapter 5, "To find out Planetary Position" (प्रधान्यि सिति रत्ति कोम्का;), Chapter- 10, "Surroundings at the time of birth" (सूल्वन्दिकागर्षय;), of Volume-1 and Chapter 99 - "Horary System" (प्रधान्यि सिति कोम्का;), of Volume- 2.
